contentBlocks.original:
शिवः शक्त्या युक्तो यदि भवति शक्तः प्रभवितुं
न चेदेवं देवो न खलु कुशलः स्पन्दितुमपि ।
अतस्त्वामाराध्यां हरिहरविरिञ्चादिभिरपि
प्रणन्तुं स्तोतुं वा कथमकृतपुण्यः प्रभवति ॥ १॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivaḥ śaktyā yukto yadi bhavati śaktaḥ prabhavituṃ
na cedevaṃ devo na khalu kuśalaḥ spanditumapi .
atastvāmārādhyāṃ hariharaviriñcādibhirapi
praṇantuṃ stotuṃ vā kathamakṛtapuṇyaḥ prabhavati .. 1..
contentBlocks.translation: United with Śakti, Śiva is endowed with the power to create the universe. Otherwise, He is incapable even of movement. Therefore, who except those endowed with great merits acquired in the past can be fortunate enough to salute or praise Thee, Mother Divine, who art the adored of even Hari, Hara and Viriñci? 1.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिवः (śivaḥ) – Shiva; शक्त्या (śaktyā) – with Śakti; युक्तः (yuktaḥ) – united; यदि (yadi) – if; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; शक्तः (śaktaḥ) – capable; प्रभवितुं (prabhavituṃ) – to create; न (na) – not; चेत् (ced) – if; एवं (evaṃ) – thus; देवः (devaḥ) – god; न (na) – not; खलु (khalu) – indeed; कुशलः (kuśalaḥ) – capable; स्पन्दितुम् (spanditum) – to move; अपि (api) – even; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; त्वाम् (tvām) – thee; आराध्यां (ārādhyām) – adorable; हरिहरविरिञ्चादिभिः (hariharaviriñcādibhiḥ) – by Hari, Hara, Viriñci, etc. (hari:Hari | hara:Hara | viriñca:Viriñci | ādibhiḥ:etc.); अपि (api) – even; प्रणन्तुं (praṇantuṃ) – to salute; स्तोतुं (stotuṃ) – to praise; वा (vā) – or; कथम् (katham) – how; अकृतपुण्यः (akṛtapuṇyaḥ) – without merits (akṛta:not done | puṇyaḥ:merits); प्रभवति (prabhavati) – is able;

contentBlocks.original:
तनीयांसं पांसुं तव चरणपङ्केरुहभवं
विरिञ्चिस्सञ्चिन्वन् विरचयति लोकानविकलम् ।
वहत्येनं शौरिः कथमपि सहस्रेण शिरसां
हरस्संक्षुद्यैनं भजति भसितोद्धूलनविधिम् ॥ २॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tanīyāṃsaṃ pāṃsuṃ tava caraṇapaṅkeruhabhavaṃ
viriñcissañcinvan viracayati lokānavikalam .
vahatyenaṃ śauriḥ kathamapi sahasreṇa śirasāṃ
harassaṃkṣudyainaṃ bhajati bhasitoddhūlanavidhim .. 2..
contentBlocks.translation: Gathering a minute particle of dust from Thy lotus feet, Brahmā the creator brings into being this universe (limitless and mysterious) without any imperfection. The sustentator Viṣṇu as Ādiśeṣa somehow supports this universe (made out of that dust) with His thousand hoods. And Hara, the destroyer, crushing it into powder, rubs the ashes all over His body (at the time of dissolution). 2.
contentBlocks.meanings: तनीयांसं (tanīyāṃsaṃ) – minute particle; पांसुं (pāṃsuṃ) – dust; तव (tava) – your; चरणपङ्केरुहभवं (caraṇapaṅkeruhabhavaṃ) – from lotus feet (caraṇa:feet | paṅkeruha:lotus | bhavaṃ:born from); विरिञ्चिः (viriñciḥ) – Brahmā; सञ्चिन्वन् (sañcinvan) – gathering; विरचयति (viracayati) – creates; लोकान् (lokān) – worlds; अविकलम् (avikalam) – without imperfection; वहति (vahati) – supports; एनं (enaṃ) – this; शौरिः (śauriḥ) – Viṣṇu; कथमपि (kathamapi) – somehow; सहस्रेण (sahasreṇa) – with thousand; शिरसां (śirasāṃ) – hoods; हरः (haraḥ) – Hara (Śiva); संक्षुद्य (saṃkṣudya) – crushing; एनं (enaṃ) – this; भजति (bhajati) – rubs; भसितोद्धूलनविधिम् (bhasitoddhūlanavidhim) – ashes all over body (bhasita:ashes | uddhūlana:scattering | vidhim:method);

contentBlocks.original:
अविद्यानामन्त-स्तिमिर-मिहिरद्वीपनगरी
जडानां चैतन्य-स्तबक-मकरन्द-स्रुतिझरी ।
दरिद्राणां चिन्तामणिगुणनिका जन्मजलधौ
निमग्नानां दंष्ट्रा मुररिपु-वराहस्य भवति ॥ ३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avidyānāmanta-stimira-mihiradvīpanagarī
jaḍānāṃ caitanya-stabaka-makaranda-srutijharī .
daridrāṇāṃ cintāmaṇiguṇanikā janmajaladhau
nimagnānāṃ daṃṣṭrā muraripu-varāhasya bhavati .. 3..
contentBlocks.translation: The dust of Thy feet is the Island City, wherefrom takes place the luminous sun-rise of spiritual illumination driving away the over-casting darkness of ignorance in the hearts of devotees. It forms the cluster of flower buds, from which gushes forth the nectar of intelligence, enlivening the dull-witted. It is a veritable necklace of wish-yielding gems for the poverty-stricken. And for those immersed in the ocean of Samsara, it becomes their up-lifter like the Tusk of Viṣṇu (which raised the earth from submergence in Pralaya waters when He incarnated as the Cosmic Boar). 3.
contentBlocks.meanings: अविद्यानाम् (avidyānām) – of ignorance; अन्त (anta) – end; स्तिमिर (stimira) – darkness; मिहिर (mihira) – sun; द्वीप (dvīpa) – island; नगरी (nagarī) – city; जडानाम् (jaḍānām) – of the dull-witted; चैतन्य (caitanya) – consciousness; स्तबक (stabaka) – cluster of buds; मकरन्द (makaranda) – nectar; स्रुतिझरी (srutijharī) – flowing stream; दरिद्राणाम् (daridrāṇām) – of the poor; चिन्तामणि (cintāmaṇi) – wish-yielding gem; गुणनिका (guṇanikā) – necklace; जन्मजलधौ (janmajaladhau) – in the ocean of birth (janma:birth | jaladhau:in the ocean); निमग्नानाम् (nimagnānām) – of those immersed; दंष्ट्रा (daṃṣṭrā) – tusk; मुररिपु (muraripu) – enemy of Mura; वराहस्य (varāhasya) – of the boar; भवति (bhavati) – becomes;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदन्यः पाणिभ्यामभयवरदो दैवतगणः
त्वमेका नैवासि प्रकटितवराभीत्यभिनया ।
भयात् त्रातुं दातुं फलमपि च वाञ्छासमधिकं
शरण्ये लोकानां तव हि चरणावेव निपुणौ ॥ ४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadanyaḥ pāṇibhyāmabhayavarado daivatagaṇaḥ
tvamekā naivāsi prakaṭitavarābhītyabhinayā .
bhayāt trātuṃ dātuṃ phalamapi ca vāñchāsamadhikaṃ
śaraṇye lokānāṃ tava hi caraṇāveva nipuṇau .. 4..
contentBlocks.translation: All Deities other than Thee bestow boons and give shelter from fear by the pose of their hands. Thou alone art not given to any such external demonstration of giving boons and shelter. For, O Refuge of All, Thy very feet (without any demonstration) are themselves inherently capable of sheltering devotees from the great fear of Samsāra and of giving them much more than what they pray for! 4.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वदन्यः (tvadanyaḥ) – other than Thee (tvad:Thee | anyaḥ:other); पाणिभ्याम् (pāṇibhyām) – by the hands; अभयवरदः (abhayavaradaḥ) – granting boons and fearlessness (abhaya:fearlessness | varadaḥ:granting boons); दैवतगणः (daivatagaṇaḥ) – group of deities (daivata:deity | gaṇaḥ:group); त्वम् (tvam) – Thou; एका (ekā) – alone; न (na) – not; एव (eva) – indeed; असि (asi) – art; प्रकटितवराभीत्यभिनया (prakaṭitavarābhītyabhinayā) – demonstration of boons and fearlessness (prakaṭita:demonstration | vara:boons | abhīti:fearlessness | abhinayā:gesture); भयात् (bhayāt) – from fear; त्रातुम् (trātum) – to protect; दातुम् (dātum) – to give; फलम् (phalam) – fruit; अपि (api) – also; च (ca) – and; वाञ्छासमधिकम् (vāñchāsamadhikam) – more than desired (vāñchā:desire | samadhikam:more than); शरण्ये (śaraṇye) – O Refuge; लोकानाम् (lokānām) – of the worlds; तव (tava) – Thy; हि (hi) – indeed; चरणौ (caraṇau) – feet; एव (eva) – indeed; निपुणौ (nipuṇau) – capable;

contentBlocks.original:
हरिस्त्वामाराध्य प्रणतजनसौभाग्यजननीं
पुरा नारी भूत्वा पुररिपुमपि क्षोभमनयत् ।
स्मरोऽपि त्वां नत्वा रतिनयनलेह्येन वपुषा
मुनीनामप्यन्तः प्रभवति हि मोहाय महताम् ॥ ५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
haristvāmārādhya praṇatajanasaubhāgyajananīṃ
purā nārī bhūtvā puraripumapi kṣobhamanayat .
smaro'pi tvāṃ natvā ratinayanalehyena vapuṣā
munīnāmapyantaḥ prabhavati hi mohāya mahatām .. 5..
contentBlocks.translation: Adoring Thee, who art the bestower of prosperity on all Thy votaries, Hari (Viṣṇu) was able to become a charming female and stir waves of passion in the minds of no less a Deity than Hara, the Destroyer of the Three Cities. And Smara (Cupid, the god of love), through Thy adoration, got a form—a veritable feast for the eyes of his consort Rati—, with which he has become capable of causing deep infatuation even in the minds of sages. 5.
contentBlocks.meanings: हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari (Viṣṇu); त्वाम् (tvām) – Thee; आराध्य (ārādhya) – adoring; प्रणतजनसौभाग्यजननीम् (praṇatajanasaubhāgyajananīm) – bestower of prosperity (praṇata:votaries | jana:people | saubhāgya:prosperity | jananīm:bestower); पुरा (purā) – formerly; नारी (nārī) – female; भूत्वा (bhūtvā) – becoming; पुररिपुम् (puraripum) – enemy of cities (pura:cities | ripum:enemy); अपि (api) – even; क्षोभम् (kṣobham) – agitation; अनयत् (anayat) – caused; स्मरः (smaraḥ) – Cupid; अपि (api) – also; त्वां (tvāṃ) – Thee; नत्वा (natvā) – adoring; रतिनयनलेह्येन (ratinayanalehyena) – feast for Rati's eyes (rati:Rati | nayana:eyes | lehyena:feast); वपुषा (vapuṣā) – with form; मुनीनाम् (munīnām) – of sages; अपि (api) – even; अन्तः (antaḥ) – within; प्रभवति (prabhavati) – capable; हि (hi) – indeed; मोहाय (mohāya) – for infatuation; महताम् (mahatām) – of the great;

contentBlocks.original:
धनुः पौष्पं मौर्वी मधुकरमयी पञ्च विशिखाः
वसन्तः सामन्तो मलयमरुदायोधनरथः ।
तथाप्येकः सर्वं हिमगिरिसुते कामपि कृपाम्
अपाङ्गात्ते लब्ध्वा जगदिद-मनङ्गो विजयते ॥ ६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanuḥ pauṣpaṃ maurvī madhukaramayī pañca viśikhāḥ
vasantaḥ sāmanto malayamarudāyodhanarathaḥ .
tathāpyekaḥ sarvaṃ himagirisute kāmapi kṛpām
apāṅgātte labdhvā jagadida-manaṅgo vijayate .. 6..
contentBlocks.translation: (Look at Kāma Deva [Cupid] and his equipments—how ineffective they are in themselves!) His bow is made only of flowers; its bow-string is a line of honey-bees; he has only five arrows, and these are made of flowers; his minister is the (periodical and undependable) spring season; his battle-chariot is the (shifting and formless) Malaya breeze; and above all he is Ananga, the bodiless one. Yet, O daughter of the Mountain! blessed by Thy gracious side-glance, he, by himself alone, is victorious over the whole world. 6.
contentBlocks.meanings: धनुः (dhanuḥ) – bow; पौष्पं (pauṣpam) – made of flowers; मौर्वी (maurvī) – bow-string; मधुकरमयी (madhukaramayī) – made of honey-bees (madhukara:honey-bees | mayī:made of); पञ्च (pañca) – five; विशिखाः (viśikhāḥ) – arrows; वसन्तः (vasantaḥ) – spring season; सामन्तः (sāmantaḥ) – minister; मलयमरुदायोधनरथः (malayamarudāyodhanarathaḥ) – battle-chariot is Malaya breeze (malaya:Malaya | marut:breeze | āyodhana:battle | rathaḥ:chariot); तथापि (tathāpi) – yet; एकः (ekaḥ) – alone; सर्वं (sarvam) – all; हिमगिरिसुते (himagirisute) – O daughter of the Mountain (himagiri:Mountain | sute:daughter); कामपि (kāmapi) – some; कृपाम् (kṛpām) – grace; अपाङ्गात्ते (apāṅgātte) – from your side-glance (apāṅga:side-glance | te:your); लब्ध्वा (labdhvā) – having obtained; जगदिदम् (jagadidam) – this world (jagat:world | idam:this); अनङ्गः (anaṅgaḥ) – bodiless; विजयते (vijayate) – is victorious;

contentBlocks.original:
क्वणत्काञ्चीदामा करिकलभकुम्भस्तननता
परिक्षीणा मध्ये परिणतशरच्चन्द्रवदना ।
धनुर्बाणान् पाशं सृणिमपि दधाना करतलैः
पुरस्तादास्तां नः पुरमथितुराहोपुरुषिका ॥ ७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kvaṇatkāñcīdāmā karikalabhakumbhastananatā
parikṣīṇā madhye pariṇataśaraccandravadanā .
dhanurbāṇān pāśaṃ sṛṇimapi dadhānā karatalaiḥ
purastādāstāṃ naḥ puramathiturāhopuruṣikā .. 7..
contentBlocks.translation: May the Divine Mother Tripurasundarī, the Pride (Ahanta or I-sense) of Siva the Destroyer of the Three Cities., vouchsafe Her presence before us—the Mother with Her slender waist girdled with jingling mini-bells, with Her frame slightly bent in the middle by the weight of Her breasts that bulge like the frontal globes of the forehead of a young elephant, with her face resembling the autumnal moon, and with her hand sporting a bow, arrows, a noose and a goad! 7.
contentBlocks.meanings: क्वणत्काञ्चीदामा (kvaṇatkāñcīdāmā) – with jingling girdle (kvaṇat:jingling | kāñcī:girdle | dāmā:with); करिकलभकुम्भस्तननता (karikalabhakumbhastananatā) – breasts like elephant's forehead (kari:elephant | kalabha:young | kumbha:forehead | stana:breast | natā:bent); परिक्षीणा (parikṣīṇā) – slender; मध्ये (madhye) – in the middle; परिणतशरच्चन्द्रवदना (pariṇataśaraccandravadanā) – face like autumnal moon (pariṇata:matured | śarat:autumn | candra:moon | vadanā:face); धनुर्बाणान् (dhanurbāṇān) – bow and arrows (dhanus:bow | bāṇān:arrows); पाशं (pāśam) – noose; सृणिमपि (sṛṇimapi) – and a goad (sṛṇi:goad | api:and); दधाना (dadhānā) – holding; करतलैः (karatalaiḥ) – with hands; पुरस्तात् (purastāt) – before; आस्तां (āstām) – may be; नः (naḥ) – us; पुरमथितुः (puramathituḥ) – of the destroyer of cities (pura:city | mathituḥ:destroyer); आहोपुरुषिका (āhopuruṣikā) – pride;

contentBlocks.original:
सुधासिन्धोर्मध्ये सुरविटपिवाटीपरिवृते
मणिद्वीपे नीपोपवनवति चिन्तामणिगृहे ।
शिवाकारे मञ्चे परमशिवपर्यङ्कनिलयां
भजन्ति त्वां धन्याः कतिचन चिदानन्दलहरीम् ॥ ८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhāsindhormadhye suraviṭapivāṭīparivṛte
maṇidvīpe nīpopavanavati cintāmaṇigṛhe .
śivākāre mañce paramaśivaparyaṅkanilayāṃ
bhajanti tvāṃ dhanyāḥ katicana cidānandalaharīm .. 8..
contentBlocks.translation: Fortunate indeed are the few who adore Thee, the Inundation of Bliss-Consciousness, as abiding on a mattress, that is Paramaśiva (the Supreme Śiva or Sadāśiva) spread on a couch, which too is an aspect of Śiva, placed in a chamber of wish-yielding gems (Cintāmaṇi), amidst a Nipa pleasure garden, in Maṇidvīpa (Isle of Gems), which is situated in the Nectar-Ocean and fringed by rows of celestial Kalpaka trees. 8.
contentBlocks.meanings: सुधासिन्धोः (sudhāsindhoḥ) – of the nectar-ocean (sudhā:nectar | sindhoḥ:of the ocean); मध्ये (madhye) – in the middle; सुरविटपिवाटीपरिवृते (suraviṭapivāṭīparivṛte) – surrounded by divine trees (sura:divine | viṭapi:trees | vāṭī:garden | parivṛte:surrounded by); मणिद्वीपे (maṇidvīpe) – in the isle of gems (maṇi:gems | dvīpe:in the isle); नीपोपवनवति (nīpopavanavati) – having nipa groves (nīpa:nipa | upavana:groves | vati:having); चिन्तामणिगृहे (cintāmaṇigṛhe) – in the house of wish-gems (cintāmaṇi:wish-gem | gṛhe:in the house); शिवाकारे (śivākāre) – in the form of Śiva (śiva:Śiva | ākāre:in the form); मञ्चे (mañce) – on the couch; परमशिवपर्यङ्कनिलयाम् (paramaśivaparyaṅkanilayām) – abiding on Śiva's couch (parama:supreme | śiva:Śiva | paryaṅka:couch | nilayām:abiding); भजन्ति (bhajanti) – adore; त्वाम् (tvām) – Thee; धन्याः (dhanyāḥ) – fortunate; कतिचन (katicana) – a few; चिदानन्दलहरीम् (cidānandalaharīm) – wave of bliss-consciousness (cit:consciousness | ānanda:bliss | laharīm:wave);

contentBlocks.original:
महीं मूलाधारे कमपि मणिपूरे हुतवहं
स्थितं स्वाधिष्ठाने हृदि मरुतमाकाशमुपरि ।
मनोऽपि भ्रूमध्ये सकलमपि भित्वा कुलपथं
सहस्रारे पद्मे सह रहसि पत्या विहरसे ॥ ९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahīṃ mūlādhāre kamapi maṇipūre hutavahaṃ
sthitaṃ svādhiṣṭhāne hṛdi marutamākāśamupari .
mano'pi bhrūmadhye sakalamapi bhitvā kulapathaṃ
sahasrāre padme saha rahasi patyā viharase .. 9..
contentBlocks.translation: Having penetrated the Prithvi (Earth) element situated in the Mulādhāra, the Jala (Water) element in the Manipūra, the Agni (Fire) element in the Svādhiṣthāna, the Vāyu (Air) element in the Heart or Anāhata, the Ākāśa (Sky) element above the former in the Visuddhi, and Manas (mind) in the Ājñā between the eye-brows, Thou, ascending through the Suṣumnā or the Kula Path, sportest with Thy Consort in the solitude of Sahasrāra the Thousand-Petalled Lotus (above in the head). 9.
contentBlocks.meanings: महीं (mahīm) – earth; मूलाधारे (mūlādhāre) – in the Mūlādhāra (mūla:root | ādhāre:in the base); कमपि (kamapi) – some; मणिपूरे (maṇipūre) – in the Maṇipūra (maṇi:gem | pūre:in the city); हुतवहं (hutavahaṃ) – fire; स्थितं (sthitaṃ) – situated; स्वाधिष्ठाने (svādhiṣṭhāne) – in the Svādhiṣṭhāna (sva:own | adhiṣṭhāne:in the seat); हृदि (hṛdi) – in the heart; मरुतम् (marutam) – air; आकाशम् (ākāśam) – sky; उपरि (upari) – above; मनः (manaḥ) – mind; अपि (api) – also; भ्रूमध्ये (bhrūmadhye) – between the eyebrows (bhrū:eyebrow | madhye:in the middle); सकलम् (sakalam) – all; अपि (api) – also; भित्वा (bhitvā) – having penetrated; कुलपथं (kulapathaṃ) – Kula path (kula:family | pathaṃ:path); सहस्रारे (sahasrāre) – in the Sahasrāra (sahasra:thousand | āre:in the petal); पद्मे (padme) – in the lotus; सह (saha) – with; रहसि (rahasi) – in solitude; पत्या (patyā) – with the consort; विहरसे (viharase) – you sport;

contentBlocks.original:
सुधाधारासारैश्चरणयुगलान्तर्विगलितैः
प्रपञ्चं सिञ्चन्ती पुनरपि रसाम्नायमहसः ।
अवाप्य स्वां भूमिं भुजगनिभमध्युष्टवलयं
स्वमात्मानं कृत्वा स्वपिषि कुलकुण्डे कुहरिणि ॥ १०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhā-dhārā-sāraiś caraṇa-yugalāntar vigalitaiḥ
prapañcaṃ siñcantī punarapi rasāmnāyamahasaḥ .
avāpya svāṃ bhūmiṃ bhujaganibhamadhyuṣṭavalayaṃ
svamātmānaṃ kṛtvā svapiṣi kulakuṇḍe kuhariṇi .. 10..
contentBlocks.translation: Drenching the whole manifested multiplicity (the prapañca, here meaning the seventy two thousand Nādis of the Jīva) with the nectar flowing from Thy feet, Thou (the Kuṇḍalinī representing Tripurasundarī) descendest from the exuberance of the Nectarine Radiance of the Moon (here the Sahasrāra where the Kuṇḍalinī unites with Śiva) into the hollow of Thy own sphere in the Mūlādhāra at the lower end of the Suṣumnā, assuming Thy serpentine form (of three and a half coils) and sleepest therein. 10.
contentBlocks.meanings: सुधाधारासारैः (sudhā-dhārā-sāraiḥ) – with essence of nectar streams (sudhā:nectar | dhārā:streams | sāraiḥ:with essence); चरणयुगलान्तः (caraṇa-yugala-antar) – between the pair of feet (caraṇa:feet | yugala:pair | antar:between); विगलितैः (vigalitaiḥ) – flowing; प्रपञ्चं (prapañcam) – the manifested multiplicity; सिञ्चन्ती (siñcantī) – drenching; पुनरपि (punarapi) – again; रसाम्नायमहसः (rasāmnāya-mahasaḥ) – from the Nectarine Radiance (rasa:nectar | āmnāya:tradition | mahasaḥ:radiance); अवाप्य (avāpya) – having reached; स्वां (svām) – own; भूमिं (bhūmim) – sphere; भुजगनिभमध्युष्टवलयम् (bhujaga-nibha-madhyuṣṭa-valayam) – serpentine form with coils (bhujaga:serpent | nibha:like | madhyuṣṭa:inhabited | valayam:coils); स्वमात्मानं (svamātmānam) – own self; कृत्वा (kṛtvā) – having made; स्वपिषि (svapiṣi) – sleepest; कुलकुण्डे (kulakuṇḍe) – in the kula-kunda (kula:family | kuṇḍe:in the kunda); कुहरिणि (kuhariṇi) – in the hollow;

contentBlocks.original:
चतुर्भिः श्रीकण्ठैः शिवयुवतिभिः पञ्चभिरपि
प्रभिन्नाभिः शम्भोर्नवभिरपि मूलप्रकृतिभिः ।
चतुश्चत्वारिंशद्वसुदलकलाश्रत्रिवलय-
त्रिरेखाभिः सार्धं तव शरणकोणाः परिणताः ॥ ११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
caturbhiḥ śrīkaṇṭhaiḥ śivayuvatibhiḥ pañcabhirapi
prabhinnābhiḥ śambhornavabhirapi mūlaprakṛtibhiḥ .
catuś-catvārimśad vasu-dala-kalāśra-tri-valaya-
trirekhābhiḥ sārdhaṃ tava śaraṇakoṇāḥ pariṇatāḥ .. 11..
contentBlocks.translation: The four Śrī-Kaṇṭhas (Siva-cakras) and the five Śiva-yuvatis (Śakti-cakras) are the nine Mūla-prakṛtis or basic manifestations, and these are apart from Śambhu (the Bindu or small circle in the centre). Then there are two lotuses, one of eight petals and another of sixteen, besides three surrounding circles and three lines. This forms Thy mansion with forty-four Konas (triangles). 11.
contentBlocks.meanings: चतुर्भिः (caturbhiḥ) – by four; श्रीकण्ठैः (śrīkaṇṭhaiḥ) – by Śrī-Kaṇṭhas (śrī:Śrī | kaṇṭhaiḥ:by Kaṇṭhas); शिवयुवतिभिः (śivayuvatibhiḥ) – by Śiva-yuvatis (śiva:Śiva | yuvatibhiḥ:by yuvatis); पञ्चभिरपि (pañcabhirapi) – by five also (pañcabhiḥ:by five | api:also); प्रभिन्नाभिः (prabhinnābhiḥ) – by distinct; शम्भोः (śambhoḥ) – of Śambhu; नवभिरपि (navabhirapi) – by nine also (navabhiḥ:by nine | api:also); मूलप्रकृतिभिः (mūlaprakṛtibhiḥ) – by basic manifestations (mūla:basic | prakṛtibhiḥ:by manifestations); चतुश्चत्वारिंशद्वसुदलकलाश्रत्रिवलय- (catuścatvāriṃśadvasudalakālāśratrivalaya-) – forty-four petal, circle (catuḥ:four | catvāriṃśat:forty | vasu:petal | dala:petal | kalā:circle | āśra:circle | tri:three | valaya:circle); त्रिरेखाभिः (trirekhābhiḥ) – by three lines (tri:three | rekhābhiḥ:by lines); सार्धं (sārdhaṃ) – together with; तव (tava) – your; शरणकोणाः (śaraṇakoṇāḥ) – shelter triangles (śaraṇa:shelter | koṇāḥ:triangles); परिणताः (pariṇatāḥ) – formed;

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदीयं सौन्दर्यं तुहिनगिरिकन्ये तुलयितुं
कवीन्द्राः कल्पन्ते कथमपि विरिञ्चिप्रभृतयः ।
यदालोकौत्सुक्यादमरललना यान्ति मनसा
तपोभिर्दुष्प्रापामपि गिरिशसायुज्यपदवीम् ॥ १२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadīyaṃ saundaryaṃ tuhinagirikanye tulayituṃ
kavīndrāḥ kalpante kathamapi viriñciprabhṛtayaḥ .
yadālokautsukyādamaralalanā yānti manasā
tapobhirduṣprāpāmapi giriśasāyujyapadavīm .. 12..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Mountain of Snow! The greatest of poets like Brahmā—in trying assiduously to portray Thy beauty, fail to find any other object to describe it through comparison. For, even the heavenly damsels (who are the most noted entities available to compare in respect of beauty) attain, out of their eagerness to experience Thy beauty, only to an imaginative identification with the State of Oneness with Siva, which is difficult to gain even by severe austerities. (They seek to do so because only Thy Eternal Consort Śiva has the privilege of absorption in Thy beauty, and oneness with Him alone can help one experience it.) 12.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वदीयं (tvadīyaṃ) – your; सौन्दर्यं (saundaryaṃ) – beauty; तुइनगिरिकन्ये (tuhinagirikanye) – O Daughter of the Mountain of Snow (tuhina:snow | giri:mountain | kanye:daughter); तुलयितुं (tulayituṃ) – to compare; कवीन्द्राः (kavīndrāḥ) – great poets (kavi:poet | indra:lord); कल्पन्ते (kalpante) – attempt; कथमपि (kathamapi) – somehow; विरिञ्चिप्रभृतयः (viriñciprabhṛtayaḥ) – Brahmā and others (viriñci:Brahmā | prabhṛtayaḥ:and others); यदालोकौत्सुक्यात् (yadālokautsukyāt) – due to eagerness to see (yad:which | āloka:seeing | autsukyāt:due to eagerness); अमरललना (amaralalanā) – heavenly damsels (amara:immortal | lalanā:damsels); यान्ति (yānti) – attain; मनसा (manasā) – by mind; तपोभिः (tapobhiḥ) – by austerities; दुष्प्रापामपि (duṣprāpāmapi) – even though difficult to attain (duṣprāpām:difficult to attain | api:even though); गिरिशसायुज्यपदवीम् (giriśasāyujyapadavīm) – oneness with Śiva (giriśa:Śiva | sāyujya:oneness | padavīm:path);

contentBlocks.original:
नरं वर्षीयांसं नयनविरसं नर्मसु जडं
तवापाङ्गालोके पतितमनुधावन्ति शतशः ।
गलद्वेणीबन्धाः कुचकलशविस्रस्तसिचया
हठात् त्रुट्यत्काञ्च्यो विगलितदुकूला युवतयः ॥ १३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
naraṃ varṣīyāṃsaṃ nayanavirasaṃ narmasu jaḍaṃ
tavāpāṅgāloke patitamanudhāvanti śataśaḥ .
galadveṇībandhāḥ kucakalaśavisrastasicayā
haṭhāt truṭyatkāñcyo vigalitadukūlā yuvatayaḥ .. 13..
contentBlocks.translation: If Thy gracious side glance falls on even a very decrepit old man who is ugly to look at, and whose erotic sensibilities are dead, he will be followed in all haste in their hundreds by love-lorn young women having their locks scattered, their rotund breasts exposed by the loosening of their inner-garmerts, and their girdles suddenly broken in excitement, thus letting their wearing clothes slip down. 13.
contentBlocks.meanings: नरं (naraṃ) – man; वर्षीयांसं (varṣīyāṃsaṃ) – very old; नयनविरसं (nayanavirasaṃ) – ugly to look at (nayana:eye | virasaṃ:tasteless); नर्मसु (narmasu) – in erotic matters; जडं (jaḍaṃ) – dull; तव (tava) – your; अपाङ्गालोके (apāṅgāloke) – side glance (apāṅga:side | āloke:glance); पतितम् (patitam) – fallen; अनुधावन्ति (anudhāvanti) – follow; शतशः (śataśaḥ) – in hundreds; गलद्वेणीबन्धाः (galadveṇībandhāḥ) – with loosened hair (galad:loosened | veṇībandhāḥ:hair ties); कुचकलशविस्रस्तसिचया (kucakalaśavisrastasicayā) – with exposed breasts (kuca:breast | kalaśa:pot | visrasta:exposed | sicayā:heap); हठात् (haṭhāt) – suddenly; त्रुट्यत्काञ्च्यः (truṭyatkāñcyaḥ) – with broken girdles (truṭyat:broken | kāñcyaḥ:girdles); विगलितदुकूला (vigalitadukūlā) – with slipping clothes (vigalita:slipping | dukūlā:clothes); युवतयः (yuvatayaḥ) – young women;

contentBlocks.original:
क्षितौ षट्पञ्चाशद् द्विसमधिकपञ्चाशदुदके
हुताशे द्वाषष्टिश्चतुरधिकपञ्चाशदनिले ।
दिवि द्विष्षट्त्रिंशन्मनसि च चतुष्षष्टिरिति ये
मयूखास्तेषामप्युपरि तव पादाम्बुजयुगम् ॥ १४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣitau ṣaṭpañcāśad dvisamadhikapañcāśadudake
hutāśe dvāṣaṣṭiścaturadhikapañcāśadanile .
divi dviṣṣaṭtriṃśanmanasi ca catuṣṣaṣṭiriti ye
mayūkhāsteṣāmapyupari tava pādāmbujayugam .. 14..
contentBlocks.translation: In the Bindu in the centre of the Sahasrāra is Thy transcendent Station (pādāmbuja-yugam or pair of lotus feet) far above the Cakras to which Thy Rays (or luminous-manifestation as Śaktis) reach in the following combinations—fifty six in the Mūlādhāra which partakes of the character of Prithvi (Earth Element), fifty-two in Maṇipūra which partakes of the character of Jala (Water Element), sixty two in the Svādhiṣṭhāna which partakes of the character of Agni (Fire Element), fifty four in Anahata which partakes of the character of Vayu (Air Element), seventy-two in Viśuddhi which partakes of the nature of Akasa (Ether Element), and sixty four in Ājñā which partakes of the character of Manas (Mind). 14.
contentBlocks.meanings: क्षितौ (kṣitau) – on the earth; षट्पञ्चाशत् (ṣaṭpañcāśat) – fifty-six (ṣaṭ:six | pañcāśat:fifty); द्विसमधिकपञ्चाशत् (dvisamadhikapañcāśat) – fifty-two (dvi:two | samadhika:more than | pañcāśat:fifty); उदके (udake) – in water; हुताशे (hutāśe) – in fire; द्वाषष्टिः (dvāṣaṣṭiḥ) – sixty-two (dva:two | ṣaṣṭiḥ:sixty); चतुरधिकपञ्चाशत् (caturadhikapañcāśat) – fifty-four (catur:four | adhika:more than | pañcāśat:fifty); अनिले (anile) – in air; दिवि (divi) – in the sky; द्विष्षट्त्रिंशत् (dviṣṣaṭtriṃśat) – thirty-two (dvi:two | ṣaṭtriṃśat:thirty); मनसि (manasi) – in the mind; च (ca) – and; चतुष्षष्टिः (catuṣṣaṣṭiḥ) – sixty-four (catuḥ:four | ṣaṣṭiḥ:sixty); इति (iti) – thus; ये (ye) – those who; मयूखाः (mayūkhāḥ) – rays; तेषाम् (teṣām) – of them; अपि (api) – also; उपरि (upari) – above; तव (tava) – your; पादाम्बुजयुगम् (pādāmbujayugam) – pair of lotus feet (pāda:feet | ambuja:lotus | yugam:pair);

contentBlocks.original:
शरज्ज्योत्स्नाशुद्धां शशियुतजटाजूटमकुटां
वरत्रासत्राणस्फटिकघटिकापुस्तककराम् ।
सकृन्न त्वा नत्वा कथमिव सतां संन्निदधते
मधुक्षीरद्राक्षामधुरिमधुरीणाः फणितयः ॥ १५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śarajjyotsnāśuddhāṃ śaśiyutajaṭājūṭamakuṭāṃ
vara-trāsa-trāṇa -sphaṭika-ghuṭikā-pustaka karām .
sakṛnna tvā natvā kathamiva satāṃ saṃnnidadhate
madhukṣīradrākṣāmadhurimadhurīṇāḥ bhaṇitayaḥ .. 15..
contentBlocks.translation: How can torrents of words, excelling even honey, milk and grapes, help flowing from the mouth of good men who but once make prostration to Thee who art endowed with the lustre of the autumnal moon, who art holding Thy two hands in the pose of granting boons and offering protection, and sporting in the other two a rosary of crystal beads and a book, and who wearest the crescent moon in Thy crown of plaited locks! 15.
contentBlocks.meanings: शरज्ज्योत्स्नाशुद्धां (śarajjyotsnāśuddhām) – pure as autumn moonlight (śarad:autumn | jyotsnā:moonlight | śuddhām:pure); शशियुतजटाजूटमकुटां (śaśiyutajaṭājūṭamakuṭām) – crowned with moon-adorned matted hair (śaśiyuta:moon-adorned | jaṭā:matted hair | jūṭa:crown | makuṭām:crowned); वरत्रासत्राणस्फटिकघटिकापुस्तककराम् (varatrāsatrāṇasphaṭikaghaṭikāpustakakarām) – hands granting boons, protection, holding crystal beads, book (vara:boon | trāsa:fear | trāṇa:protection | sphaṭika:crystal | ghaṭikā:beads | pustaka:book | karām:hands); सकृन्न (sakṛnna) – once; त्वा (tvā) – Thee; नत्वा (natvā) – having bowed; कथमिव (kathamiva) – how indeed; सतां (satām) – of good men; संन्निदधते (saṃnnidadhate) – flow; मधुक्षीरद्राक्षामधुरिमधुरीणाः (madhukṣīradrākṣāmadhurimadhurīṇāḥ) – sweet as honey, milk, grapes (madhu:honey | kṣīra:milk | drākṣā:grapes | madhurima:sweetness | dhurīṇāḥ:sweet); फणितयः (phaṇitayaḥ) – words;

contentBlocks.original:
कवीन्द्राणां चेतःकमलवनबालातपरुचिं
भजन्ते ये सन्तः कतिचिदरुणामेव भवतीम् ।
विरिञ्चिप्रेयस्यास्तरुणतरश‍ृङ्गारलहरी-
गभीराभिर्वाग्भिर्विदधति सतां रञ्जनममी ॥ १६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kavīndrāṇāṃ cetaḥkamalavanabālātaparuciṃ
bhajante ye santaḥ katicidaruṇāmeva bhavatīm .
viriñci-preyasyās taruṇatara śrṅgāra-laharī-
gabhīrābhirvāgbhirvidadhati satāṃ rañjanamamī .. 16..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother, Thou Aruṇā, the Crimson-coloured Goddess, art like the light of morning’s rising sun to the lotus flowers constituted of the minds of gifted poets (helping, as Thou dost, their poesy to blossom forth). Therefore, those devoted men who adore Thee become capable of delighting the minds of assemblies of literary connoisseurs with the majestic flow of their words surging like waves of erotic sentiments emanating from youthful Sarasvati, the Goddess of Learning. 16.
contentBlocks.meanings: कवीन्द्राणां (kavīndrāṇām) – of the best poets (kavi:poet | indra:best | ṇām:of); चेतःकमलवनबालातपरुचिं (cetaḥkamalavanabālātaparuciṃ) – light of morning's rising sun (cetaḥ:mind | kamala:lotus | vana:forest | bāla:young | āta:rising | ruciṃ:light); भजन्ते (bhajante) – adore; ये (ye) – who; सन्तः (santaḥ) – devoted men; कतिचित् (katicit) – some; अरुणाम् (aruṇām) – Aruṇā; एव (eva) – indeed; भवतीम् (bhavatīm) – Thou; विरिञ्चिप्रेयस्याः (viriñcipreyasyāḥ) – of Sarasvati, beloved of Brahma (viriñci:Brahma | preyasyāḥ:beloved of); तरुणतर (taruṇatara) – more youthful; श‍ृङ्गारलहरी (śṛṅgāralaharī) – waves of erotic sentiments (śṛṅgāra:erotic sentiments | laharī:waves); गभीराभिः (gabhīrābhiḥ) – profound; वाग्भिः (vāgbhiḥ) – with words; विधधति (vidadhati) – create; सताम् (satām) – of the good; रञ्जनम् (rañjanam) – delight; अमी (amī) – these;

contentBlocks.original:
सवित्रीभिर्वाचां शशिमणिशिलाभङ्गरुचिभिः
वशिन्याद्याभिस्त्वां सह जननि संचिन्तयति यः ।
स कर्ता काव्यानां भवति महतां भङ्गिरुचिभिः
वचोभिर्वाग्देवीवदनकमलामोदमधुरैः ॥ १७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
savitrībhirvācāṃ śaśimaṇiśilābhaṅgarucibhiḥ
vaśinyādyābhistvāṃ saha janani saṃcintayati yaḥ .
sa kartā kāvyānāṃ bhavati mahatāṃ bhaṅgirucibhiḥ
vacobhir vāgdevī-vadana-kamal’āmoda madhuraiḥ .. 17..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! Those who meditate on Thee in association with Vaśinī and allied Deities—who are all the sources of speech and whose radiance resembles the lustre of freshly cut Candrakānta gem (moon stone)—can become the authors of poetical works as delightful as those of great ones (like Vālmīkī and Kālidāsa), and sweet with the fragrance of the mouth of Sarasvatī, the goddess of poesy and learning. 17.
contentBlocks.meanings: सवित्रीभिः (savitrībhiḥ) – with Savitrī; वाचाम् (vācām) – of words; शशिमणिशिलाभङ्गरुचिभिः (śaśimaṇiśilābhaṅgarucibhiḥ) – with moonstone-like radiance (śaśi:moon | maṇi:gem | śilā:stone | bhaṅga:like | ruci:radiance | bhiḥ:with); वशिन्याद्याभिः (vaśinyādyābhiḥ) – with Vaśinī and others (vaśinī:Vaśinī | ādi:and others | ābhiḥ:with); त्वाम् (tvām) – you; सह (saha) – together with; जननि (janani) – O Mother; संचिन्तयति (saṃcintayati) – meditates; यः (yaḥ) – who; स (sa) – he; कर्त्ता (kartā) – author; काव्यानाम् (kāvyānām) – of poems; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; महताम् (mahatām) – of great ones; भङ्गिरुचिभिः (bhaṅgirucibhiḥ) – with delightful radiance (bhaṅgi:delightful | ruci:radiance | bhiḥ:with); वचोभिः (vacobhiḥ) – with words; वाग्देवीवदनकमलामोदमधुरैः (vāgdevī-vadana-kamal’āmoda-madhuraiḥ) – sweet with Sarasvatī's fragrance (vāgdevī:Sarasvatī | vadana:mouth | kamala:lotus | āmoda:fragrance | madhuraiḥ:sweet with);

contentBlocks.original:
तनुच्छायाभिस्ते तरुणतरणिश्रीसरणिभिः
दिवं सर्वामुर्वीमरुणिमनि मग्नां स्मरति यः ।
भवन्त्यस्य त्रस्यद्वनहरिणशालीननयनाः
सहोर्वश्या वश्याः कति कति न गीर्वाणगणिकाः ॥ १८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tanucchāyābhiste taruṇataraṇiśrīsaraṇibhiḥ
divaṃ sarvāmurvīmaruṇimani magnāṃ smarati yaḥ .
bhavantyasya trasyadvanahariṇaśālīnanayanāḥ
sahorvaśyā vaśyāḥ kati kati na gīrvāṇagaṇikāḥ .. 18..
contentBlocks.translation: How can numerous celestial courtesans like Urvaśī with eyes resembling in beauty those of timid does of the forest, help being attracted by a person who meditates on the beauty of Thy form which bathes the heaven and the earth in its crimson radiance resembling the rising sun. 18.
contentBlocks.meanings: तनुच्छायाभिः (tanucchāyābhiḥ) – with delicate shadows (tanu:delicate | chāyābhiḥ:with shadows); ते (te) – your; तरुणतरणिश्रीसरणिभिः (taruṇataraṇiśrīsaraṇibhiḥ) – with the beauty of the rising sun (taruṇa:rising | taraṇi:sun | śrī:beauty | saraṇibhiḥ:with paths); दिवं (divaṃ) – heaven; सर्वाम् (sarvām) – all; उर्वीम् (urvīm) – earth; अरुणिमनि (aruṇimani) – in crimson radiance (aruṇa:crimson | mani:radiance); मग्नाम् (magnām) – immersed; स्मरति (smarati) – remembers; यः (yaḥ) – who; भवन्ति (bhavanti) – become; अस्य (asya) – his; त्रस्यद्वनहरिणशालीननयनाः (trasyadvanahariṇaśālīnanayanāḥ) – with eyes like timid deer (trasyad:timid | vana:forest | hariṇa:deer | śālīna:graceful | nayanāḥ:eyes); सह (saha) – with; उर्वश्या (urvaśyā) – Urvaśī; वश्याः (vaśyāḥ) – subdued; कति (kati) – how many; कति (kati) – how many; न (na) – not; गीर्वाणगणिकाः (gīrvāṇagaṇikāḥ) – celestial courtesans (gīrvāṇa:celestial | gaṇikāḥ:courtesans);

contentBlocks.original:
मुखं बिन्दुं कृत्वा कुचयुगमधस्तस्य तदधो
हरार्धं ध्यायेद्यो हरमहिषि ते मन्मथकलाम् ।
स सद्यः संक्षोभं नयति वनिता इत्यतिलघु
त्रिलोकीमप्याशु भ्रमयति रवीन्दुस्तनयुगाम् ॥ १९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mukhaṃ binduṃ kṛtvā kucayugamadhastasya tadadho
harārdhaṃ dhyāyedyo haramahiṣi te manmathakalām .
sa sadyaḥ saṃkṣobhaṃ nayati vanitā ityatilaghu
trilokīmapyāśu bhramayati ravīndustanayugām .. 19..
contentBlocks.translation: O Consort of Hara! A votary who thinks of a woman’s face in the Bindu (the dot in the triangle), her twin breasts below it and ‘the half of Hara’ (harārdha), still below, and contemplates Thy Manmathakalā on those spots (and thus gets identified with her in meditation) will quickly stir the mind of any woman. This indeed is a trifle for him. For, in no time he can fascinate even the Triloki (the three worlds conceived as a woman) with the sun and moon as breasts. 19.
contentBlocks.meanings: मुखं (mukhaṃ) – face; बिन्दुं (binduṃ) – dot; कृत्वा (kṛtvā) – having made; कुचयुगम् (kucayugam) – pair of breasts (kuca:breast | yugam:pair); अधस्तस्य (adhastasya) – below that; तदधः (tadadho) – below that; हरार्धं (harārdhaṃ) – half of Hara (hara:Hara (Śiva) | ardham:half); ध्यायेत् (dhyāyet) – should meditate; यः (yaḥ) – who; हरमहिषि (haramahiṣi) – O Consort of Hara (hara:Hara (Śiva) | mahiṣi:consort); ते (te) – your; मन्मथकलाम् (manmathakalām) – Manmatha art (manmatha:Manmatha (Cupid) | kalām:art); स (sa) – he; सद्यः (sadyaḥ) – quickly; संक्षोभं (saṃkṣobhaṃ) – agitation; नयति (nayati) – leads; वनिता (vanitā) – woman; इति (iti) – thus; अतिलघु (atilaghu) – very easy; त्रिलोकीम् (trilokīm) – three worlds (tri:three | lokīm:worlds); अपि (api) – even; आशु (āśu) – quickly; भ्रमयति (bhramayati) – fascinates; रवीन्दु (ravīndu) – sun and moon (ravi:sun | indu:moon); स्तनयुगाम् (stanayugām) – pair of breasts (stana:breast | yugām:pair);

contentBlocks.original:
किरन्तीमङ्गेभ्यः किरणनिकुरम्बामृतरसं
हृदि त्वामाधत्ते हिमकरशिलामूर्तिमिव यः ।
स सर्पाणां दर्पं शमयति शकुन्ताधिप इव
ज्वरप्लुष्टान् दृष्ट्या सुखयति सुधाधारसिरया ॥ २०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kirantīmaṅgebhyaḥ kiraṇanikurambāmṛtarasaṃ
hṛdi tvāmādhatte himakaraśilāmūrtimiva yaḥ .
sa sarpāṇāṃ darpaṃ śamayati śakuntādhipa iva
jvarapluṣṭān dṛṣṭyā sukhayati sudhādhārasirayā .. 20..
contentBlocks.translation: He who meditates on Thee, who sends forth luminous waves of Bliss from Thy body as an image of moonstone does, will be capable of humbling the pride and ferocity of serpents by his mere look, like Garuḍa himself. Nay, like the nectar showering Nādi (nerve), his look can cure any ailment, including the afflictions of fever. 20.
contentBlocks.meanings: किरन्तीम् (kirantīm) – emitting; अङ्गेभ्यः (aṅgebhyaḥ) – from the limbs; किरणनिकुरम्बामृतरसं (kiraṇanikurambāmṛtarasaṃ) – nectar of rays cluster (kiraṇa:rays | nikuramba:cluster | amṛtarasa:nectar); हृदि (hṛdi) – in the heart; त्वाम् (tvām) – you; आधत्ते (ādhatte) – places; हिमकरशिलामूर्तिमिव (himakaraśilāmūrtimiva) – like a moonstone image (himakara:moon | śilā:stone | mūrti:image | iva:like); यः (yaḥ) – who; स (sa) – he; सर्पाणां (sarpāṇāṃ) – of serpents; दर्पं (darpaṃ) – pride; शमयति (śamayati) – pacifies; शकुन्ताधिप (śakuntādhipa) – Garuḍa (śakunta:birds | adhipa:lord); इव (iva) – like; ज्वरप्लुष्टान् (jvarapluṣṭān) – afflicted by fever (jvara:fever | pluṣṭān:afflicted); दृष्ट्या (dṛṣṭyā) – by look; सुखयति (sukhayati) – comforts; सुधाधारसिरया (sudhādhārasirayā) – nectar showering nerve (sudhā:nectar | dhāra:shower | sirā:nerve);

contentBlocks.original:
तटिल्लेखातन्वीं तपनशशिवैश्वानरमयीं
निषण्णां षण्णामप्युपरि कमलानां तव कलाम् ।
महापद्माटव्यां मृदितमलमायेन मनसा
महान्तः पश्यन्तो दधति परमाह्लादलहरीम् ॥ २१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṭillekhātanvīṃ tapanaśaśivaiśvānaramayīṃ
niṣaṇṇāṃ ṣaṇṇāmapyupari kamalānāṃ tava kalām .
mahāpadmāṭavyāṃ mṛditamalamāyena manasā
mahāntaḥ paśyanto dadhati paramāhlādalaharīm .. 21..
contentBlocks.translation: The noble spiritual aspirants, whose minds are free from impurities like lust and greed and from the hold of Māyā consisting of ignorance, egotism and the like, are filled with thrills of spiritual Bliss by experiencing Thy lightning-like Kalā (Śakti in union with Śiva known as Sadākhyā) in the core of the Thousand-petalled Lotus, which transcends the six lotuses beginning with the Mūlādhāra inclusive of Thy manifestations as fire, sun and moon in them. 21.
contentBlocks.meanings: तटिल्लेखातन्वीं (taṭillekhātanvīṃ) – lightning-like slender (taṭillekhā:lightning-like | tanvīṃ:slender); तपनशशिवैश्वानरमयीं (tapanaśaśivaiśvānaramayīṃ) – sun, moon, fire embodied (tapana:sun | śaśi:moon | vaiśvānara:fire | mayīṃ:embodied); निषण्णां (niṣaṇṇāṃ) – situated; षण्णामपि (ṣaṇṇāmapi) – even above six (ṣaṇṇām:of six | api:even); उपरि (upari) – above; कमलानां (kamalānāṃ) – of lotuses; तव (tava) – your; कलाम् (kalām) – part; महापद्माटव्यां (mahāpadmāṭavyāṃ) – in the great lotus forest (mahā:great | padma:lotus | āṭavyāṃ:in the forest); मृदितमलमायेन (mṛditamalamāyena) – by purified mind (mṛdita:purified | mala:impurity | māyena:by mind); मनसा (manasā) – by mind; महान्तः (mahāntaḥ) – great souls; पश्यन्तः (paśyantaḥ) – seeing; दधति (dadhati) – experience; परमाह्लादलहरीम् (paramāhlādalaharīm) – wave of supreme bliss (parama:supreme | āhlāda:bliss | laharīm:wave);

contentBlocks.original:
भवानि त्वं दासे मयि वितर दृष्टिं सकरुणा-
मिति स्तोतुं वाञ्छन् कथयति भवानि त्वमिति यः ।
तदैव त्वं तस्मै दिशसि निजसायुज्यपदवीं
मुकुन्दब्रह्मेन्द्रस्फुटमकुटनीराजितपदाम् ॥ २२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavāni tvaṃ dāse mayi vitara dṛṣṭiṃ sakaruṇā-
miti stotuṃ vāñchan kathayati bhavāni tvamiti yaḥ .
tadaiva tvaṃ tasmai diśasi nijasāyujyapadavīṃ
mukunda-brahmendra-sphuṭa-makuṭa-nīrājita-padām .. 22..
contentBlocks.translation: Whoever, desiring to pray to Thee in terms like ‘Oh Bhavāni. bestow Thy gracious glance on me, Thy servant, even before he utters ‘ Bhavāni tvam ’ (May I) become Thou’, Thou art wont to bestow on him the status of oneness with Thy feet, at which Divinities like Viṣṇu, Brahmā and Indra are performing the lustration ceremony with the brilliance of their diadems (as they bow down their heads in prostration). 22.
contentBlocks.meanings: भवानि (bhavāni) – O Bhavani; त्वं (tvaṃ) – Thou; दासे (dāse) – to servant; मयि (mayi) – in me; वितर (vitara) – bestow; दृष्टिं (dṛṣṭiṃ) – glance; सकरुणा (sakaruṇā) – with compassion; इति (iti) – thus; स्तोतुं (stotuṃ) – to praise; वाञ्छन् (vāñchan) – desiring; कथयति (kathayati) – says; भवानि (bhavāni) – O Bhavani; त्वम् (tvam) – Thou; इति (iti) – thus; यः (yaḥ) – who; तदा (tadā) – then; एव (eva) – indeed; त्वं (tvaṃ) – Thou; तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; दिशसि (diśasi) – grant; निजसायुज्यपदवीं (nijasāyujyapadavīṃ) – state of oneness (nija:own | sāyujya:oneness | padavīṃ:state); मुकुन्दब्रह्मेन्द्रस्फुटमकुटनीराजितपदाम् (mukundabrahmendrasphuṭamakuṭanīrājitapadām) – feet worshipped by gods (mukunda:Vishnu | brahma:Brahma | indra:Indra | sphuṭa:clear | makuṭa:crown | nīrājita:worshipped | padām:feet);

contentBlocks.original:
त्वया हृत्वा वामं वपुरपरितृप्तेन मनसा
शरीरार्धं शम्भोरपरमपि शङ्के हृतमभूत् ।
यदेतत्त्वद्रूपं सकलमरुणाभं त्रिनयनं
कुचाभ्यामानम्रं कुटिलशशिचूडालमकुटम् ॥ २३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvayā hṛtvā vāmaṃ vapuraparitṛptena manasā
śarīrārdhaṃ śambhoraparamapi śaṅke hṛtamabhūt .
yadetattvadrūpaṃ sakalamaruṇābhaṃ trinayanaṃ
kucābhyāmānamraṃ kuṭilaśaśicūḍālamakuṭam .. 23..
contentBlocks.translation: I have a feeling that, unsatisfied even after having appropriated the left half of Śambhu (Śiva) as Ardhanarīśvara, (a form half man and half woman), Thou hast also invaded his right half; for Thy form that shines in my heart is totally crimson in complexion and slightly bent by the weight of the two breasts, besides having three eyes and the crescent moon in the diadem. 23.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्वया (tvayā) – by you; हृत्वा (hṛtvā) – having appropriated; वामं (vāmaṃ) – left; वपुः (vapuḥ) – form; अपरितृप्तेन (aparitṛptena) – unsatisfied; मनसा (manasā) – by the mind; शरीरार्धं (śarīrārdhaṃ) – half of the body (śarīra:body | ardhaṃ:half); शम्भोः (śambhoḥ) – of Śambhu; अपरम् (aparam) – other; अपि (api) – also; शङ्के (śaṅke) – I suspect; हृतम् (hṛtam) – taken; अभूत् (abhūt) – became; यत् (yat) – which; एतत् (etat) – this; त्वद्रूपं (tvadrūpaṃ) – your form (tvat:your | rūpaṃ:form); सकलम् (sakalam) – entire; अरुणाभं (aruṇābhaṃ) – crimson in complexion (aruṇa:crimson | ābhaṃ:complexion); त्रिनयनं (trinayanaṃ) – three-eyed (tri:three | nayanaṃ:eye); कुचाभ्याम् (kucābhyām) – by the two breasts (kuca:breast | abhyām:by the two); आनम्रं (ānamraṃ) – slightly bent; कुटिलशशिचूडालमकुटम् (kuṭilaśaśicūḍālamakuṭam) – with crescent moon diadem (kuṭila:curved | śaśi:moon | cūḍāla:crest | makuṭam:diadem);

contentBlocks.original:
जगत्सूते धाता हरिरवति रुद्रः क्षपयते
तिरस्कुर्वन्नेतत्स्वमपि वपुरीशस्तिरयति ।
सदापूर्वः सर्वं तदिदमनुगृह्णाति च शिव-
स्तवाज्ञामालम्ब्य क्षणचलितयोर्भ्रूलतिकयोः ॥ २४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagatsūte dhātā hariravati rudraḥ kṣapayate
tiraskurvannetatsvamapi vapurīśastirayati .
sadāpūrvaḥ sarvaṃ tadidamanugṛhṇāti ca śiva-
stavājñāmālambya kṣaṇacalitayorbhrūlatikayoḥ .. 24..
contentBlocks.translation: Brahmā brings forth the universe; Hari (Visṇu) sustains and protects it; Rudra destroys it; and Isa (Maheśvara) absorbs all these Deities (including the universe in involution) into Himself and disappears into Sadāśiva (the Ultimate Category). Then (when it is time for a new cycle of creation to begin) Sadāśiva, on receiving the mandate from Thee by a movement of Thy creeper-like brows, blesses (i. e. manifests and restores) them into activity (as before in the previous cycle). 24.
contentBlocks.meanings: जगत्सूते (jagatsūte) – creates the universe (jagat:universe | sūte:creates); धाता (dhātā) – Brahmā; हरिः (hariḥ) – Hari (Visṇu); अवति (avati) – protects; रुद्रः (rudraḥ) – Rudra; क्षपयते (kṣapayate) – destroys; तिरस्कुर्वन् (tiraskurvan) – concealing; एतत् (etat) – this; स्वम् (svam) – own; अपि (api) – also; वपुः (vapuḥ) – form; ईशः (īśaḥ) – Isa (Maheśvara); तिरयति (tirayati) – absorbs; सदापूर्वः (sadāpūrvaḥ) – eternal; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; तत् (tat) – that; इदम् (idam) – this; अनुगृह्णाति (anugṛhṇāti) – blesses; च (ca) – and; शिवः (śivaḥ) – Śiva; तव (tava) – your; आज्ञाम् (ājñām) – command; आलम्ब्य (ālambya) – receiving; क्षणचलितयोः (kṣaṇacalitayoḥ) – momentarily moved (kṣaṇa:moment | calitayoḥ:moved); भ्रूलतिकयोः (bhrūlatikayoḥ) – of the creeper-like brows (bhrū:brow | latikayoḥ:creeper-like);

contentBlocks.original:
त्रयाणां देवानां त्रिगुणजनितानां तव शिवे
भवेत् पूजा पूजा तव चरणयोर्या विरचिता ।
तथा हि त्वत्पादोद्वहनमणिपीठस्य निकटे
स्थिता ह्येते शश्वन्मुकुलितकरोत्तंसमकुटाः ॥ २५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trayāṇāṃ devānāṃ triguṇajanitānāṃ tava śive
bhavet pūjā pūjā tava caraṇayoryā viracitā .
tathā hi tvatpādodvahanamaṇipīṭhasya nikaṭe
sthitā hyete śaśvanmukulitakarottaṃsamakuṭāḥ .. 25..
contentBlocks.translation: The worship done at Thy feet, O Consort of Śiva, is also the worship of all the three Deities Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva, who have their origin in Thy three Guṇas (Rajas, Sattva and Tamas). They require no special worship, because they are ever waiting with their joined palms held above their diademed heads in salutation to Thee by the side of the foot-stool of diamonds that bear Thy feet. 25.
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रयाणां (trayāṇāṃ) – of the three; देवानां (devānāṃ) – of the deities; त्रिगुणजनितानां (triguṇajanitānāṃ) – born of three qualities (tri:three | guṇa:qualities | janitānāṃ:born of); तव (tava) – your; शिवे (śive) – O Śiva's consort; भवेत् (bhavet) – may be; पूजा (pūjā) – worship; पूजा (pūjā) – worship; तव (tava) – your; चरणयोः (caraṇayoḥ) – of the feet; या (yā) – which; विरचिता (viracitā) – is performed; तथा (tathā) – thus; हि (hi) – indeed; त्वत्पादोद्वहनमणिपीठस्य (tvatpādodvahanamaṇipīṭhasya) – of the gem-studded footstool (tvat:your | pāda:feet | udvahana:bearing | maṇi:gem | pīṭhasya:of the stool); निकटे (nikaṭe) – near; स्थिताः (sthitāḥ) – standing; हि (hi) – indeed; एते (ete) – these; शश्वत् (śaśvat) – always; मुकुलितकरोत्तंसमकुटाः (mukulitakarottaṃsamakuṭāḥ) – with folded hands and crowned (mukulita:folded | kara:hands | uttaṃsa:adorned | makuṭāḥ:crowned);

contentBlocks.original:
विरिञ्चिः पञ्चत्वं व्रजति हरिराप्नोति विरतिं
विनाशं कीनाशो भजति धनदो याति निधनम् ।
वितन्द्री माहेन्द्री विततिरपि संमीलितदृशा
महासंहारेऽस्मिन् विहरति सति त्वत्पतिरसौ ॥ २६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viriñciḥ pañcatvaṃ vrajati harirāpnoti viratiṃ
vināśaṃ kīnāśo bhajati dhanado yāti nidhanam .
vitandrī māhendrī vitatirapi saṃmīlitadṛśā
mahāsaṃhāre'smin viharati sati tvatpatirasau .. 26..
contentBlocks.translation: Viriñcīi (Brahmā) is reduced into elements: Hari ( Viṣṇu) retires into passivity: Kīnāśa(Yama the god of death) himself dies; Kubera the god of wealth meets with his end; and Indra with all his followers closes his eyes in destruction. When such, O Sati (chaste Consort of Śiva), is the state of all beings at the time of the total dissolution (mahāsamhāra ) of the universe, Thy husband Sadāśiva alone is sporting. 26.
contentBlocks.meanings: विरिञ्चिः (viriñciḥ) – Brahmā; पञ्चत्वं (pañcatvaṃ) – reduced to elements (pañca:five | tvaṃ:state); व्रजति (vrajati) – goes; हरिः (hariḥ) – Viṣṇu; आप्नोति (āpnoti) – attains; विरतिं (viratiṃ) – passivity; विनाशं (vināśaṃ) – destruction; कीनाशः (kīnāśaḥ) – Yama; भजति (bhajati) – experiences; धनदः (dhanadaḥ) – Kubera; याति (yāti) – goes; निधनम् (nidhanam) – end; वितन्द्री (vitandrī) – Indra's; माहेन्द्री (māhendrī) – of Indra; विततिः (vitatiḥ) – followers; अपि (api) – also; संमीलितदृशा (saṃmīlitadṛśā) – with closed eyes (saṃmīlita:closed | dṛśā:eyes); महासंहारे (mahāsaṃhāre) – in the great dissolution (mahā:great | saṃhāre:in dissolution); अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; विहरति (viharati) – sports; सति (sati) – O Sati; त्वत्पतिः (tvatpatiḥ) – your husband (tvat:your | patiḥ:husband); असौ (asau) – he;

contentBlocks.original:
जपो जल्पः शिल्पं सकलमपि मुद्राविरचना
गतिः प्रादक्षिण्यक्रमणमशनाद्याहुतिविधिः ।
प्रणामस्संवेशस्सुखमखिलमात्मार्पणदृशा
सपर्यापर्यायस्तव भवतु यन्मे विलसितम् ॥ २७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
japo jalpaḥ śilpam sakalam api mudrā-viracanā
gatiḥ prādakṣiṇya-kramaṇam aśanādy’āhuti-vidhiḥ .
praṇāmaḥ samveśaḥ sukham akilam ātmārpaṇa-dṛśā
saparyā-paryāyas tava bhavatu yan me vilasitam ..27..
contentBlocks.translation: May everything that I do with the sense of self-dedication (Ātmārpaṇa dṛśā) be items in Thy service—my prattle, the utterance of Thy Mantra; the movements of my hand, the gestures and poses of Thy worship; my walking, Thy circumambulation; my eating, fire-sacrifice to Thee; the stretching of the body in sleep and rest, prostration to Thee; and all my enjoyments, offerings made to Thee. 27.
contentBlocks.meanings: जपः (japaḥ) – chanting; जल्पः (jalpaḥ) – prattle; शिल्पं (śilpam) – art; सकलम् (sakalam) – all; अपि (api) – also; मुद्राविरचना (mudrā-viracanā) – gesture creation (mudrā:gesture | viracanā:creation); गतिः (gatiḥ) – movement; प्रादक्षिण्यक्रमणम् (prādakṣiṇya-kramaṇam) – circumambulation (prādakṣiṇya:circumambulation | kramaṇam:movement); अशनाद्याहुतिविधिः (aśanādi-āhuti-vidhiḥ) – eating as offering (aśana:eating | ādi:etc. | āhuti:offering | vidhiḥ:method); प्रणामः (praṇāmaḥ) – prostration; संवेशः (samveśaḥ) – rest; सुखम् (sukham) – comfort; अखिलम् (akhilam) – all; आत्मार्पणदृशा (ātmārpaṇa-dṛśā) – with self-dedication (ātma:self | arpaṇa:dedication | dṛśā:with view); सपर्यापर्यायः (saparyā-paryāyaḥ) – service (saparyā:worship | paryāyaḥ:sequence); तव (tava) – your; भवतु (bhavatu) – may it be; यत् (yat) – that which; मे (me) – my; विलसितम् (vilasitam) – activity;

contentBlocks.original:
सुधामप्यास्वाद्य प्रतिभयजरामृत्युहरिणीं
विपद्यन्ते विश्वे विधिशतमखाद्या दिविषदः ।
करालं यत्क्ष्वेलं कबलितवतः कालकलना
न शम्भोस्तन्मूलं तव जननि ताटङ्कमहिमा ॥ २८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sudhāmapyāsvādya pratibhayajarāmṛtyuhariṇīṃ
vipadyante viśve vidhiśatamakhādyā diviṣadaḥ .
karālaṃ yatkṣvelaṃ kabalitavataḥ kālakalanā
na śambhostanmūlaṃ tava janani tāṭaṅkamahimā .. 28..
contentBlocks.translation: Even after consuming Amrta (nectar), which confers freedom from the fear-inspiring decrepitude of old age and of death, Deities like Brahmā and Indra perish finally at the time of cosmic dissolution. But even in spite of taking the terrible poison of Kālakūṭa, Thy Consort Śiva enjoys a life-span that has no end. The cause of this, O Mother, is indeed the unique glory of Thy ear ornament! 28.
contentBlocks.meanings: सुधाम् (sudhām) – nectar; अपि (api) – even; आस्वाद्य (āsvādya) – having consumed; प्रतिभय (pratibhaya) – fear-inspiring; जरामृत्युहरिणीम् (jarāmṛtyuhariṇīm) – removing old age and death (jarā:old age | mṛtyu:death | hariṇīm:removing); विपद्यन्ते (vipadyante) – perish; विश्वे (viśve) – in the universe; विधिशतमखाद्याः (vidhiśatamakhādyāḥ) – Brahmā, Indra, and others (vidhi:Brahmā | śata:hundred | makha:Indra | ādyāḥ:and others); दिविषदः (diviṣadaḥ) – deities; करालम् (karālam) – terrible; यत् (yat) – which; क्ष्वेलम् (kṣvelam) – poison; कबलितवतः (kabalitavataḥ) – having consumed; कालकलना (kālakalanā) – end of time (kāla:time | kalanā:end); न (na) – not; शम्भोः (śambhoḥ) – of Śiva; तन्मूलम् (tanmūlam) – root cause; तव (tava) – your; जननि (janani) – O Mother; ताटङ्कमहिमा (tāṭaṅkamahimā) – glory of ear ornament (tāṭaṅka:ear ornament | mahimā:glory);

contentBlocks.original:
किरीटं वैरिञ्चं परिहर पुरः कैटभभिदः
कठोरे कोटीरे स्खलसि जहि जम्भारिमुकुटम् ।
प्रणम्रेष्वेतेषु प्रसभमुपयातस्य भवनं
भवस्याभ्युत्थाने तव परिजनोक्तिर्विजयते ॥ २९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kirīṭaṃ vairiñcaṃ parihara puraḥ kaiṭabhabhidaḥ
kaṭhore koṭīre skhalasi jahi jambhārimukuṭam .
praṇamreṣveteṣu prasabhamupayātasya bhavanaṃ
bhavasyābhyutthāne tava parijanoktirvijayate .. 29..
contentBlocks.translation: On Thy Consort Bhava approaching Thy abode unannounced, Thou springest up in such great haste to receive Him, whereupon Thy attendants (in their concern for thy safety from any possible injury) caution Thee, crying out, “Deign to keep away from the diadem of Viriñci (Brahmā); avoid tumbling over the heavy crown of Kaiṭabhārī (Viṣṇu); beware of the crest of Jambhāri (Indra)." 29.
contentBlocks.meanings: किरीटं (kirīṭam) – crown; वैरिञ्चं (vairiñcam) – of Viriñci (Brahmā); परिहर (parihara) – avoid; पुरः (puraḥ) – in front; कैटभभिदः (kaiṭabhabhidaḥ) – of Kaiṭabhārī (Viṣṇu) (kaiṭabha:Kaiṭabha | bhidaḥ:breaker); कठोरे (kaṭhore) – hard; कोटीरे (koṭīre) – crest; स्खलसि (skhalasi) – you stumble; जहि (jahi) – avoid; जम्भारिमुकुटम् (jambhārimukuṭam) – crown of Jambhāri (Indra) (jambhāri:Jambhāri (Indra) | mukuṭam:crown); प्रणम्रेषु (praṇamreṣu) – in the bowed; एतेषु (eteṣu) – these; प्रसभम् (prasabham) – forcefully; उपयातस्य (upayātasya) – approaching; भवनं (bhavanam) – abode; भवस्य (bhavasya) – of Bhava (Śiva); अभ्युत्थाने (abhyutthāne) – rising; तव (tava) – your; परिजनोक्तिः (parijanoktiḥ) – attendants' words (parijana:attendants | uktiḥ:words); विजयते (vijayate) – triumphs;

contentBlocks.original:
स्वदेहोद्भूताभिर्घृणिभिरणिमाद्याभिरभितो
निषेव्ये नित्ये त्वामहमिति सदा भावयति यः ।
किमाश्चर्यं तस्य त्रिनयनसमृद्धिं तृणयतो
महासंवर्ताग्निर्विरचयति निराजनविधिम् ॥ ३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svadehodbhūtābhirghṛṇibhiraṇimādyābhirabhito
niṣevye nitye tvāmahamiti sadā bhāvayati yaḥ .
kimāścaryaṃ tasya trinayanasamṛddhiṃ tṛṇayato
mahāsaṃvartāgnirviracayati nirājanavidhim .. 30..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother eternal and adored of all! By ever meditating with a feeling of identification with Thee, who art surrounded by divine powers like Animā which are only rays emanating from Thy feet, a devotee attains to glories far above even those of Śiva. What wonder then that even the conflagration of Cosmic Dissolution proves only to be the rite of Nīrājana to him. 30.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्वदेहोद्भूताभिः (svadehodbhūtābhiḥ) – by powers born of own body (sva:own | deha:body | udbhūtābhiḥ:by powers born); घृणिभिः (ghṛṇibhiḥ) – by rays; अणिमाद्याभिः (aṇimādyābhiḥ) – by Animā and others (aṇimā:Animā | ādyābhiḥ:and others); अभितः (abhitaḥ) – surrounded by; निषेव्ये (niṣevye) – to be served; नित्ये (nitye) – eternal; त्वाम् (tvām) – Thee; अहम् (aham) – I; इति (iti) – thus; सदा (sadā) – always; भावयति (bhāvayati) – meditates; यः (yaḥ) – who; किम् (kim) – what; आश्चर्यं (āścaryaṃ) – wonder; तस्य (tasya) – his; त्रिनयनसमृद्धिं (trinayanasamṛddhiṃ) – glories above Śiva (tri:three | nayana:eyes | samṛddhiṃ:prosperity); तृणयतः (tṛṇayataḥ) – considering as straw; महासंवर्ताग्निः (mahāsaṃvartāgniḥ) – cosmic dissolution fire (mahā:great | saṃvarta:dissolution | agniḥ:fire); विरचयति (viracayati) – performs; निराजनविधिम् (nirājanavidhiṃ) – rite of Nīrājana (nirājana:Nīrājana | vidhiṃ:rite);

contentBlocks.original:
चतुष्षष्ट्या तन्त्रैः सकलमतिसंधाय भुवनं
स्थितस्तत्तत्सिद्धिप्रसवपरतन्त्रैः पशुपतिः ।
पुनस्त्वन्निर्बन्धादखिलपुरुषार्थैकघटना-
स्वतन्त्रं ते तन्त्रं क्षितितलमवातीतरदिदम् ॥ ३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
catuṣṣaṣṭyā tantraiḥ sakalamatisaṃdhāya bhuvanaṃ
sthitastattatsiddhiprasavaparatantraiḥ paśupatiḥ .
punastvannirbandhādakhilapuruṣārthaikaghaṭanā-
svatantraṃ te tantraṃ kṣititalamavātītaradidam .. 31..
contentBlocks.translation: Paśupati (Śiva) at first remained satisfied after ‘deluding’ (atisandhāya) the world, by giving out the sixtyfour Tantras, which expound practices conferring only one or another of the various psychic powers and worldly fulfilments. Afterwards, on Thy special insistence, He revealed this Thy own Tantra to the world, independent of all the others and capable of conferring all the Purusārthas—Dharma, Artha, Kama and Mokṣa—on the votaries, by Itself. 31.
contentBlocks.meanings: चतुष्षष्ट्या (catuṣṣaṣṭyā) – sixty-four; तन्त्रैः (tantraiḥ) – by the Tantras; सकलम् (sakamalam) – entire; अतिसंधाय (atisandhāya) – deluding; भुवनम् (bhuvanam) – world; स्थितः (sthitaḥ) – remained; तत्तत् (tattat) – various; सिद्धि (siddhi) – psychic powers; प्रसव (prasava) – conferring; परतन्त्रैः (paratantraiḥ) – dependent Tantras; पशुपतिः (paśupatiḥ) – Lord Śiva; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; त्वत् (tvat) – your; निर्बन्धात् (nirbandhāt) – insistence; अखिलपुरुषार्थैकघटना (akhilapuruṣārthaikaghaṭanā) – conferring all Purusārthas (akhila:all | puruṣārtha:Purusārthas | eka:one | ghaṭanā:conferring); स्वतन्त्रम् (svatantram) – independent; ते (te) – your; तन्त्रम् (tantram) – Tantra; क्षितितलम् (kṣititalam) – earth; अवातीत (avātīta) – revealed; इदम् (idam) – this;

contentBlocks.original:
शिवः शक्तिः कामः क्षितिरथ रविः शीतकिरणः
स्मरो हंसः शक्रस्तदनु च परामारहरयः ।
अमी हृल्लेखाभिस्तिसृभिरवसानेषु घटिता
भजन्ते वर्णास्ते तव जननि नामावयवताम् ॥ ३२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivaḥ śaktiḥ kāmaḥ kṣitiratha raviḥ śītakiraṇaḥ
smaro haṃsaḥ śakrastadanu ca parāmāraharayaḥ .
amī hṛllekhābhistisṛbhiravasāneṣu ghaṭitā
bhajante varṇāste tava janani nāmāvayavatām .. 32..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! The parts that combine to form Thy name (Mantra) are three groups of syllables—first, the group Ka, e, i and la indicated by the words Śiva, Śakti, Kāma and Kṣitī; second, the group ha, sa, ka, ha and la denoted by Ravi, Śītakiraṇa, Smara, Hamsa and Śakra; and third, the group Sa, Ka, and la denoted by Parā, Māra and Hari, together with Hṛllekhā (syllable Hrīm) added at the end of each of the three groups of syllables. 32.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिवः (śivaḥ) – Shiva; शक्तिः (śaktiḥ) – Shakti; कामः (kāmaḥ) – Kama; क्षितिः (kṣitiḥ) – Earth; अथ (atha) – then; रविः (raviḥ) – Sun; शीतकिरणः (śītakiraṇaḥ) – Moon (śīta:cool | kiraṇaḥ:rays); स्मरः (smaraḥ) – Cupid; हंसः (haṃsaḥ) – Swan; शक्रः (śakraḥ) – Indra; तदनु (tadanu) – thereafter; च (ca) – and; परामारहरयः (parāmāraharayaḥ) – Parā, Māra, Hari (parā:Parā | māra:Māra | hari:Hari); अमी (amī) – these; हृल्लेखाभिः (hṛllekhābhiḥ) – with Hṛllekhā (hṛllekhā:Hṛllekhā | bhiḥ:with); तिसृभिः (tisṛbhiḥ) – three; अवसानेषु (avasāneṣu) – at the ends; घटिता (ghaṭitā) – joined; भजन्ते (bhajante) – worship; वर्णाः (varṇāḥ) – syllables; ते (te) – those; तव (tava) – your; जननि (janani) – Mother; नामावयवताम् (nāmāvayavatām) – parts of the name (nāma:name | avayavatām:parts);

contentBlocks.original:
स्मरं योनिं लक्ष्मीं त्रितयमिदमादौ तव मनो-
र्निधायैके नित्ये निरवधिमहाभोगरसिकाः ।
भजन्ति त्वां चिन्तामणिगुननिबद्धाक्षवलयाः
शिवाग्नौ जुह्वन्तः सुरभिघृतधाराहुतिशतैः ॥ ३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
smaraṃ yoniṃ lakṣmīṃ tritayamidamādau tava mano-
rnidhāyaike nitye niravadhimahābhogarasikāḥ .
bhajanti tvāṃ cintāmaṇigunanibaddhākṣavalayāḥ
śivāgnau juhvantaḥ surabhighṛtadhārāhutiśataiḥ .. 33..
contentBlocks.translation: Some connoisseurs of the highest Enjoyment (Mahābhogarasikāḥ), adding before the beginning of Thy Mantra (verse 32) the Bīja-syllables of Kāmarāja (Klīm), Bhuvaneśvarī (Hrīm), and Śri (Śrīm) and adorned with a necklace of Cintāmaṇi, worship Thee with oblations consisting of countless streams of Kāmadhenu’s ghee in the purified fire of Śivā (i. e. Śakti established as the Trikona in the Anahata-cakra). 33.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्मरं (smaraṃ) – Kāmarāja (Klīm); योनिं (yoniṃ) – Bhuvaneśvarī (Hrīm); लक्ष्मीं (lakṣmīṃ) – Śri (Śrīm); त्रितयम् (tritayam) – trio; इदम् (idam) – this; आदौ (ādau) – at the beginning; तव (tava) – of Thy; मनः (manaḥ) – mind; निधाय (nidhāya) – placing; एके (eke) – some; नित्ये (nitye) – eternal; निरवधिमहाभोगरसिकाः (niravadhimahābhogarasikāḥ) – connoisseurs of highest enjoyment (niravadhi:endless | mahābhoga:great enjoyment | rasikāḥ:connoisseurs); भजन्ति (bhajanti) – worship; त्वाम् (tvām) – Thee; चिन्तामणिगुननिबद्धाक्षवलयाः (cintāmaṇigunanibaddhākṣavalayāḥ) – adorned with Cintāmaṇi necklace (cintāmaṇi:Cintāmaṇi | guna:qualities | nibaddha:bound | akṣa:beads | valayāḥ:necklace); शिवाग्नौ (śivāgnau) – in Śivā's fire (śivā:Śivā | agnau:in the fire); जुह्वन्तः (juhvantaḥ) – offering; सुरभिघृतधाराहुतिशतैः (surabhighṛtadhārāhutiśataiḥ) – with streams of ghee oblations (surabhi:fragrant | ghṛta:ghee | dhārā:streams | huti:oblations | śataiḥ:hundreds);

contentBlocks.original:
शरीरं त्वं शम्भोः शशिमिहिरवक्षोरुहयुगं
तवात्मानं मन्ये भगवति नवात्मानमनघम् ।
अतश्शेषश्शेषीत्ययमुभयसाधारणतया
स्थितः संबन्धो वां समरसपरानन्दपरयोः ॥ ३४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śarīraṃ tvaṃ śambhoḥ śaśimihiravakṣoruhayugaṃ
tavātmānaṃ manye bhagavati navātmānamanagham .
ataśśeṣaśśeṣītyayamubhayasādhāraṇatayā
sthitaḥ saṃbandho vāṃ samarasaparānandaparayoḥ .. 34..
contentBlocks.translation:
O Bhagavati! Thou art verily the body of Śambhu with the sun and the moon as the two breasts. And Thy being is verily the flawless Śambhu, having nine aspects (navātman). Therefore in the matter of relationship of Seṣa (the accessory or the subservient factor) and Śeṣī (the principal or essential entity), both (of ye) Parānanda (the Ānanda-bhairava) and Parā (the Ānanda-bhairavī) stand on an equal footing.
Note: In order to show the difference between the conceptions of the Devī and Śiva entertained by the Samayins and the Kaulas the position of the Kaulas is now stated in the verses 34 and 35. Afterwards in the six verses beginning with the 36th verse ‘Tavājñā cakrastham’, the conception of the Samayins is elaborated. There are two schools of Kaulas known as Pūrva Kaulas and Uttara Kaulas. First, the conception of Pūrva Kaulas is given. 34.
contentBlocks.meanings: शरीरं (śarīraṃ) – body; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; शम्भोः (śambhoḥ) – of Śambhu; शशिमिहिरवक्षोरुहयुगं (śaśimihiravakṣoruhayugaṃ) – with sun and moon breasts (śaśi:moon | mihira:sun | vakṣoruhayugaṃ:pair of breasts); तव (tava) – your; आत्मानं (ātmānaṃ) – self; मन्ये (manye) – I consider; भगवति (bhagavati) – O Goddess; नवात्मानमनघम् (navātmānamanagham) – flawless ninefold self (nava:nine | ātmānam:self | anagham:flawless); अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; शेषः (śeṣaḥ) – accessory; शेषी (śeṣī) – principal; इति (iti) – thus; अयम् (ayam) – this; उभयसाधारणतया (ubhayasādhāraṇatayā) – equally common to both (ubhaya:both | sādhāraṇatayā:commonality); स्थितः (sthitaḥ) – established; संबन्धः (saṃbandhaḥ) – relationship; वां (vāṃ) – of you both; समरसपरानन्दपरयोः (samarasaparānandaparayoḥ) – of equal supreme bliss (samara:equal | parānanda:supreme bliss | parayoḥ:of both);

contentBlocks.original:
मनस्त्वं व्योम त्वं मरुदसि मरुत्सारथिरसि
त्वमापस्त्वं भूमिस्त्वयि परिणतायां न हि परम् ।
त्वमेव स्वात्मानं परिणमयितुं विश्ववपुषा
चिदानन्दाकारं शिवयुवति भावेन बिभृषे ॥ ३५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
manastvaṃ vyoma tvaṃ marudasi marutsārathirasi
tvamāpastvaṃ bhūmistvayi pariṇatāyāṃ na hi param .
tvameva svātmānaṃ pariṇamayituṃ viśvavapuṣā
cidānandākāraṃ śivayuvati bhāvena bibhṛṣe .. 35..
contentBlocks.translation: Thou art the Mind, Thou art Ākāśa; Thou art also Fire. Thou art Water and Earth too. When Thou hast transformed Thyself in this way into the form of the universe, there is nothing beyond not included in Thee. It is to transform Thyself into the universe that Thou assumest this form of Consciousness-Bliss as Siva’s Consort (His Śakti). 35.
contentBlocks.meanings: मनः (manaḥ) – mind; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; व्योम (vyoma) – sky; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; मरुत् (marut) – wind; असि (asi) – are; मरुत्सारथि (marutsārathi) – charioteer of the winds (marut:wind | sārathi:charioteer); रसि (rasi) – are; त्वम् (tvam) – you; आपः (āpaḥ) – water; त्वम् (tvam) – you; भूमिः (bhūmiḥ) – earth; त्वयि (tvayi) – in you; परिणतायाम् (pariṇatāyām) – transformed; न (na) – not; हि (hi) – indeed; परम् (param) – beyond; त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – only; स्वात्मानम् (svātmānam) – own self; परिणमयितुम् (pariṇamayitum) – to transform; विश्ववपुषा (viśvavapuṣā) – universal form (viśva:universe | vapuṣā:form); चित् (cit) – consciousness; आनन्द (ānanda) – bliss; आकारम् (ākāram) – form; शिवयुवति (śivayuvati) – Siva's consort (śiva:Siva | yuvati:consort); भावेन (bhāvena) – as; बिभृषे (bibhṛṣe) – assume;

contentBlocks.original:
तवाज्ञाचक्रस्थं तपनशशिकोटिद्युतिधरं
परं शम्भुं वन्दे परिमिलितपार्श्वं परचिता ।
यमाराध्यन् भक्त्या रविशशिशुचीनामविषये
निरालोकेऽलोके निवसति हि भालोकभवने ॥ ३६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tavājñācakrasthaṃ tapanaśaśikoṭidyutidharaṃ
paraṃ śambhuṃ vande parimilitapārśvaṃ paracitā .
yamārādhyan bhaktyā raviśaśiśucīnāmaviṣaye
nirāloke'loke nivasati hi bhālokabhavane .. 36..
contentBlocks.translation: I salute the Supreme Śambhu residing in Thy Ājñā-Cakra, who is resplendent as crores of suns and moons put together, and whose (left) side is integrated with the Supreme Consciousness embodied as the Devi. He who adores Him with deep devotion attains that Self-Conscious and Self-luminous State which is not a Loka (Region or Plane), which transcends the pale of the light of Moon, Sun and Fire, and which is beyond the ken of all. 36.
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; आज्ञाचक्रस्थं (ājñācakrasthaṃ) – residing in Ājñā-Cakra (ājñā:command | cakra:wheel | sthaṃ:residing); तपनशशिकोटिद्युतिधरं (tapanaśaśikoṭidyutidharaṃ) – resplendent as crores of suns and moons (tapana:sun | śaśi:moon | koṭi:crores | dyuti:splendor | dharaṃ:bearing); परं (paraṃ) – supreme; शम्भुं (śambhuṃ) – Śambhu; वन्दे (vande) – I salute; परिमिलितपार्श्वं (parimilitapārśvaṃ) – integrated side (parimilita:integrated | pārśvaṃ:side); परचिता (paracitā) – Supreme Consciousness; यम् (yam) – whom; आराध्यन् (ārādhyan) – adoring; भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; रविशशिशुचीनामविषये (raviśaśiśucīnāmaviṣaye) – beyond sun, moon, fire (ravi:sun | śaśi:moon | śucīnām:of fires | aviṣaye:beyond the realm); निरालोकेऽलोके (nirāloke'loke) – in the non-luminous realm (nirāloke:in the non-luminous | aloke:realm); निवसति (nivasati) – resides; हि (hi) – indeed; भालोकभवने (bhālokabhavane) – in the luminous abode (bhāloka:luminous world | bhavane:in the abode);

contentBlocks.original:
विशुद्धौ ते शुद्धस्फटिकविशदं व्योमजनकं
शिवं सेवे देवीमपि शिवसमानव्यवसिताम् ।
ययोः कान्त्या यान्त्याः शशिकिरणसारूप्यसरणे-
विधूतान्तर्ध्वान्ता विलसति चकोरीव जगती ॥ ३७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśuddhau te śuddhasphaṭikaviśadaṃ vyomajanakaṃ
śivaṃ seve devīmapi śivasamānavyavasitām .
yayoḥ kāntyā yāntyāḥ śaśikiraṇasārūpyasaraṇe-
vidhūtāntardhvāntā vilasati cakorīva jagatī .. 37..
contentBlocks.translation: In Thy Viśuddhi cakra I meditate on Siva, the creator of Ākāśa (Vyoman), resembling a pure crystal in purity, along with the Devī who is equal to Śiva in all respects. In the Lunar Brilliance proceeding from them both, the whole universe, free from the Darkness of Ignorance, rejoices like the bird Cakorī (female partridge). 37.
contentBlocks.meanings: विशुद्धौ (viśuddhau) – in the Viśuddhi; ते (te) – your; शुद्धस्फटिकविशदं (śuddhasphaṭikaviśadam) – pure crystal-like (śuddha:pure | sphaṭika:crystal | viśadam:like); व्योमजनकं (vyomajanakam) – creator of Ākāśa (vyoma:Ākāśa | janakam:creator); शिवं (śivam) – Śiva; सेवे (seve) – I meditate; देवीम् (devīm) – on the Devī; अपि (api) – also; शिवसमानव्यवसिताम् (śivasamānavyavasitām) – equal to Śiva (śiva:Śiva | samāna:equal | vyavasitām:determined); ययोः (yayoḥ) – from whom; कान्त्या (kāntyā) – by brilliance; यान्त्याः (yāntyāḥ) – proceeding; शशिकिरणसारूप्यसरणे (śaśikiraṇasārūpyasaraṇe) – in lunar brilliance (śaśi:moon | kiraṇa:rays | sārūpya:resemblance | saraṇe:path); विधूतान्तर्ध्वान्ता (vidhūtāntardhvāntā) – free from darkness (vidhūta:dispelled | antar:inner | dhvāntā:darkness); विलसति (vilasati) – rejoices; चकोरीव (cakorīva) – like the Cakorī (cakorī:Cakorī | iva:like); जगती (jagatī) – the universe;

contentBlocks.original:
समुन्मीलत् संवित् कमलमकरन्दैकरसिकं
भजे हंसद्वन्द्वं किमपि महतां मानसचरम् ।
यदालापादष्टादशगुणितविद्यापरिणति-
र्यदादत्ते दोषाद् गुणमखिलमद्भ्यः पय इव ॥ ३८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samunmīlat saṃvit kamalamakarandaikarasikaṃ
bhaje haṃsadvandvaṃ kimapi mahatāṃ mānasacaram .
yadālāpādaṣṭādaśaguṇitavidyāpariṇati-
ryadādatte doṣād guṇamakhilamadbhyaḥ paya iva .. 38..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! I adore the pair of Swans (Śiva Śakti) who take delight in imbibing the honey of the full-blown Lotus of Knowledge of the Anāhata-cakra, and who swim in the Mānasa lake of the mind of enlightened ones. Their mutual conversation is what has become the eighteen Vidyas, and they separate good from evil, as milk from the water with which it is diluted (as swans do). 38.
contentBlocks.meanings: समुन्मीलत् (samunmīlat) – blossoming; संवित् (saṃvit) – consciousness; कमलमकरन्दैकरसिकं (kamalamakarandaikarasikaṃ) – delighting in lotus nectar (kamala:lotus | makaranda:nectar | eka:one | rasikaṃ:delighting); भजे (bhaje) – I adore; हंसद्वन्द्वं (haṃsadvandvaṃ) – pair of swans (haṃsa:swan | dvandvaṃ:pair); किमपि (kimapi) – something; महतां (mahatāṃ) – of the great; मानसचरम् (mānasacaram) – moving in the mind (mānasa:mind | caram:moving); यदालापात् (yadālāpāt) – from whose conversation; अष्टादशगुणितविद्यापरिणति (aṣṭādaśaguṇitavidyāpariṇati) – eighteen-fold knowledge transformation (aṣṭādaśa:eighteen | guṇita:multiplied | vidyā:knowledge | pariṇati:transformation); यदादत्ते (yadādatte) – who takes; दोषात् (doṣāt) – from fault; गुणम् (guṇam) – virtue; अखिलम् (akhilam) – entire; अद्भ्यः (adbhyaḥ) – from water; पय (paya) – milk; इव (iva) – like;

contentBlocks.original:
तव स्वाधिष्ठाने हुतवहमधिष्ठाय निरतं
तमीडे संवर्तं जननि महतीं तां च समयाम् ।
यदालोके लोकान् दहति महति क्रोधकलिते
दयार्द्रा या दृष्टिः शिशिरमुपचारं रचयति ॥ ३९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava svādhiṣṭhāne hutavahamadhiṣṭhāya nirataṃ
tamīḍe saṃvartaṃ janani mahatīṃ tāṃ ca samayām .
yadāloke lokān dahati mahati krodhakalite
dayārdrā yā dṛṣṭiḥ śiśiramupacāraṃ racayati .. 39..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! Invoking Samvarta (Kālāgni-Rudra), the lord of the Fire of Dissolution in the Fire Element of Thy Svadhisthana-cakra, I adore Him along with Thee, Samaya, the great Potency (Maha-Sakti) of Rudra. When the angry looks of Rudra burn up the universe, it is Thy merciful look that bestows on them the reviving blessing of coolness. 39.
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; स्वाधिष्ठाने (svādhiṣṭhāne) – in the Svadhisthana; हुतवहमधिष्ठाय (hutavahamadhiṣṭhāya) – invoking the Fire (hutavaha:Fire | adhiṣṭhāya:invoking); निरतं (nirataṃ) – engaged; तमीडे (tamīḍe) – I adore Him; संवर्तं (saṃvartaṃ) – Samvarta; जननि (janani) – O Mother; महतीं (mahatīṃ) – great; तां (tāṃ) – that; च (ca) – and; समयाम् (samayām) – Samaya; यदालोके (yadāloke) – when looks (yadā:when | āloke:looks); लोकान् (lokān) – worlds; दहति (dahati) – burns; महति (mahati) – great; क्रोधकलिते (krodhakalite) – filled with anger (krodha:anger | kalite:filled with); दयार्द्रा (dayārdrā) – compassionate; या (yā) – which; दृष्टिः (dṛṣṭiḥ) – look; शिशिरमुपचारं (śiśiramupacāraṃ) – cooling blessing (śiśira:cooling | upacāraṃ:blessing); रचयति (racayati) – bestows;

contentBlocks.original:
तटित्त्वन्तं शक्त्या तिमिरपरिपन्थिफुरणया
स्फुरन्नानारत्नाभरणपरिणद्धेन्द्रधनुषम् ।
तव श्यामं मेघं कमपि मणिपूरैकशरणं
निषेवे वर्षन्तं हरमिहिरतप्तं त्रिभुवनम् ॥ ४०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
taṭittvantaṃ śaktyā timiraparipanthiphuraṇayā
sphuran-nānā-ratn’ābharaṇa-pariṇaddhendra dhanuṣam .
tava śyāmaṃ meghaṃ kamapi maṇipūraikaśaraṇaṃ
niṣeve varṣantaṃ haramihirataptaṃ tribhuvanam .. 40..
contentBlocks.translation: I worship that unique dark-blue Rain Cloud which abides ever in the Manipura sending showers on the universe that has been burnt by the sun of Rudra—the Rain-cloud that is illumined by the brilliant lightning in the form of Śakti, and revealing the rain-bow made by the reflection of the numerous sparkling gems (set in the Kuṇḍalinī), and dispelling the darkness reigning in the Maṇipūra. 40.
contentBlocks.meanings: तटित्त्वन्तं (taṭittvantaṃ) – illumined by lightning (taṭit:lightning | tvantaṃ:illumined by); शक्त्या (śaktyā) – by Śakti; तिमिरपरिपन्थिफुरणया (timiraparipanthiphuraṇayā) – dispelling darkness (timira:darkness | paripanthin:obstacle | phuraṇayā:dispelling); स्फुरन्नानारत्नाभरणपरिणद्धेन्द्रधनुषम् (sphuran-nānā-ratn’ābharaṇa-pariṇaddhendra-dhanuṣam) – rainbow with sparkling gems (sphuran:sparkling | nānā:various | ratna:gems | ābharaṇa:ornaments | pariṇaddha:adorned | indra:Indra | dhanuṣam:rainbow); तव (tava) – your; श्यामं (śyāmaṃ) – dark-blue; मेघं (meghaṃ) – cloud; कमपि (kamapi) – unique; मणिपूरैकशरणं (maṇipūraikaśaraṇaṃ) – abides in Maṇipūra (maṇipūra:Maṇipūra | eka:one | śaraṇaṃ:refuge); निषेवे (niṣeve) – I worship; वर्षन्तं (varṣantaṃ) – showering; हरमिहिरतप्तं (haramihirataptaṃ) – burnt by Rudra's sun (hara:Rudra | mihira:sun | taptaṃ:burnt); त्रिभुवनम् (tribhuvanam) – three worlds;

contentBlocks.original:
तवाधारे मूले सह समयया लास्यपरया
नवात्मानं मन्ये नवरसमहाताण्डवनटम् ।
उभाभ्यामेताभ्यामुदयविधिमुद्दिश्य दयया
सनाथाभ्यां जज्ञे जनकजननीमज्जगदिदम् ॥ ४१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tavādhāre mūle saha samayayā lāsyaparayā
navātmānaṃ manye navarasamahātāṇḍavanaṭam .
ubhābhyām etābhyām udaya-vidhim uddiśya dayayā
sanāthābhyāṃ jajñe janakajananīmajjagadidam .. 41..
contentBlocks.translation: In Thy Mūlādhāra-cakra, I meditate on the Navāt-man (Mahā bhairava), who, expressing nine sentiments, is engaged in the? Mahā-tānḍava dance in the company of Samayā (Mahā bhairavī) who is dancing the Lāsya type of dance. The universe has come to have a father and a mother in ye both, who have come together graciously for its regeneration (after its being burnt up). 41.
contentBlocks.meanings: तवाधारे (tavādhāre) – in your base (tava:your | ādhāre:in the base); मूले (mūle) – at the root; सह (saha) – with; समयया (samayayā) – with Samayā; लास्यपरया (lāsyaparayā) – engaged in Lāsya dance (lāsya:Lāsya dance | parayā:engaged in); नवात्मानं (navātmānam) – Navātman; मnye (manye) – I meditate; नवरसमहाताण्डवनटम् (navarasamahātāṇḍavanaṭam) – dancing Mahātāṇḍava with nine sentiments (nava:nine | rasa:sentiments | mahā:great | tāṇḍava:Tāṇḍava dance | naṭam:dancing); उभाभ्याम् (ubhābhyām) – by both; एताभ्याम् (etābhyām) – by these; उदयविधिम् (udayavidhim) – method of creation (udaya:creation | vidhim:method); उद्दिश्य (uddiśya) – aiming at; दयया (dayayā) – with compassion; सनाथाभ्याम् (sanāthābhyām) – protected by both (sa:with | nātha:protector | bhyām:by both); जज्ञे (jajñe) – was born; जनकजननीम् (janakajananīm) – father and mother (janaka:father | jananīm:mother); अज्जगदिदम् (ajagadi) – this universe;

contentBlocks.original:
सौन्दर्यलहरी
गतैर्माणिक्यत्वं गगनमणिभिः सान्द्रघटितं
किरीटं ते हैमं हिमगिरिसुते कीर्तयति यः ।
स नीडेयच्छायाच्छुरणशबलं चन्द्रशकलं
धनुः शौनासीरं किमिति न निबध्नाति धिषणाम् ॥ ४२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
saundaryalaharī
gatairmāṇikyatvaṃ gaganamaṇibhiḥ sāndraghaṭitaṃ
kirīṭaṃ te haimaṃ himagirisute kīrtayati yaḥ .
sa nīḍeyacchāyācchuraṇaśabalaṃ candraśakalaṃ
dhanuḥ śaunāsīraṃ kimiti na nibadhnāti dhiṣaṇām .. 42..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Snow-capped Mountain! A poet who describes the golden crown on Thy head, set densely with precious gems (māṇikya) constituted of the twelve suns—will he not have the impression that the crescent moon on Thy head is Indra’s bow (rainbow), on seeing the variegated hues it reflects from the diffused light of the surrounding luminous bodies falling on it. 42.
contentBlocks.meanings: सौन्दर्यलहरी (saundaryalaharī) – wave of beauty (saundarya:beauty | laharī:wave); गतैः (gatair) – gone; माणिक्यत्वं (māṇikyatvaṃ) – state of ruby (māṇikya:ruby | tvaṃ:state of); गगनमणिभिः (gaganamaṇibhiḥ) – by sky-jewels (gagana:sky | maṇibhiḥ:by jewels); सान्द्रघटितं (sāndraghaṭitaṃ) – densely constituted (sāndra:dense | ghaṭitaṃ:constituted); किरीटं (kirīṭaṃ) – crown; ते (te) – your; हैमं (haimaṃ) – golden; हिमगिरिसुते (himagirisute) – daughter of the snow mountain (himagiri:snow mountain | sute:daughter); कीर्तयति (kīrtayati) – describes; यः (yaḥ) – who; स (sa) – he; नीडेयच्छायाच्छुरणशबलं (nīḍeyacchāyācchuraṇaśabalaṃ) – variegated by rainbow hues (nīḍeya:rainbow | chāyā:hues | churaṇa:diffused | śabalaṃ:variegated); चन्द्रशकलं (candraśakalaṃ) – crescent moon (candra:moon | śakalaṃ:crescent); धनुः (dhanuḥ) – bow; शौनासीरं (śaunāsīraṃ) – Indra's bow; किमिति (kimiti) – why; न (na) – not; निबध्नाति (nibadhnāti) – binds; धिषणाम् (dhiṣaṇām) – intellect;

contentBlocks.original:
धुनोतु ध्वान्तं नस्तुलितदलितेन्दीवरवनं
घनस्निग्धश्लक्ष्णं चिकुरनिकुरुम्बं तव शिवे ।
यदीयं सौरभ्यं सहजमुपलब्धुं सुमनसो
वसन्त्यस्मिन् मन्ये वलमथनवाटीविटपिनाम् ॥ ४३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhunotu dhvāntaṃ nastulitadalitendīvaravanaṃ
ghanasnigdhaślakṣṇaṃ cikuranikurumbaṃ tava śive .
yadīyaṃ saurabhyaṃ sahajamupalabdhuṃ sumanaso
vasantyasmin manye valamathanavāṭīviṭapinām .. 43..
contentBlocks.translation: O Consort of Śiva! May Thy braid of locks resembling a forest of full-blown blue lotus flowers, luxuriant, soft and oily, remove the darkness of ignorance in our hearts. I presume that the heavenly flowers of Indra’s garden have taken a place in Thy locks to imbibe a little of the natural fragrance of those locks (and not to add fragrance to the locks like flowers ordinarily worn by women.) 43.
contentBlocks.meanings: धुनोतु (dhunotu) – may remove; ध्वान्तं (dhvāntaṃ) – darkness; नः (naḥ) – our; तुलितदलितेन्दीवरवनं (tulitadalitendīvaravanaṃ) – like a forest of blue lotuses (tulita:full-blown | dalita:bloomed | indīvara:blue lotus | vanaṃ:forest); घनस्निग्धश्लक्ष्णं (ghanasnigdhaślakṣṇaṃ) – luxuriant, soft and oily (ghana:dense | snigdha:oily | ślakṣṇaṃ:soft); चिकुरनिकुरुम्बं (cikuranikurumbaṃ) – braid of locks (cikura:hair | nikurumbaṃ:cluster); तव (tava) – your; शिवे (śive) – O Śiva's consort; यदीयं (yadīyaṃ) – whose; सौरभ्यं (saurabhyaṃ) – fragrance; सहजम् (sahajam) – natural; उपलब्धुं (upalabdhuṃ) – to obtain; सुमनसः (sumanasaḥ) – flowers; वसन्ति (vasanti) – reside; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; मन्ये (manye) – I presume; वलमथनवाटीविटपिनाम् (valamathanavāṭīviṭapinām) – of Indra's garden trees (vala:Indra | mathana:churning | vāṭī:garden | viṭapinām:of trees);

contentBlocks.original:
तनोतु क्षेमं नस्तव वदनसौन्दर्यलहरी-
परीवाहस्रोतःसरणिरिव सीमन्तसरणिः ।
वहन्ती सिन्दूरं प्रबलकबरीभारतिमिर-
द्विषां बृन्दैर्बन्दीकृतमिव नवीनार्ककिरणम् ॥ ४४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tanotu kṣemaṃ nastava vadanasaundaryalaharī-
parīvāhasrotaḥsaraṇiriva sīmantasaraṇiḥ .
vahantī sindūraṃ prabalakabarībhāratimira-
dviṣāṃ bṛndairbandīkṛtamiva navīnārkakiraṇam .. 44..
contentBlocks.translation: May we be blessed ever more by the parting middle line of Thy hair, which appears to be a canal for carrying the overflowing flood of beauty of Thy face. The streak of vermilion adorning that line looks like the rays of the rising sun entrapped by the opposing enemy hordes of darkness constituted of Thy hair hemming it on both sides. 44.
contentBlocks.meanings: तनोतु (tanotu) – may it grant; क्षेमं (kṣemam) – welfare; नः (naḥ) – to us; तव (tava) – your; वदनसौन्दर्यलहरी (vadanasaundaryalaharī) – wave of face's beauty (vadana:face | saundarya:beauty | laharī:wave); परीवाहस्रोतःसरणिः (parīvāhasrotaḥsaraṇiḥ) – canal of overflowing stream (parīvāha:overflowing | srotaḥ:stream | saraṇiḥ:canal); इव (iva) – like; सीमन्तसरणिः (sīmantasaraṇiḥ) – parting line (sīmanta:parting | saraṇiḥ:line); वहन्ती (vahantī) – carrying; सिन्दूरं (sindūram) – vermilion; प्रबलकबरीभारतिमिर (prabalakabarībhāratimira) – darkness of thick hair (prabala:strong | kabarī:hair | bhāra:mass | timira:darkness); द्विषां (dviṣām) – of enemies; बृन्दैः (bṛndaiḥ) – by groups; बन्दीकृतमिव (bandīkṛtamiva) – as if captured (bandī:captured | kṛtam:made | iva:as if); नवीनार्ककिरणम् (navīnārkakiraṇam) – rays of the new sun (navīna:new | arka:sun | kiraṇam:rays);

contentBlocks.original:
अरालैः स्वाभाव्यादलिकलभसश्रीभिरलकैः
परीतं ते वक्त्रं परिहसति पङ्केरुहरुचिम् ।
दरस्मेरे यस्मिन् दशनरुचिकिञ्जल्करुचिरे
सुगन्धौ माद्यन्ति स्मरदहनचक्षुर्मधुलिहः ॥ ४५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arālaiḥ svābhāvyādalikalabhasaśrībhiralakaiḥ
parītaṃ te vaktraṃ parihasati paṅkeruharucim .
darasmere yasmin daśanarucikiñjalkarucire
sugandhau mādyanti smaradahanacakṣurmadhulihaḥ .. 45..
contentBlocks.translation: Thy face surrounded by Thy naturally curly Alakas (fore-locks), beautiful like a swarm of young honey beetles, mocks at the celebrated beauty of lotus flowers. In that smiling and fragrant face, excelling the beauty of lotus flowers and rendered charming by the brilliance of Thy lotus-filament-iike rows of teeth, revel the intoxicated honey suckers of the eyes of Śiva, the destroyer of Cupid. 45.
contentBlocks.meanings: अरालैः (arālaiḥ) – curved; स्वाभाव्यात् (svābhāvyāt) – naturally; अलिकलभसश्रीभिः (alikalabhasaśrībhiḥ) – with beauty of young bees (ali:bee | kalabha:young | śrībhiḥ:with beauty); अलकैः (alakaiḥ) – with fore-locks; परीतम् (parītam) – surrounded; ते (te) – your; वक्त्रं (vaktraṃ) – face; परिहसति (parihasati) – mocks; पङ्केरुहरुचिम् (paṅkeruharucim) – beauty of lotus flowers (paṅka:mud | iruha:growing | rucim:beauty); दरस्मेरे (darasmere) – slightly smiling; यस्मिन् (yasmin) – in which; दशनरुचिकिञ्जल्करुचिरे (daśanarucikiñjalkarucire) – charming with teeth's brilliance (daśana:teeth | ruci:brilliance | kiñjalka:filament | rucire:charming); सुगन्धौ (sugandhau) – fragrant; माद्यन्ति (mādyanti) – revel; स्मरदहनचक्षुः (smaradahanacakṣuḥ) – eyes of Śiva (smara:Cupid | dahana:destroyer | cakṣuḥ:eyes); मधुलिहः (madhulihaḥ) – honey suckers;

contentBlocks.original:
ललाटं लावण्यद्युतिविमलमाभाति तव य-
द्द्वितीयं तन्मन्ये मकुटघटितं चन्द्रशकलम् ।
विपर्यासन्यासादुभयमपि संभूय च मिथः
सुधालेपस्यूतिः परिणमति राकाहिमकरः ॥ ४६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lalāṭaṃ lāvaṇyadyutivimalamābhāti tava ya-
ddvitīyaṃ tanmanye makuṭaghaṭitaṃ candraśakalam .
viparyāsanyāsādubhayamapi saṃbhūya ca mithaḥ
sudhālepasyūtiḥ pariṇamati rākāhimakaraḥ .. 46..
contentBlocks.translation: Thy forehead, shining with the pure brilliance of its divine beauty, is, I fancy, a second crescent moon inverted and attached below Thy crown (in addition to the crescent moon already on it).
