If these two halves (the one on Thy crown with ends up and the other the crescent moon of Thy forehead with ends down) are attached in the reverse order, they can form the full moon with the nectarine fluid dripping from it. 46.
contentBlocks.meanings: ललाटं (lalāṭam) – forehead; लावण्य (lāvaṇya) – beauty; द्युतिविमलम् (dyutivimalam) – pure brilliance (dyuti:brilliance | vimalam:pure); आभाति (ābhāti) – shines; तव (tava) – your; यद् (yad) – which; द्वितीयं (dvitīyam) – second; तन्मन्ये (tanmanye) – I think that (tat:that | manye:I think); मकुटघटितं (makuṭaghaṭitam) – attached to the crown (makuṭa:crown | ghaṭitam:attached); चन्द्रशकलम् (candraśakalam) – crescent moon (candra:moon | śakalam:crescent); विपर्यास (viparyāsa) – inversion; न्यासात् (nyāsāt) – by placement; उभयम् (ubhayam) – both; अपि (api) – also; संभूय (saṃbhūya) – together; च (ca) – and; मिथः (mithaḥ) – mutually; सुधालेपस्यूतिः (sudhālepasūtiḥ) – nectar fluid dripping (sudhā:nectar | lepa:fluid | sūtiḥ:dripping); परिणमति (pariṇamati) – transforms; राका (rākā) – full moon; हिमकरः (himakaraḥ) – moon (hima:cold | karaḥ:maker);

contentBlocks.original:
भ्रुवौ भुग्ने किंचिद्भुवनभयभङ्गव्यसनिनि
त्वदीये नेत्राभ्यां मधुकररुचिभ्यां धृतगुणम् ।
धनुर्मन्ये सव्येतरकरगृहीतं रतिपतेः
प्रकोष्ठे मुष्टौ च स्थगयति निगूढान्तरमुमे ॥ ४७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhruvau bhugne kiṃcidbhuvanabhayabhaṅgavyasanini
tvadīye netrābhyāṃ madhukararucibhyāṃ dhṛtaguṇam .
dhanurmanye savyetarakaragṛhītaṃ ratipateḥ
prakoṣṭhe muṣṭau ca sthagayati nigūḍhāntaramume .. 47..
contentBlocks.translation: O Uma, ever intent on remedying the distress of the worlds! I am led to believe that Thy two slightly knitted eyebrows form the bow of Kāmadeva and Thy honey-beetle like black eyes its string, while its middle portion is hidden by the nasal ridge, as if it were the clenched fist and forearm of Kama Deva’s right arm holding it. 47.
contentBlocks.meanings: भ्रुवौ (bhruvau) – eyebrows; भुग्ने (bhugne) – slightly bent; किंचित् (kiṃcit) – slightly; भुवनभयभङ्गव्यसनिनि (bhuvanabhayabhaṅgavyasanini) – intent on remedying world's distress (bhuvana:world | bhaya:fear | bhaṅga:remedy | vyasanini:intent on); त्वदीये (tvadīye) – Thy; नेत्राभ्यां (netrābhyāṃ) – with eyes; मधुकररुचिभ्यां (madhukararucibhyāṃ) – honey-beetle like (madhukara:honey-beetle | ruci:like); धृतगुणम् (dhṛtaguṇam) – holding the string (dhṛta:holding | guṇam:string); धनुः (dhanuḥ) – bow; मन्ये (manye) – I think; सव्येतरकरगृहीतम् (savyetarakaragṛhītam) – held by right hand (savya:left | itara:other | kara:hand | gṛhītam:held); रतिपतेः (ratipateḥ) – of Kāmadeva (rati:love | pateḥ:lord); प्रकोष्ठे (prakoṣṭhe) – forearm; मुष्टौ (muṣṭau) – fist; च (ca) – and; स्थगयति (sthagayati) – conceals; निगूढान्तरम् (nigūḍhāntaram) – hidden middle portion (nigūḍha:hidden | antaram:middle portion); उमे (ume) – O Uma;

contentBlocks.original:
अहः सूते सव्यं तव नयनमर्कात्मकतया
त्रियामां वामं ते सृजति रजनीनायकतया ।
तृतीया ते दृष्टिर्दरदलितहेमाम्बुजरुचिः
समाधत्ते संध्यां दिवसनिशयोरन्तरचरीम् ॥ ४८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ahaḥ sūte savyaṃ tava nayanamarkātmakatayā
triyāmāṃ vāmaṃ te sṛjati rajanīnāyakatayā .
tṛtīyā te dṛṣṭirdaradalitahemāmbujaruciḥ
samādhatte saṃdhyāṃ divasaniśayorantaracarīm .. 48..
contentBlocks.translation: Thy right eye as the sun causes the day time, while from the left eye is born the night time with the moon as its lord. Then there is Thy third eye (on the forehead above), resembling a golden lotus slightly in bloom, which is responsible for the two Sandhyas, the twilight time that comes between day and night (in the morning and the evening). 48.
contentBlocks.meanings: अहः (ahaḥ) – day; सूते (sūte) – gives birth; सव्यं (savyaṃ) – right; तव (tava) – your; नयनम् (nayanam) – eye; अर्कात्मकतया (arkātmakatayā) – as the sun (arka:sun | ātmakatayā:as); त्रियामाम् (triyāmām) – night; वामं (vāmaṃ) – left; ते (te) – your; सृजति (sṛjati) – creates; रजनीनायकतया (rajanīnāyakatayā) – with the moon as lord (rajanī:night | nāyaka:lord | tayā:as); तृतीया (tṛtīyā) – third; ते (te) – your; दृष्टिः (dṛṣṭiḥ) – eye; दरदलितहेमाम्बुजरुचिः (daradalitahemāmbujaruciḥ) – resembling a golden lotus (dara:slightly | dalita:bloomed | hema:golden | ambuja:lotus | ruciḥ:resembling); समाधत्ते (samādhatte) – establishes; संध्याम् (saṃdhyām) – twilight; दिवसनिशयोः (divasaniśayoḥ) – of day and night (divasa:day | niśayoḥ:of night); अन्तरचरीम् (antaracarīm) – in between;

contentBlocks.original:
विशाला कल्याणी स्फुटरुचिरयोध्या कुवलयैः
कृपाधाराधारा किमपि मधुराभोगवतिका ।
अवन्ती दृष्टिस्ते बहुनगरविस्तारविजया
ध्रुवं तत्तन्नामव्यवहरणयोग्या विजयते ॥ ४९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśālā kalyāṇī sphuṭarucirayodhyā kuvalayaiḥ
kṛpādhārādhārā kimapi madhurābhogavatikā .
avantī dṛṣṭiste bahunagaravistāravijayā
dhruvaṃ tattannāmavyavaharaṇayogyā vijayate .. 49..
contentBlocks.translation: All glory to Thy eyes which are wide (Viśāla); auspicious (Kalyāṇī) because of being brilliantly clear; undefeated (Ayodhyā) even by blue lilies; shedding a continuous flow of grace (Kṛpādhārā dhārā); sweet without display (Avyakta-madhura); long (Ābhogavatī); and offering protection to the world (Avantī). Surpassing (Vijayā) all these great cities in their uniqueness, Thy eyes deserve to be denoted by all the names mentioned above. 49.
contentBlocks.meanings: विशाला (viśālā) – wide; कल्याणी (kalyāṇī) – auspicious; स्फुटरुचिरयोध्या (sphuṭarucirayodhyā) – undefeated by blue lilies (sphuṭa:clear | ruci:brilliance | ayodhyā:undefeated); कुवलयैः (kuvalayaiḥ) – by blue lilies; कृपाधाराधारा (kṛpādhārādhārā) – shedding a flow of grace (kṛpā:grace | dhārā:flow | dhārā:continuous); किमपि (kimapi) – somewhat; मधुराभोगवतिका (madhurābhogavatikā) – sweet without display (madhura:sweet | ābhoga:without display | vatikā:long); अवन्ती (avantī) – offering protection; दृष्टिः (dṛṣṭiḥ) – sight; ते (te) – your; बहुनगरविस्तारविजया (bahunagaravistāravijayā) – surpassing great cities (bahu:many | nagara:cities | vistāra:expansion | vijayā:victorious); ध्रुवं (dhruvaṃ) – certainly; तत्तन्नामव्यवहरणयोग्या (tattannāmavyavaharaṇayogyā) – deserving all names (tat:that | tat:that | nāma:name | vyavaharaṇa:usage | yogyā:deserving); विजयते (vijayate) – triumphs;

contentBlocks.original:
कवीनां संदर्भस्तबकमकरन्दैकरसिकं
कटाक्षव्याक्षेपभ्रमरकलभौ कर्णयुगलम् ।
अमुञ्चन्तौ दृष्ट्वा तव नवरसास्वादतरला-
वसूयासंसर्गादलिकनयनं किंचिदरुणम् ॥ ५०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kavīnāṃ saṃdarbhastabakamakarandaikarasikaṃ
kaṭākṣavyākṣepabhramarakalabhau karṇayugalam .
amuñcantau dṛṣṭvā tava navarasāsvādataralā-
vasūyāsaṃsargādalikanayanaṃ kiṃcidaruṇam .. 50..
contentBlocks.translation: The two honey-bees of Thy long eyes are unceasingly hovering about Thy ear, ever absorbed in Imbibing the honey dripping from the flower bunch of the nine poetic sentiments poured into them by poet-devotees (singing hymns on Thee). Seeing this good fortune of the two eyes, Thy third eye in the forehead looks slightly red out of jealous hostility. 50.
contentBlocks.meanings: कवीनां (kavīnāṃ) – of the poets; संदर्भ (saṃdarbha) – context; स्तबक (stabaka) – cluster; मकरन्द (makaranda) – nectar; एक (eka) – one; रसिकं (rasikaṃ) – connoisseur; कटाक्ष (kaṭākṣa) – glance; व्याक्षेप (vyākṣepa) – distraction; भ्रमर (bhramara) – bee; कलभौ (kalabhau) – young bees; कर्णयुगलम् (karṇayugalam) – pair of ears (karṇa:ear | yugalam:pair); अमुचन्तौ (amuñcantau) – not leaving; दृष्ट्वा (dṛṣṭvā) – having seen; तव (tava) – your; नवरस (navarasa) – nine sentiments; आस्वाद (āsvāda) – taste; तरला (taralā) – agitated; वसूया (vasūyā) – jealousy; संसर्गात् (saṃsargāt) – due to contact; अलिक (alika) – forehead; नयनं (nayanaṃ) – eye; किंचित् (kiṃcit) – slightly; अरुणम् (aruṇam) – red;

contentBlocks.original:
शिवे श‍ृङ्गारार्द्रा तदितरजने कुत्सनपरा
सरोषा गङ्गायां गिरिशचरिते विस्मयवती ।
हराहिभ्यो भीता सरसिरुहसौभाग्यजयिनी
सखीषु स्मेरा ते मयि जननी दृष्टिः सकरुणा ॥ ५१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śive śṛṅgārārdrā taditarajane kutsanaparā
saroṣā gaṅgāyāṃ giriśacarite vismayavatī .
harāhibhyo bhītā sarasiruhasaubhāgyajayinī
sakhīṣu smerā te mayi jananī dṛṣṭiḥ sakaruṇā .. 51..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! The expression of Thy look at Siva is characterised by the sentiment of love; at others with that of dislike; at the co-wife Gangā with that of anger; at the hearing of the great deeds of Siva, with that of wonder; at the great serpents forming the ornament of Siva, with that of dread; at the sight of Thy comrades, with that of light-hearted sympathy characterised by a patronising smile; and at me, a devotee, with that of a compassionate expression. And besides, Thy look has the red-tinged loveliness of a lotus flower, indicating heroism. 51.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिवे (śive) – at Shiva; श‍ृङ्गारार्द्रा (śṛṅgārārdrā) – moist with love (śṛṅgāra:love | ārdra:moist); तदितरजने (taditarajane) – at others (tad:that | itara:other | jane:people); कुत्सनपरा (kutsanaparā) – engaged in disdain (kutsana:disdain | parā:engaged in); सरोषा (saroṣā) – angry; गङ्गायाम् (gaṅgāyām) – at Ganga; गिरिशचरिते (giriśacarite) – at Shiva's deeds (giriśa:Shiva | carite:deeds); विस्मयवती (vismayavatī) – full of wonder (vismaya:wonder | vatī:full of); हराहिभ्यः (harāhibhyaḥ) – from Shiva's serpents (hara:Shiva | ahi:serpents | bhyaḥ:from); भीता (bhītā) – afraid; सरसिरुहसौभाग्यजयिनी (sarasiruha-saubhāgya-jayinī) – surpassing lotus beauty (sarasiruha:lotus | saubhāgya:beauty | jayinī:surpassing); सखीषु (sakhīṣu) – among friends; स्मेरा (smerā) – smiling; ते (te) – your; मयि (mayi) – at me; जननी (jananī) – O Mother; दृष्टिः (dṛṣṭiḥ) – look; सकरुणा (sakaruṇā) – with compassion (sa:with | karuṇā:compassion);

contentBlocks.original:
गते कर्णाभ्यर्णं गरुत इव पक्ष्माणि दधती
पुरां भेत्तुश्चित्तप्रशमरसविद्रावणफले ।
इमे नेत्रे गोत्राधरपतिकुलोत्तंसकलिके
तवाकर्णाकृष्टस्मरशरविलासं कलयतः ॥ ५२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gate karṇābhyarṇaṃ garuta iva pakṣmāṇi dadhatī
purām behttuś citta-praśama-rasa-vidrūvaṇa-phale .
ime netre gotrādharapatikulottaṃsakalike
tavākarṇākṛṣṭasmaraśaravilāsaṃ kalayataḥ .. 52..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! Thou the flower bud placed on the crest of the Mountain King’s dynasty! These long eyes of Thine, which extend up to the ears, with eye-lashes resembling the feathery wings attached to arrows and which are engaged in disturbing the placidity of the mind of Śiva look like the arrows of Cupid aimed and drawn up to the ear. 52.
contentBlocks.meanings: गते (gate) – gone; कर्णाभ्यर्णं (karṇābhyarṇaṃ) – up to the ears (karṇa:ear | abhyarṇaṃ:up to); गरुत (garuta) – like Garuda; इव (iva) – like; पक्ष्माणि (pakṣmāṇi) – eye-lashes; दधती (dadhatī) – bearing; पुरां (purām) – of cities; भेत्तु (bhettu) – destroyer; चित्तप्रशमरसविद्रावणफले (cittapraśamarasavidrāvaṇaphale) – disturbing Śiva's mind (citta:mind | praśama:calmness | rasa:essence | vidrāvaṇa:disturbing | phale:result); इमे (ime) – these; नेत्रे (netre) – eyes; गोत्राधरपतिकुलोत्तंसकलिके (gotrādharapatikulottaṃsakalike) – crest of the Mountain King’s dynasty (gotra:dynasty | adhara:lower | pati:lord | kula:family | uttaṃsa:crest | kalike:bud); तव (tava) – your; आकर्णाकृष्ट (ākarṇākṛṣṭa) – drawn up to the ear (ākarṇa:up to the ear | ākṛṣṭa:drawn); स्मरशरविलासं (smaraśaravilāsaṃ) – Cupid's arrow play (smara:Cupid | śara:arrow | vilāsaṃ:play); कलयतः (kalayataḥ) – considering;

contentBlocks.original:
विभक्तत्रैवर्ण्यं व्यतिकरितलीलाञ्जनतया
विभाति त्वन्नेत्रत्रितयमिदमीशानदयिते ।
पुनः स्रष्टुं देवान् द्रुहिणहरिरुद्रानुपरतान्
रजः सत्त्वं बिभ्रत्तम इति गुणानां त्रयमिव ॥ ५३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vibhaktatraivarṇyaṃ vyatikaritalīlāñjanatayā
vibhāti tvannetratritayamidamīśānadayite .
punaḥ sraṣṭuṃ devān druhiṇaharirudrānuparatān
rajaḥ sattvaṃ bibhrattama iti guṇānāṃ trayamiva .. 53..
contentBlocks.translation: O Consort of Īśvara! Thy three eyes look tricoloured when the black of the beautifying collyrium shines by the side of their natural white and reddish tinges, each keeping its distinctiveness. It looks as if these three colours represent the three Guṇas of Rajas, Sattva and Tamas, which Thou assumest with a view to revive Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra after their dissolution in the Pralaya, and start them once again on the creative activity. 53.
contentBlocks.meanings: विभक्तत्रैवर्ण्यं (vibhaktatraivarṇyaṃ) – having three distinct colors (vibhakta:distinct | trai:three | varṇyaṃ:colors); व्यतिकरितलीलाञ्जनतया (vyatikaritalīlāñjanatayā) – by playful collyrium application (vyatikarita:applied | līlā:playful | añjanatayā:by collyrium); विभाति (vibhāti) – shines; त्वत् (tvat) – your; नेत्रत्रितयम् (netratritayam) – three eyes (netra:eye | tritayam:three); इदम् (idam) – this; ईशानदयिते (īśānadayite) – O Consort of Īśvara (īśāna:Īśvara | dayite:consort); पुनः (punaḥ) – again; स्रष्टुम् (sraṣṭum) – to create; देवान् (devān) – gods; द्रुहिणहरिरुद्रानुपरतान् (druhiṇaharirudrānuparatān) – Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Rudra inactive (druhiṇa:Brahmā | hari:Viṣṇu | rudra:Rudra | anuparatān:inactive); रजः (rajaḥ) – rajas; सत्त्वं (sattvaṃ) – sattva; बिभ्रत् (bibhrat) – assuming; तम (tama) – tamas; इति (iti) – thus; गुणानां (guṇānāṃ) – of qualities; त्रयम् (trayam) – three; इव (iva) – like;

contentBlocks.original:
पवित्रीकर्तुं नः पशुपतिपराधीनहृदये
दयामित्रैर्नेत्रैररुणधवलश्यामरुचिभिः ।
नदः शोणो गङ्गा तपनतनयेति ध्रुवममुं
त्रयाणां तीर्थानामुपनयसि संभेदमनघम् ॥ ५४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pavitrīkartuṃ naḥ paśupatiparādhīnahṛdaye
dayāmitrairnetrairaruṇadhavalaśyāmarucibhiḥ .
nadaḥ śoṇo gaṅgā tapanatanayeti dhruvamamuṃ
trayāṇāṃ tīrthānāmupanayasi saṃbhedamanagham .. 54..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother who art ever devoted to Śiva! It seems certain that with Thy kindly eyes having the three colours of red, white and black, Thou presentest to us the confluence of the holy rivers of Soṇa, Gaṅgā and Yamunā to sanctify ourselves (by getting immersed in them). 54.
contentBlocks.meanings: पवित्रीकर्तुं (pavitrīkartuṃ) – to sanctify (pavitrī:sanctify | kartuṃ:to do); नः (naḥ) – us; पशुपतिपराधीनहृदये (paśupatiparādhīnahṛdaye) – in heart devoted to Śiva (paśupati:Śiva | parādhīna:devoted | hṛdaye:in heart); दयामित्रैः (dayāmitraiḥ) – with kindly eyes (dayā:kindness | mitraiḥ:with eyes); नेत्रैः (netraiḥ) – with eyes; अरुणधवलश्यामरुचिभिः (aruṇadhavalaśyāmarucibhiḥ) – with red, white, black hues (aruṇa:red | dhavala:white | śyāma:black | rucibhiḥ:with hues); नदः (nadaḥ) – river; शोणः (śoṇaḥ) – Soṇa; गङ्गा (gaṅgā) – Gaṅgā; तपनतनय (tapanatanaya) – Yamunā (tapana:sun | tanaya:daughter); इति (iti) – thus; ध्रुवम् (dhruvam) – certainly; अम् (amum) – this; त्रयाणाम् (trayāṇām) – of the three; तीर्थानाम् (tīrthānām) – of holy places; उपनयसि (upanayasi) – you present; संभेदम् (saṃbhedam) – confluence; अनघम् (anagham) – pure;

contentBlocks.original:
निमेषोन्मेषाभ्यां प्रलयमुदयं याति जगती
तवेत्याहुः सन्तो धरणिधरराजन्यतनये ।
त्वदुन्मेषाज्जातं जगदिदमशेषं प्रलयतः
परित्रातुं शङ्के परिहृतनिमेषास्तव दृशः ॥ ५५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nimeṣonmeṣābhyāṃ pralayamudayaṃ yāti jagatī
tavetyāhuḥ santo dharaṇidhararājanyatanaye .
tvadunmeṣājjātaṃ jagadidamaśeṣaṃ pralayataḥ
paritrātuṃ śaṅke parihṛtanimeṣāstava dṛśaḥ .. 55..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the king of mountains! Great men say that the closing and opening of Thy eye-lids mark the dissolution and creation of this universe. Therefore it must be to prevent this universe, that has sprung at the opening of Thy eyes, from going into dissolution that Thou dost not wink but keepest Thy eyes always open. 55.
contentBlocks.meanings: निमेषोन्मेषाभ्यां (nimeṣonmeṣābhyāṃ) – by closing and opening (nimeṣa:closing | unmeṣa:opening | abhyāṃ:by); प्रलयम् (pralayam) – dissolution; उदयं (udayaṃ) – creation; याति (yāti) – goes; जगती (jagatī) – universe; तव (tava) – your; इति (iti) – thus; आहुः (āhuḥ) – say; सन्तः (santaḥ) – great men; धरणिधरराजन्यतनये (dharaṇidhararājanyatanaye) – O daughter of the mountain king (dharaṇidhara:mountain | rājanya:king | tanaye:daughter); त्वदुन्मेषात् (tvadunmeṣāt) – from your opening (tvat:your | unmeṣāt:from opening); जातम् (jātam) – born; जगत् (jagat) – world; इदम् (idam) – this; अशेषम् (aśeṣam) – entire; प्रलयतः (pralayataḥ) – from dissolution; परित्रातुम् (paritrātum) – to protect; शङ्के (śaṅke) – I suspect; परिहृतनिमेषाः (parihṛtanimeṣāḥ) – avoiding closing (parihṛta:avoiding | nimeṣāḥ:closing); तव (tava) – your; दृशः (dṛśaḥ) – eyes;

contentBlocks.original:
तवापर्णे कर्णेजपनयनपैशुन्यचकिता
निलीयन्ते तोये नियतमनिमेषाः शफरिकाः ।
इयं च श्रीर्बद्धच्छदपुटकवाटं कुवलयम्
जहाति प्रत्यूषे निशि च विघटय्य प्रविशति ॥ ५६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tavāparṇe karṇejapanayanapaiśunyacakitā
nilīyante toye niyatamanimeṣāḥ śapharikāḥ .
iyaṃ ca śrīrbaddhacchadapuṭakavāṭaṃ kuvalayam
jahāti pratyūṣe niśi ca vighaṭayya praviśati .. 56..
contentBlocks.translation: O Aparṇā! The Śapharikas (female fish) hide themselves in water in fear, afraid of the tell-tale activities of Thy eyes against them, their rivals; and Śri the Goddess of Beauty, abandons the closed petals of blue lily during the day (in order to reside in Thy lotus-like eyes), and returns again at night to the blooming blue lily (when Thy eyes are dosed in sleep). 56.
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; अपर्णे (aparṇe) – O Aparṇā; कर्णे (karṇe) – in the ear; जपनयनपैशुन्यचकिता (japanayanapaiśunyacakitā) – afraid of tell-tale eyes (japa:murmuring | nayana:eyes | paiśunya:tell-tale | cakitā:afraid); निलीयन्ते (nilīyante) – hide themselves; तोये (toye) – in water; नियतमनिमेषाः (niyatamanimeṣāḥ) – fixed and unblinking (niyata:fixed | animeṣāḥ:unblinking); शफरिकाः (śapharikāḥ) – female fish; इयं (iyaṃ) – this; च (ca) – and; श्रीः (śrīḥ) – Goddess of Beauty; बद्धच्छदपुटकवाटं (baddhacchadapuṭakavāṭaṃ) – closed petals of blue lily (baddha:closed | chada:petals | puṭa:cover | kavāṭaṃ:door); कुवलयम् (kuvalayam) – blue lily; जहाति (jahāti) – abandons; प्रत्यूषे (pratyūṣe) – at dawn; निशि (niśi) – at night; च (ca) – and; विघटय्य (vighaṭayya) – opening; प्रविशति (praviśati) – enters;

contentBlocks.original:
दृशा द्राघीयस्या दरदलितनीलोत्पलरुचा
दवीयांसं दीनं स्नपय कृपया मामपि शिवे ।
अनेनायं धन्यो भवति न च ते हानिरियता
वने वा हर्म्ये वा समकरनिपातो हिमकरः ॥ ५७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dṛśā drāghīyasyā daradalitanīlotpalarucā
davīyāṃsaṃ dīnaṃ snapaya kṛpayā māmapi śive .
anenāyaṃ dhanyo bhavati na ca te hāniriyatā
vane vā harmye vā samakaranipāto himakaraḥ .. 57..
contentBlocks.translation: O Consort of Śiva! Grace my miserable self too, in spite of my being far removed from Thee (for lack of devotion), with the long-ranging and compassionate look of Thy eyes, which defeat the slightly blooming blue water lily in beauty. By this my humble self shall feel blessed, while to Thee it involves no loss. The rays of the moon fall alike, indeed, on a mansion and a wilderness. 57.
contentBlocks.meanings: दृशा (dṛśā) – with the look; द्राघीयस्या (drāghīyasyā) – long-ranging; दरदलितनीलोत्पलरुचा (daradalitanīlotpalarucā) – defeating blue water lily (dara:slightly blooming | dalita:defeated | nīla:blue | utpala:water lily | rucā:in beauty); दवीयांसं (davīyāṃsaṃ) – far removed; दीनं (dīnaṃ) – miserable; स्नपय (snapaya) – grace; कृपया (kṛpayā) – with compassion; मामपि (māmapi) – myself too (mām:myself | api:too); शिवे (śive) – O Consort of Śiva; अनेन (anena) – by this; अयं (ayaṃ) – this; धन्यः (dhanyaḥ) – blessed; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; न (na) – not; च (ca) – and; ते (te) – to you; हानिः (hāniḥ) – loss; इयता (iyatā) – by this; वने (vane) – in the forest; वा (vā) – or; हर्म्ये (harmye) – in the mansion; वा (vā) – or; समकरनिपातः (samakaranipātaḥ) – equal fall (sama:equal | kara:ray | nipātaḥ:fall); हिमकरः (himakaraḥ) – moon (hima:cool | karaḥ:ray);

contentBlocks.original:
अरालं ते पालीयुगलमगराजन्यतनये
न केषामाधत्ते कुसुमशरकोदण्डकुतुकम् ।
तिरश्चीनो यत्र श्रवणपथमुल्लङ्घ्य विलस-
न्नपाङ्गव्यासङ्गो दिशति शरसंधानधिषणाम् ॥ ५८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arālaṃ te pālīyugalamagarājanyatanaye
na keṣāmādhatte kusumaśarakodaṇḍakutukam .
tiraścīno yatra śravaṇapathamullaṅghya vilasa-
nnapāṅgavyāsaṅgo diśati śarasaṃdhānadhiṣaṇām .. 58..
contentBlocks.translation: O daughter of the Mountain King! Who will not perceive the graces of the bow of Kāmā, the flower-arrowed god of love, on seeing Thy pair of arched ridges between the eyes and the ears (Pālī-ugalam.) For, Thy long side-glances across the ridges reaching up to Thy ears give the impression of an arrow mounted on a bow-string. 58.
contentBlocks.meanings: अरालं (arālaṃ) – curved; ते (te) – your; पालीयुगलम् (pālīyugalam) – pair of ridges (pālī:ridge | yugalam:pair); अगराजन्यतनये (agarājanyatanaye) – O daughter of the Mountain King (aga:mountain | rājanya:king | tanaye:daughter); न (na) – not; केषाम् (keṣām) – of whom; आधत्ते (ādhatte) – perceives; कुसुमशरकोदण्डकुतुकम् (kusumaśarakodaṇḍakutukam) – graces of the flower-arrowed bow (kusuma:flower | śara:arrow | kodaṇḍa:bow | kutukam:graces); तिरश्चीनः (tiraścīnaḥ) – oblique; यत्र (yatra) – where; श्रवणपथमुल्लङ्घ्य (śravaṇapathamullaṅghya) – crossing the path to ears (śravaṇa:ears | patham:path | ullaṅghya:crossing); विलसन् (vilasan) – shining; अपाङ्गव्यासङ्गः (apāṅgavyāsaṅgaḥ) – side-glance (apāṅga:side | vyāsaṅgaḥ:glance); दिशति (diśati) – gives; शरसंधानधिषणाम् (śarasaṃdhānadhiṣaṇām) – impression of an arrow (śara:arrow | saṃdhāna:mounting | dhiṣaṇām:impression);

contentBlocks.original:
स्फुरद्गण्डाभोगप्रतिफलितताटङ्कयुगलं
चतुश्चक्रं मन्ये तव मुखमिदं मन्मथरथम् ।
यमारुह्य द्रुह्यत्यवनिरथमर्केन्दुचरणं
महावीरो मारः प्रमथपतये सज्जितवते ॥ ५९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sphuradgaṇḍābhogapratiphalitatāṭaṅkayugalaṃ
catuścakraṃ manye tava mukhamidaṃ manmatharatham .
yamāruhya druhyatyavanirathamarkenducaraṇaṃ
mahāvīro māraḥ pramathapataye sajjitavate .. 59..
contentBlocks.translation: I fancy that Thy face, having two ear-ornaments and their reflection in Thy glistening cheeks, is verily the four-wheeled chariot of Manmatha, the god of love (who can stir the mind of people to its depth). It is seated in this chariot of Thy face that he became valiant enough to inflict pangs on Śiva, the Lord of Pramatha hosts, when, ready for the destruction of the Tripuras, he was mounted on his earth-chariot having the sun and the moon for its two wheels. 59.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्फुरद्गण्डाभोगप्रतिफलितताटङ्कयुगलं (sphuradgaṇḍābhogapratiphalitatāṭaṅkayugalaṃ) – shining cheeks reflecting earrings (sphurat:shining | gaṇḍa:cheeks | ābhoga:expanse | pratiphalita:reflected | tāṭaṅka:earrings | yugalaṃ:pair); चतुश्चक्रं (catuścakraṃ) – four-wheeled (catur:four | cakraṃ:wheel); मन्ये (manye) – I consider; तव (tava) – your; मुखमिदं (mukhamidaṃ) – this face (mukham:face | idam:this); मन्मथरथम् (manmatharatham) – chariot of Manmatha (manmatha:Manmatha | ratham:chariot); यमारुह्य (yamāruhya) – having mounted; द्रुह्यति (druhyati) – inflicts pangs; अवनिरथम् (avaniratham) – earth-chariot (avani:earth | ratham:chariot); अर्केन्दुचरणं (arkenducaraṇaṃ) – sun and moon wheels (arka:sun | indu:moon | caraṇaṃ:wheels); महावीरः (mahāvīraḥ) – great hero (mahā:great | vīraḥ:hero); मारः (māraḥ) – Mara; प्रमथपतये (pramathapataye) – to the lord of Pramathas (pramatha:Pramathas | pataye:to the lord); सज्जितवते (sajjitavate) – ready for;

contentBlocks.original:
सरस्वत्याः सूक्तीरमृतलहरीकौशलहरीः
पिबन्त्याः शर्वाणि श्रवणचुलुकाभ्यामविरलम् ।
चमत्कारश्लाघाचलितशिरसः कुण्डलगणो
झणत्कारैस्तारैः प्रतिवचनमाचष्ट इव ते ॥ ६०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarasvatyāḥ sūktīramṛtalaharīkauśalaharīḥ
pibantyāḥ śarvāṇi śravaṇaculukābhyāmaviralam .
camatkāraślāghācalitaśirasaḥ kuṇḍalagaṇo
jhaṇatkāraistāraiḥ prativacanamācaṣṭa iva te .. 60..
contentBlocks.translation: O consort of Śiva! Sarasvatī the Goddess of Learning, imbibing continuously through the cup of her ears Thy sweet speech that humbles the delectableness of the flood of the immortal drink Amṛta, replies congratulating Thee, as it were, by the loud clang of her ear-ornaments, as she shakes her head in appreciation of Thy delightful speech. 60.
contentBlocks.meanings: सरस्वत्याः (sarasvatyāḥ) – of Sarasvatī; सूक्तीः (sūktīḥ) – eloquent speeches; अमृतलहरी (amṛtalaharī) – wave of nectar (amṛta:nectar | laharī:wave); कौशलहरीः (kauśalaharīḥ) – waves of skill (kauśala:skill | harīḥ:waves); पिबन्त्याः (pibantyāḥ) – drinking; शर्वाणि (śarvāṇi) – O consort of Śiva; श्रवणचुलुकाभ्याम् (śravaṇaculukābhyām) – with the cups of ears (śravaṇa:ears | culukābhyām:with the cups); अविरलम् (aviralām) – continuously; चमत्कारश्लाघा (camatkāraślāghā) – praise of wonder (camatkāra:wonder | ślāghā:praise); चलितशिरसः (calitaśirasaḥ) – nodding head (calita:nodding | śirasaḥ:head); कुण्डलगणः (kuṇḍalagaṇaḥ) – group of earrings (kuṇḍala:earrings | gaṇaḥ:group); झणत्कारैः (jhaṇatkāraiḥ) – with jingling sounds (jhaṇat:jingling | kāraiḥ:with sounds); तारैः (tāraiḥ) – loud; प्रतिवचनम् (prativacanam) – reply; आचष्ट (ācaṣṭa) – speaks; इव (iva) – as if; ते (te) – your;

contentBlocks.original:
असौ नासावंशस्तुहिनगिरिवंशध्वजपटि
त्वदीयो नेदीयः फलतु फलमस्माकमुचितम् ।
वहन्नन्तर्मुक्ताः शिशिरतरनिश्वासगलितं
समृद्ध्या यत्तासां बहिरपि च मुक्तामणिधरः ॥ ६१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
asau nāsāvaṃśastuhinagirivaṃśadhvajapaṭi
tvadīyo nedīyaḥ phalatu phalamasmākamucitam .
vahannantarmuktāḥ śiśirataraniśvāsagalitaṃ
samṛddhyā yattāsāṃ bahirapi ca muktāmaṇidharaḥ .. 61..
contentBlocks.translation: O Flag of the House of the Mountain of Snow! May Thy nose, which is, as it were, the hollow bamboo staff of that flag, bestow on us the desired fruit. The hollow of that staff-like nose is full of pearls; for it is out of their abundance that one pearl, pushed out by the moon-cooled breath of the left nostril, has come out to be Thy nasal pendant. 61.
contentBlocks.meanings: असौ (asau) – this; नासावंशः (nāsāvaṃśaḥ) – nose-like bamboo staff (nāsā:nose | vaṃśaḥ:bamboo staff); तुहिनगिरिवंशध्वजपटि (tuhinagirivaṃśadhvajapaṭi) – Flag of the Mountain of Snow (tuhina:snow | giri:mountain | vaṃśa:lineage | dhvaja:flag | paṭi:cloth); त्वदीयः (tvadīyaḥ) – thy; नेदीयः (nedīyaḥ) – nearer; फलतु (phalatu) – may bestow; फलम् (phalam) – fruit; अस्माकम् (asmākam) – our; उचितम् (ucitam) – desired; वहन् (vahan) – carrying; अन्तः (antaḥ) – inside; मुक्ताः (muktāḥ) – pearls; शिशिरतर (śiśiratar) – cooler; निश्वास (niśvāsa) – breath; गलितम् (galitam) – fallen; समृद्ध्या (samṛddhyā) – abundance; यत् (yat) – which; तासाम् (tāsām) – of them; बहि: (bahiḥ) – outside; अपि (api) – also; च (ca) – and; मुक्तामणिधरः (muktāmaṇidharaḥ) – wearing a pearl pendant (muktā:pearl | maṇi:jewel | dharaḥ:wearing);

contentBlocks.original:
प्रकृत्या रक्तायास्तव सुदति दन्तच्छदरुचेः
प्रवक्ष्ये सादृश्यं जनयतु फलं विद्रुमलता ।
न बिम्बं तद्बिम्बप्रतिफलनरागादरुणितं
तुलामध्यारोढुं कथमिव विलज्जेत कलया ॥ ६२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prakṛtyā raktāyāstava sudati dantacchadaruceḥ
pravakṣye sādṛśyaṃ janayatu phalaṃ vidrumalatā .
na bimbaṃ tadbimbapratiphalanarāgādaruṇitaṃ
tulāmadhyāroḍhuṃ kathamiva vilajjeta kalayā .. 62..
contentBlocks.translation: O Devi with attractive rows of teeth! I shall now try to find out an object of comparison to describe Thy naturally red lips. But I am afraid I shall have to wait for this until the coral creeper (red all over) produces a fruit (which one can expect to be more red than the creeper itself). But is there not the Bimba fruit as a fitting object of comparison? No; for how will Bimba fruit not feel ashamed to stand before those lips, a mere reflection of which has made that fruit red! 62.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रकृत्या (prakṛtyā) – by nature; रक्तायाः (raktāyāḥ) – red; तव (tava) – your; सुदति (sudati) – O beautiful-toothed; दन्तच्छदरुचेः (dantacchadaruceḥ) – of the lips' beauty (danta:teeth | chada:covering | ruceḥ:of beauty); प्रवक्ष्ये (pravakṣye) – I shall declare; सादृश्यम् (sādṛśyam) – similarity; जनयतु (janayatu) – let it produce; फलं (phalaṃ) – fruit; विद्रुमलता (vidrumalatā) – coral creeper (vidruma:coral | latā:creeper); न (na) – not; बिम्बं (bimbaṃ) – Bimba fruit; तद्बिम्बप्रतिफलनरागात् (tadbimbapratiphalanarāgāt) – from reflection's redness (tad:that | bimba:reflection | pratiphalana:reflection | rāgāt:from redness); अरुणितं (aruṇitaṃ) – reddened; तुलाम् (tulām) – comparison; अध्यारोढुं (adhyāroḍhuṃ) – to ascend; कथम् (katham) – how; इव (iva) – like; विलज्जेत (vilajjeta) – would feel ashamed; कलया (kalayā) – by a part;

contentBlocks.original:
स्मितज्योत्स्नाजालं तव वदनचन्द्रस्य पिबतां
चकोराणामासीदतिरसतया चञ्चुजडिमा ।
अतस्ते शीतांशोरमृतलहरीमम्लरुचयः
पिबन्ति स्वच्छन्दं निशि निशि भृशं काञ्जिकधिया ॥ ६३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
smitajyotsnājālaṃ tava vadanacandrasya pibatāṃ
cakorāṇāmāsīdatirasatayā cañcujaḍimā .
ataste śītāṃśoramṛtalaharīmamlarucayaḥ
pibanti svacchandaṃ niśi niśi bhṛśaṃ kāñjikadhiyā .. 63..
contentBlocks.translation: The Cakora bird has got its tongue insensitised because of the extreme satiety generated by drinking the luminous light of Thy smiling face. It is for this reason that every night it drinks to its fill the light of the ‘natural moon’ under the Impression that it is some sour gruel (that will relieve the insensitiveness of its tongue). 63.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्मितज्योत्स्नाजालं (smitajyotsnājālaṃ) – network of smiling moonlight (smita:smiling | jyotsnā:moonlight | jālaṃ:network); तव (tava) – your; वदनचन्द्रस्य (vadanacandrasya) – of the moon-like face (vadana:face | candra:moon | sya:of); पिबतां (pibatāṃ) – of those drinking; चकोराणाम् (cakorāṇām) – of the Cakora birds; आसीत् (āsīt) – was; अतिरसतया (atirasatayā) – due to extreme satiety (ati:extreme | rasatā:satiety | yā:due to); चञ्चुजडिमा (cañcujaḍimā) – insensitiveness of the tongue (cañcu:tongue | jaḍimā:insensitiveness); अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; ते (te) – they; शीतांशोः (śītāṃśoḥ) – of the cool-rayed moon (śīta:cool | aṃśoḥ:rayed); अमृतलहरीम् (amṛtalaharīm) – nectar waves (amṛta:nectar | laharīm:waves); अम्लरुचयः (amlarucayaḥ) – with sour taste (amla:sour | rucayaḥ:taste); पिबन्ति (pibanti) – drink; स्वच्छन्दं (svacchandaṃ) – freely; निशि (niśi) – at night; निशि (niśi) – at night; भृशं (bhṛśaṃ) – intensely; काञ्जिकधिया (kāñjikadhiyā) – thinking it sour gruel (kāñjika:sour gruel | dhiyā:thinking);

contentBlocks.original:
अविश्रान्तं पत्युर्गुणगणकथाम्रेडनजपा
जपापुष्पच्छाया तव जननि जिह्वा जयति सा ।
यदग्रासीनायाः स्फटिकदृषदच्छच्छविमयी
सरस्वत्या मूर्तिः परिणमति माणिक्यवपुषा ॥ ६४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aviśrāntaṃ patyurguṇagaṇakathāmreḍanajapā
japāpuṣpacchāyā tava janani jihvā jayati sā .
yadagrāsīnāyāḥ sphaṭikadṛṣadacchacchavimayī
sarasvatyā mūrtiḥ pariṇamati māṇikyavapuṣā .. 64..
contentBlocks.translation: Hail unto Thy tongue which defies the Japa (hibiscus) flowers in its redness and is constantly engaged in the Japa (mutterings) that give expression to the glories of Thy Consort! The ruddiness of that tongue is so intense that the goddess of speech Sarasvatī, who dwells on the tip of that tongue, gets her crystal-like white complexion changed into the colour of a ruby (noted for its reddish tinge). 64.
contentBlocks.meanings: अविश्रान्तं (aviśrāntaṃ) – unceasing; पत्युः (patyuḥ) – of the husband; गुणगणकथा (guṇagaṇakathā) – stories of virtues (guṇa:virtue | gaṇa:group | kathā:story); अम्रेडन (amreḍana) – repetition; जपा (japā) – muttering; जपापुष्पच्छाया (japāpuṣpacchāyā) – like hibiscus flower (japā:hibiscus | puṣpa:flower | chāyā:shade); तव (tava) – your; जननि (janani) – mother; जिह्वा (jihvā) – tongue; जयति (jayati) – conquers; सा (sā) – that; यत् (yat) – which; अग्रासीनायाः (agrāsīnāyāḥ) – seated at the tip (agra:tip | āsīnāyāḥ:seated); स्फटिकदृषदच्छच्छविमयी (sphaṭikadṛṣadacchacchavimayī) – crystal-like complexion (sphaṭika:crystal | dṛṣad:stone | accha:clear | chavi:complexion | mayī:consisting of); सरस्वत्या (sarasvatyā) – of Sarasvati; मूर्तिः (mūrtiḥ) – form; परिणमति (pariṇamati) – transforms; माणिक्यवपुषा (māṇikyavapuṣā) – into ruby form (māṇikya:ruby | vapuṣā:form);

contentBlocks.original:
रणे जित्वा दैत्यानपहृतशिरस्त्रैः कवचिभिर्-
निवृत्तैश्चण्डांशत्रिपुरहरनिर्माल्यविमुखैः ।
विशाखेन्द्रोपेन्द्रैः शशिविशदकर्पूरशकला
विलीयन्ते मातस्तव वदनताम्बूलकबलाः ॥ ६५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
raṇe jitvā daityānapahṛtaśirastraiḥ kavacibhir-
nivṛttais Caṇḍāmśa-Tripurahara-nirmālva-vimukhaiḥ .
viśākhendropendraiḥ śaśiviśadakarpūraśakalā
vilīyante mātastava vadanatāmbūlakabalāḥ .. 65..
contentBlocks.translation: Rejecting the leavings of Siva as the share of the devotee Caṇda, celestials like Kumāra, Indra and Viṣṇu, after their victory over the Asuras, come to Thee, with their head dress removed and only the mails on, to receive as Thy gracious gift(prasada) the betel rolls used by Thee, and chew them until they along with the white pieces of refined camphol contained in them dissolve. 65.
contentBlocks.meanings: रणे (raṇe) – in battle; जित्वा (jitvā) – having conquered; दैत्यान् (daityān) – demons; अपहृतशिरस्त्रैः (apahṛtaśirastraiḥ) – with removed headgear (apahṛta:removed | śirastraiḥ:headgear); कवचिभिः (kavacibhiḥ) – with armors; निवृत्तैः (nivṛttaiḥ) – returned; चण्डांशत्रिपुरहरनिर्माल्यविमुखैः (caṇḍāmśatripuraharanirmālyavimukhaiḥ) – averse to Śiva's leavings (caṇḍāmśa:Caṇḍa's share | tripurahara:Śiva, destroyer of Tripura | nirmālya:leavings | vimukhaiḥ:averse); विशाखेन्द्रोपेन्द्रैः (viśākhendropendraiḥ) – by Viśākha, Indra, Upendra (viśākha:Viśākha | indra:Indra | upendraiḥ:Upendra); शशिविशदकर्पूरशकला (śaśiviśadakarpūraśakalā) – moon-bright camphor pieces (śaśi:moon | viśada:bright | karpūra:camphor | śakalā:pieces); विलीयन्ते (vilīyante) – dissolve; मातः (mātaḥ) – O Mother; तव (tava) – your; वदनताम्बूलकबलाः (vadanatāmbūlakabalāḥ) – betel rolls from your mouth (vadana:mouth | tāmbūla:betel | kabalāḥ:rolls);

contentBlocks.original:
विपञ्च्या गायन्ती विविधमपदानं पशुपतेः
त्वयारब्धे वक्तुं चलितशिरसा साधुवचने ।
तदीयैर्माधुर्यैरपलपिततन्त्रीकलरवां
निजां वीणां वाणी निचुलयति चोलेन निभृतम् ॥ ६६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vipañcyā gāyantī vividhamapadānaṃ paśupateḥ
tvayārabdhe vaktuṃ calitaśirasā sādhuvacane .
tadīyairmādhuryairapalapitatantrīkalaravāṃ
nijāṃ vīṇāṃ vāṇī niculayati colena nibhṛtam .. 66..
contentBlocks.translation: When, on hearing the songs on the greatness and achievements of Siva tuned on the Vīna by Sarasvatī, the goddess of learning and fine arts, Thou beginnest to speak nodding Thy head in appreciation, the sweetness of Thy voice seems to cast ridicule on the soft melody of that musical instrument, and Sarasvatī therefore secretly puts it in its case. 66.
contentBlocks.meanings: विपञ्च्या (vipañcyā) – by the Vīna; गायन्ती (gāyantī) – singing; विविधम् (vividham) – various; अपदानं (apadānam) – achievements; पशुपतेः (paśupateḥ) – of Paśupati (Śiva); त्वया (tvayā) – by you; आरब्धे (ārabdhe) – when begun; वक्तुं (vaktuṃ) – to speak; चलितशिरसा (calitaśirasā) – with nodding head; साधुवचने (sādhuvacane) – in good words; तदीयैः (tadīyaiḥ) – by her; माधुर्यैः (mādhuryaiḥ) – by sweetness; अपलपिततन्त्रीकलरवां (apalapitatantrīkalaravāṃ) – ridiculed string sound (apalapita:ridiculed | tantrī:string | kalaravāṃ:sound); निजां (nijāṃ) – her own; वीणां (vīṇāṃ) – Vīna; वाणी (vāṇī) – Sarasvatī; निचुलयति (niculayati) – puts away; चोलेन (colena) – with a cover; निभृतम् (nibhṛtam) – secretly;

contentBlocks.original:
कराग्रेण स्पृष्टं तुहिनगिरिणा वत्सलतया
गिरीशेनोदस्तं मुहुरधरपानाकुलतया ।
करग्राह्यं शम्भोर्मुखमुकुरवृन्तं गिरिसुते
कथङ्कारं ब्रूमस्तव चिबुकमौपम्यरहितम् ॥ ६७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karāgreṇa spṛṣṭaṃ tuhinagiriṇā vatsalatayā
girīśenodastaṃ muhuradharapānākulatayā .
karagrāhyaṃ śambhormukhamukuravṛntaṃ girisute
kathaṅkāraṃ brūmastava cibukamaupamyarahitam .. 67..
contentBlocks.translation: O daughter of Himavat! How can we poets describe the unmatched beauty of Thy chin, which is stroked with all affection by Thy father the Mountain King, which is again and again lifted up by Thy Consort Śambhu in the intensity of his desire to kiss Thee repeatedly, and which forms the handle of the mirror of Thy face for Śambhu to hold and view. 67.
contentBlocks.meanings: कराग्रेण (karāgreṇa) – by the tip of the hand (kara:hand | agreṇa:by the tip); स्पृष्टं (spṛṣṭam) – touched; तिहिनगिरिणा (tuhinagiriṇā) – by the Mountain King (tuhina:snow | giriṇā:by the mountain); वत्सलतया (vatsalatayā) – with affection; गिरीशेन (girīśena) – by Śambhu (giri:mountain | īśena:by the lord); उदस्तं (udastam) – lifted up; मुहुः (muhuḥ) – again and again; अधरपानाकुलतया (adharapānākulatayā) – in desire to kiss (adhara:lip | pāna:drinking | ākulatayā:with desire); करग्राह्यम् (karagrāhyam) – held by hand (kara:hand | grāhyam:held); शम्भोः (śambhoḥ) – of Śambhu; मुखमुकुरवृन्तम् (mukhamukuravṛntam) – handle of face mirror (mukha:face | mukura:mirror | vṛntam:handle); गिरिसुते (girisute) – O daughter of the mountain (giri:mountain | sute:daughter); कथङ्कारम् (kathaṅkāram) – how; ब्रूमः (brūmaḥ) – can we describe; तव (tava) – your; चिबुकम् (cibukam) – chin; औपम्यरहितम् (aupamyarahitam) – without comparison (aupamya:comparison | rahitam:without);

contentBlocks.original:
भुजाश्लेषान् नित्यं पुरदमयितुः कण्टकवती
तव ग्रीवा धत्ते मुखकमलनालश्रियमियम् ।
स्वतः श्वेता कालागुरुबहुलजम्बालमलिना
मृणालीलालित्यम् वहति यदधो हारलतिका ॥ ६८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhujāśleṣān nityaṃ puradamayituḥ kaṇṭakavatī
tava grīvā dhatte mukhakamalanālaśriyamiyam .
svataḥ śvetā kālāgurubahulajambālamalinā
mṛṇālīlālityam vahati yadadho hāralatikā .. 68..
contentBlocks.translation: Thy neck, with horripilations caused by the constant embraces of Thy Consort, the Destroyer of the Cities, resembles the thorny stalk of Thy lotus-like face. For, below Thy neck are Thy naturally white cluster of pearl necklaces, which, being discoloured by the generously applied dark-coloured Agaru paste on the chest, bears the comeliness of the tender bottom part of the stalk of Thy lotus-like face embedded in mire. 68.
contentBlocks.meanings: भुजाश्लेषान् (bhujāśleṣān) – embraces of the arms (bhuja:arms | āśleṣān:embraces); नित्यं (nityaṃ) – constantly; पुरदमयितुः (puradamayituḥ) – Destroyer of the Cities (pura:cities | damayituḥ:destroyer); कण्टकवती (kaṇṭakavatī) – thorny; तव (tava) – your; ग्रीवा (grīvā) – neck; धत्ते (dhatte) – bears; मुखकमलनालश्रियम् (mukhakamalanālaśriyam) – beauty of lotus-stalk face (mukha:face | kamala:lotus | nāla:stalk | śriyam:beauty); इयम् (iyam) – this; स्वतः (svataḥ) – naturally; श्वेता (śvetā) – white; कालागुरुबहुलजम्बालमलिना (kālāgurubahulajambālamalinā) – dark Agaru paste discoloured (kālāguru:dark Agaru | bahula:abundant | jambāla:paste | malinā:discoloured); मृणालीलालित्यम् (mṛṇālīlālityam) – tenderness of lotus-stalk (mṛṇālī:lotus-stalk | lālityam:tenderness); वहति (vahati) – carries; यदधः (yadadho) – below which (yat:which | adhaḥ:below); हारलतिका (hāralatikā) – cluster of pearl necklaces (hāra:necklace | latikā:cluster);

contentBlocks.original:
गले रेखास्तिस्रो गतिगमकगीतैकनिपुणे
विवाहव्यानद्धप्रगुणगुणसंख्याप्रतिभुवः ।
विराजन्ते नानाविधमधुररागाकरभुवां
त्रयाणां ग्रामाणां स्थितिनियमसीमान इव ते ॥ ६९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gale rekhāstisro gatigamakagītaikanipuṇe
vivāha-vyānaddha-praguṇa-guṇa-samkhyā-pratibhuvaḥ .
virājante nānāvidhamadhurarāgākarabhuvāṃ
trayāṇāṃ grāmāṇāṃ sthitiniyamasīmāna iva te .. 69..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mistress of the musical technicalities of Gati (procedure)Gamakam (undulations) and Gītam (song)! As though for the confirmation of the three-foldness of the strands of the auspicious string made by twisting several threads and tied round Thy neck (surrounding the mangala-sutra) by Thy Consort at the time of Thy marriage, shine forth the three lines of Thy neck, standing, as it were, as the boundaries demarcating the three musical scales (or Gamakas consisting of Saḍja, Madhyama, and Gāndhāra), which form the source of the various melodies of musical modes. 69.
contentBlocks.meanings: गले (gale) – in the neck; रेखाः (rekhāḥ) – lines; तिस्रः (tisraḥ) – three; गतिगमकगीतैकनिपुणे (gatigamakagītaikanipuṇe) – expert in movement, modulation, and song (gati:movement | gamaka:modulation | gīta:song | eka:one | nipuṇe:expert); विवाहव्यानद्धप्रगुणगुणसंख्याप्रतिभुवः (vivāhavyānaddhapraguṇaguṇasaṃkhyāpratibhuvaḥ) – confirming auspicious thread's threefoldness (vivāha:marriage | vyānaddha:tied | praguṇa:auspicious thread | guṇa:strand | saṃkhyā:number | pratibhuvaḥ:confirming); विराजन्ते (virājante) – shine forth; नानाविधमधुररागाकरभुवाम् (nānāvidhamadhurarāgākarabhuvām) – source of various sweet melodies (nānāvidha:various | madhura:sweet | rāga:melody | ākara:source | bhuvām:of the worlds); त्रयाणाम् (trayāṇām) – of the three; ग्रामाणाम् (grāmāṇām) – of the musical scales; स्थितिनियमसीमानः (sthitiniyamasīmānaḥ) – boundaries of regulation (sthiti:position | niyama:regulation | sīmānaḥ:boundaries); इव (iva) – as if; ते (te) – your;

contentBlocks.original:
मृणालीमृद्वीनां तव भुजलतानां चतसृणां
चतुर्भिः सौन्दर्यं सरसिजभवः स्तौति वदनैः ।
नखेभ्यः सन्त्रस्यन् प्रथममथनादन्धकरिपो-
श्चतुर्णां शीर्षाणां सममभयहस्तार्पणधिया ॥ ७०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛṇālīmṛdvīnāṃ tava bhujalatānāṃ catasṛṇāṃ
caturbhiḥ saundaryaṃ sarasijabhavaḥ stauti vadanaiḥ .
nakhebhyaḥ santrasyan prathamamathanādandhakaripo-
ścaturṇāṃ śīrṣāṇāṃ samamabhayahastārpaṇadhiyā .. 70..
contentBlocks.translation: The lotus-born Brahmā, being afraid of the fingernails of Sadāśiva, with which the latter in former times nipped off his fifth head, is now praising simultaneously with the remaining four heads the comeliness of Thy four creeper-like arms resembling the stalk of lotus in beauty, so that Thou might give those heads protection from the fear of a fate similar to that of the lost head. 70.
contentBlocks.meanings: मृणालीमृद्वीनां (mṛṇālīmṛdvīnāṃ) – of soft as lotus stalk (mṛṇālī:lotus stalk | mṛdvī:soft); तव (tava) – your; भुजलतानां (bhujalatānāṃ) – of the creeper-like arms (bhuja:arm | latānāṃ:of the creepers); चतसृणां (catasṛṇāṃ) – of the four; चतुर्भिः (caturbhiḥ) – by the four; सौन्दर्यं (saundaryaṃ) – beauty; सरसिजभवः (sarasijabhavaḥ) – lotus-born (Brahmā) (sarasija:lotus | bhavaḥ:born); स्तौति (stauti) – praises; वदनैः (vadanaiḥ) – with faces; नखेभ्यः (nakhebhyaḥ) – from the nails; सन्त्रस्यन् (santrasyan) – being afraid; प्रथममथनात् (prathamamathanāt) – from the first churning (prathama:first | mathanāt:from churning); अन्धकरिपोः (andhakaripoḥ) – of the enemy of darkness (andhaka:darkness | ripoḥ:of the enemy); चतुर्णां (caturṇāṃ) – of the four; शीर्षाणां (śīrṣāṇāṃ) – of the heads; समम् (samam) – equally; अभयहस्तार्पणधिया (abhayahastārpaṇadhiyā) – with the thought of granting protection (abhaya:protection | hasta:hand | arpaṇa:granting | dhiyā:with the thought);

contentBlocks.original:
नखानामुद्द्योतैर्नवनलिनरागं विहसतां
कराणां ते कान्तिं कथय कथयामः कथमुमे ।
कयाचिद्वा साम्यं भजतु कलया हन्त कमलं
यदि क्रीडल्लक्ष्मीचरणतललाक्षारसछणम् ॥ ७१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nakhānāmuddyotairnavanalinarāgaṃ vihasatāṃ
karāṇāṃ te kāntiṃ kathaya kathayāmaḥ kathamume .
kayācidvā sāmyaṃ bhajatu kalayā hanta kamalaṃ
yadi krīḍal-lakṣmī-caraṇa-tala-lākṣā-rasa-caṇam .. 71..
contentBlocks.translation: O Uma! Pray, tell us how we can describe the splendour of Thy hands, which, being lit up with the radiance of Thy nails, surpasses the brightness of the morning’s lotus blooms. Perhaps these flowers can attain to some similarity with Thy nails when their redness is enhanced by contact with the lac-dye of the feet of goddess Lakṣmī who sports in them. 71.
contentBlocks.meanings: नखानाम् (nakhānām) – of the nails; उद्द्योतैः (uddyotaiḥ) – by the radiance; नव (nava) – new; नलिन (nalina) – lotus; रागं (rāgaṃ) – color; विहसतां (vihasatāṃ) – surpassing; कराणां (karāṇāṃ) – of the hands; ते (te) – your; कान्तिं (kāntiṃ) – splendour; कथय (kathaya) – tell; कथयामः (kathayāmaḥ) – we describe; कथम् (katham) – how; उमे (ume) – O Uma; कयाचित् (kayācit) – by some; वा (vā) – or; साम्यं (sāmyaṃ) – similarity; भजतु (bhajatu) – attain; कलया (kalayā) – with the art; हन्त (hanta) – alas; कमलं (kamalaṃ) – lotus; यदि (yadi) – if; क्रीडत् (krīḍat) – sporting; लक्ष्मी (lakṣmī) – Lakṣmī; चरण (caraṇa) – feet; तल (tala) – sole; लाक्षा (lākṣā) – lac-dye; रस (rasa) – juice; छणम् (chaṇam) – enhanced;

contentBlocks.original:
समं देवि स्कन्दद्विपवदनपीतं स्तनयुगं
तवेदं नः खेदं हरतु सततं प्रस्नुतमुखम् ।
यदालोक्याशङ्काकुलितहृदयो हासजनकः
स्वकुम्भौ हेरम्बः परिमृशति हस्तेन झडिति ॥ ७२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samaṃ devi skandadvipavadanapītaṃ stanayugaṃ
tavedaṃ naḥ khedaṃ haratu satataṃ prasnutamukham .
yadālokyāśaṅkākulitahṛdayo hāsajanakaḥ
svakumbhau herambaḥ parimṛśati hastena jhaḍiti .. 72..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! May we be relieved of all our sorrows by Thy breasts, from which milk is flowing always and which are being simultaneously sucked by both Skanda and Gaṇeśa, Thy sons. All of a sudden Gaṇesa (Heramba), looking at Thy breasts, feels with his hands whether the pair of frontal globes on his elephant face are there in their proper places (or whether they have disappeared to become Thy breasts present before his eyes), thus giving occasion for great fun (to his parents and onlookers). 72.
contentBlocks.meanings: समं (samaṃ) – equally; देवि (devi) – O Goddess; स्कन्दद्विपवदनपीतं (skandadvipavadanapītaṃ) – sucked by Skanda and Gaṇeśa (skanda:Skanda | dvipa:elephant | vadana:face | pītaṃ:sucked); स्तनयुगं (stanayugaṃ) – pair of breasts (stana:breast | yugaṃ:pair); तवेदं (tavedaṃ) – this of yours (tava:your | idaṃ:this); नः (naḥ) – our; खेदं (khedaṃ) – sorrow; हरतु (haratu) – may remove; सततं (satataṃ) – always; प्रस्नुतमुखम् (prasnutamukham) – flowing milk (prasnuta:flowing | mukham:mouth); यदालोक्य (yadālokya) – having seen; आशङ्काकुलितहृदयः (āśaṅkākulitahṛdayaḥ) – with doubtful heart (āśaṅkā:doubt | ākulita:agitated | hṛdayaḥ:heart); हासजनकः (hāsajanakaḥ) – causing laughter (hāsa:laughter | janakaḥ:causing); स्वकुम्भौ (svakumbhau) – his own cheeks (sva:own | kumbhau:cheeks); हेरम्बः (herambaḥ) – Heramba (Gaṇeśa); परिमृशति (parimṛśati) – feels; हस्तेन (hastena) – with hand; झडिति (jhaḍiti) – suddenly;

contentBlocks.original:
अमू ते वक्षोजावमृतरसमाणिक्यकुतुपौ
न संदेहस्पन्दो नगपतिपताके मनसि नः ।
पिबन्तौ तौ यस्मादविदितवधूसङ्गरसिकौ
कुमारावद्यापि द्विरदवदनक्रौञ्चदलनौ ॥ ७३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amū te vakṣojāvamṛtarasamāṇikyakutupau
na saṃdehaspando nagapatipatāke manasi naḥ .
pibantau tau yasmādaviditavadhūsaṅgarasikau
kumārāvadyāpi dviradavadanakrauñcadalanau .. 73..
contentBlocks.translation: O the Mountain-King’s flag of victory! We have not even the shadow of a doubt about Thy two breasts being jars made of Māṇikya (ruby) and filled with Amṛta, the immortal drink. For, it is by drinking their contents that Thy two sons, the mountain-piercing Kumāra and the elephant-headed Vināyaka, have remained young boys (celibates) without knowing woman, to this day. 73.
contentBlocks.meanings: अमू (amū) – those; ते (te) – your; वक्षोजौ (vakṣojau) – breasts; अमृतरसमाणिक्यकुतुपौ (amṛtarasamāṇikyakutupau) – jars of nectar and ruby (amṛta:nectar | rasa:essence | māṇikya:ruby | kutupau:jars); न (na) – not; सन्देहस्पन्दः (sandehaspandaḥ) – doubt arises (sandeha:doubt | spandaḥ:arises); नगपतिपताके (nagapatipatāke) – O Mountain-King's flag (naga:mountain | pati:king | patāke:flag); मनसि (manasi) – in the mind; नः (naḥ) – our; पिबन्तौ (pibantau) – drinking; तौ (tau) – those two; यस्मात् (yasmāt) – because; अविदितवधूसङ्गरसिकौ (aviditavadhūsaṅgarasikau) – unacquainted with women (avidita:unacquainted | vadhū:women | saṅga:association | rasikau:enjoyers); कुमारौ (kumārau) – young boys; अद्यापि (adyāpi) – even today; द्विरदवदन (dviradavadana) – elephant-headed (dvirada:elephant | vadana:headed); क्रौञ्चदलनौ (krauñcadalanau) – mountain-piercing (krauñca:mountain | dalanau:piercing);

contentBlocks.original:
वहत्यम्ब स्तम्बेरमदनुजकुम्भप्रकृतिभिः
समारब्धां मुक्तामणिभिरमलां हारलतिकाम् ।
कुचाभोगो बिम्बाधररुचिभिरन्तः शबलितां
प्रतापव्यामिश्रां पुरदमयितुः कीर्तिमिव ते ॥ ७४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vahatyamba stamberamadanujakumbhaprakṛtibhiḥ
samārabdhāṃ muktāmaṇibhiramalāṃ hāralatikām .
kucābhogo bimbādhararucibhirantaḥ śabalitāṃ
pratāpavyāmiśrāṃ puradamayituḥ kīrtimiva te .. 74..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! In the region of Thy breasts is a flawless necklace made of pearls got from the frontal globe of Gajāsura (the elephant-demon). It rests there with its white brilliance variegated from within by the reflection of the ruddy tinge of Thy Bimba-like lips, as if it were the confluence of the (white) fame and the (red) valour of Thy Consort, the Destroyer of the Cities. 74.
contentBlocks.meanings: वहत्यम्ब (vahatyamba) – O Mother carries (vahati:carries | amba:O Mother); स्तम्बे (stambe) – in the region; अरमदनुजकुम्भप्रकृतिभिः (aramadanujakumbhaprakṛtibhiḥ) – from the frontal globe (aramada:elephant-demon | anuja:younger brother | kumbha:frontal globe | prakṛtibhiḥ:by nature); समारब्धां (samārabdhām) – initiated; मुक्तामणिभिः (muktāmaṇibhiḥ) – with pearls (muktā:pearls | maṇibhiḥ:with jewels); अमलां (amalām) – flawless; हारलतिकाम् (hāralatikām) – necklace (hāra:necklace | latikām:creeper); कुचाभोगो (kucābhogaḥ) – region of breasts (kuca:breasts | ābhogaḥ:region); बिम्बाधररुचिभिः (bimbādhararucibhiḥ) – by the reflection of lips (bimba:bimba-like | adhara:lips | rucibhiḥ:by the reflection); अन्तः (antaḥ) – within; शबलितां (śabalitām) – variegated; प्रतापव्यामिश्रां (pratāpavyāmiśrām) – mixed with valour (pratāpa:valour | vyāmiśrām:mixed with); पुरदमयितुः (puradamayituḥ) – of the Destroyer of Cities (pura:cities | damayituḥ:destroyer); कीर्तिमिव (kīrtimiva) – like fame (kīrtim:fame | iva:like); ते (te) – of you;

contentBlocks.original:
तव स्तन्यं मन्ये धरणिधरकन्ये हृदयतः
पयःपारावारः परिवहति सारस्वतमिव ।
दयावत्या दत्तं द्रविडशिशुरास्वाद्य तव यत्
कवीनां प्रौढानामजनि कमनीयः कवयिता ॥ ७५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava stanyaṃ manye dharaṇidharakanye hṛdayataḥ
payaḥpārāvāraḥ parivahati sārasvatamiva .
dayāvatyā dattaṃ draviḍaśiśurāsvādya tava yat
kavīnāṃ prauḍhānāmajani kamanīyaḥ kavayitā .. 75..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Mountain-King! I fancy that Thy breast milk is the milk-ocean emerging as poetic inspiration from Thy heart. For, it was by drinking it, so graciously given by Thee, that the child of the Dramila country became a noted poet among great composers. 75.
contentBlocks.meanings: तव (tava) – your; स्तन्यं (stanyaṃ) – breast milk; मन्ये (manye) – I fancy; धरणिधरकन्ये (dharaṇidharakanye) – O Daughter of the Mountain-King (dharaṇi:earth | dhara:holder | kanye:daughter); हृदयतः (hṛdayataḥ) – from the heart; पयःपारावारः (payaḥpārāvāraḥ) – milk-ocean (payaḥ:milk | pārāvāraḥ:ocean); परिवहति (parivahati) – flows; सारस्वतमिव (sārasvatamiva) – like poetic inspiration (sārasvatam:poetic inspiration | iva:like); दयावत्या (dayāvatyā) – by the gracious; दत्तं (dattaṃ) – given; द्रविडशिशुः (draviḍaśiśuḥ) – child of the Dramila country (draviḍa:Dramila | śiśuḥ:child); आस्वाद्य (āsvādya) – having tasted; तव (tava) – your; यत् (yat) – which; कवीनां (kavīnāṃ) – of poets; प्रौढानाम् (prauḍhānām) – among great; अजनि (ajani) – became; कमनीयः (kamanīyaḥ) – noted; कवयिता (kavayitā) – poet;

contentBlocks.original:
हरक्रोधज्वालावलिभिरवलीढेन वपुषा
गभीरे ते नाभीसरसि कृतसङ्गो मनसिजः ।
समुत्तस्थौ तस्मादचलतनये धूमलतिका
जनस्तां जानीते तव जननि रोमावलिरिति ॥ ७६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
harakrodhajvālāvalibhiravalīḍhena vapuṣā
gabhīre te nābhīsarasi kṛtasaṅgo manasijaḥ .
samuttasthau tasmādacalatanaye dhūmalatikā
janastāṃ jānīte tava janani romāvaliriti .. 76..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother born of the Mountain! When the flames of Siva’s anger began to envelop Kāma-deva (Cupid), he took refuge in the deep lake of Thy navel. The fire that thus got extinguished sent forth a thin creeper-like column of smoke, which men describe as Thy Romāvali or the line of hair that goes up from Thy navel. 76.
contentBlocks.meanings: हर (hara) – Siva; क्रोध (krodha) – anger; ज्वाला (jvālā) – flames; वलिभिः (valibhiḥ) – by the series; अवलीढेन (avalīḍhena) – enveloped; वपुषा (vapuṣā) – by the body; गभीरे (gabhīre) – deep; ते (te) – your; नाभीसरसि (nābhīsarasi) – in the lake of navel (nābhī:navel | sarasi:in the lake); कृतसङ्गः (kṛtasaṅgaḥ) – took refuge; मनसिजः (manasijaḥ) – Kāma-deva; समुत्तस्थौ (samuttasthau) – arose; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – from there; अचलतनये (acalatanaye) – O daughter of the mountain (acala:mountain | tanaye:daughter); धूमलतिका (dhūmalatikā) – smoke creeper (dhūma:smoke | latikā:creeper); जनः (janaḥ) – people; ताम् (tām) – that; जानीते (jānīte) – know; तव (tava) – your; जननि (janani) – O Mother; रोमावलिः (romāvaliḥ) – line of hair (roma:hair | avaliḥ:line); इति (iti) – thus;

contentBlocks.original:
यदेतत् कालिन्दीतनुतरतरङ्गाकृति शिवे
कृशे मध्ये किंचिज्जननि तव यद्भाति सुधियाम् ।
विमर्दादन्योऽन्यं कुचकलशयोरन्तरगतं
तनूभूतं व्योम प्रविशदिव नाभिं कुहरिणीम् ॥ ७७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadetat kālindītanutarataraṅgākṛti śive
kṛśe madhye kiṃcijjanani tava yadbhāti sudhiyām .
vimardādanyo'nyaṃ kucakalaśayorantaragataṃ
tanūbhūtaṃ vyoma praviśadiva nābhiṃ kuhariṇīm .. 77..
contentBlocks.translation: O Consort of Siva! What in Thy slender middle region shines before me and what is seen by devotees as Thy navel-hair (Romāvali) like the subtle ripples on the surface of Kālindī (Yamuna), is the Vyoma (sky) or interspace between Thy bulging breasts, thinned by their pressure and forced into the cavity of the navel. 77.
contentBlocks.meanings: यदेतत् (yadetat) – this which; कालिन्दीतनुतरतरङ्गाकृति (kālindī-tanutara-taraṅga-ākṛti) – like subtle Yamuna ripples (kālindī:Yamuna | tanutara:subtle | taraṅga:ripples | ākṛti:like); शिवे (śive) – O Consort of Siva; कृशे (kṛśe) – slender; मध्ये (madhye) – in the middle; किंचित् (kiṃcit) – slightly; जननि (janani) – O Mother; तव (tava) – your; यद्भाति (yadbhāti) – which shines; सुधियाम् (sudhiyām) – by the wise; विमर्दात् (vimardāt) – by pressure; अन्यः (anyaḥ) – another; अन्यं (anyaṃ) – another; कुचकलशयोः (kucakalaśayoḥ) – of the breasts (kuca:breast | kalaśayoḥ:of the pair); अन्तरगतं (antaragataṃ) – entered between; तनूभूतं (tanūbhūtaṃ) – thinned; व्योम (vyoma) – sky; प्रविशदिव (praviśadiva) – as if entering; नाभिं (nābhiṃ) – navel; कुहरिणीम् (kuhariṇīm) – cavity;

contentBlocks.original:
स्थिरो गङ्गावर्तः स्तनमुकुलरोमावलिलता-
कलावालं कुण्डं कुसुमशरतेजोहुतभुजः ।
रतेर्लीलागारं किमपि तव नाभिर्गिरिसुते
बिलद्वारं सिद्धेर्गिरिशनयनानां विजयते ॥ ७८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sthiro gaṅgāvartaḥ stanamukularomāvalilatā-
kalāvālaṃ kuṇḍaṃ kusumaśaratejohutabhujaḥ .
raterlīlāgāraṃ kimapi tava nābhirgirisute
biladvāraṃ siddhergiriśanayanānāṃ vijayate .. 78..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Mountain! Indescribably unique is the glory of Thy navel, which is verily a still whirl-pool on the surface of the river Gaṅga; which forms the bed for the creeper of Thy Romāvali bearing two fruits in the shape of Thy breasts; which is the sacrificial pit wherein burns the fire of the prowess of Kama Deva, the flower-arrowed god of love; which is the pleasure house of his wife Rati; and which forms the opening of the cavern wherein the eyes of Hara found the consummation of the great austerities he had performed. 78.
contentBlocks.meanings: स्थिरः (sthiraḥ) – steady; गङ्गावर्तः (gaṅgāvartaḥ) – whirlpool of Gaṅgā (gaṅgā:Gaṅgā | āvartaḥ:whirlpool); स्तनमुकुलरोमावलिलता (stanamukularomāvalilatā) – creeper of breast-bud hairline (stana:breast | mukula:bud | romāvali:hairline | latā:creeper); कलावालं (kalāvālaṃ) – bed for creeper (kalā:creeper | vālaṃ:bed); कुण्डं (kuṇḍaṃ) – sacrificial pit; कुसुमशरतेजोहुतभुजः (kusumaśaratejohutabhujaḥ) – fire of flower-arrowed god (kusuma:flower | śara:arrow | tejaḥ:fire | hutabhujaḥ:god); रतेः (rateḥ) – of Rati; लीलागारं (līlāgāraṃ) – pleasure house (līlā:pleasure | gāraṃ:house); किमपि (kimapi) – indescribably unique; तव (tava) – your; नाभिः (nābhiḥ) – navel; गिरिसुते (girisute) – O Daughter of the Mountain (giri:mountain | sute:daughter); बिलद्वारं (biladvāraṃ) – opening of the cavern (bila:cavern | dvāraṃ:opening); सिद्धेः (siddheḥ) – of accomplishment; गिरिशनयनानां (giriśanayanānāṃ) – eyes of Hara (giriśa:Hara | nayanānāṃ:eyes); विजयते (vijayate) – triumphs;

contentBlocks.original:
निसर्गक्षीणस्य स्तनतटभरेण क्लमजुषो
नमन्मूर्तेर्नारीतिलक शनकैस्त्रुट्यत इव ।
चिरं ते मध्यस्य त्रुटिततटिनीतीरतरुणा
समावस्थास्थेम्नो भवतु कुशलं शैलतनये ॥ ७९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nisargakṣīṇasya stanataṭabhareṇa klamajuṣo
namanmūrternārītilaka śanakaistruṭyata iva .
ciraṃ te madhyasya truṭitataṭinītīrataruṇā
samāvasthāsthemno bhavatu kuśalaṃ śailatanaye .. 79..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Mountain! O Ornament of womankind! May safety be assured for Thy waist, which, being slender, is labouring under the weight of Thy breasts and therefore stooping, threatening to break under that weight, and whose precarious firmness is like that of a tree on a cracking river bank. 79.
contentBlocks.meanings: निसर्गक्षीणस्य (nisargakṣīṇasya) – naturally slender (nisarga:natural | kṣīṇasya:slender); स्तनतटभरेण (stanataṭabhareṇa) – by the weight of breasts (stana:breast | taṭa:surface | bhareṇa:by the weight); क्लमजुषः (klamajuṣaḥ) – wearied; नमन्मूर्तेः (namanmūrteḥ) – of the bending form (naman:bending | mūrteḥ:of the form); नारीतिलक (nārītilaka) – ornament of womankind (nārī:woman | tilaka:ornament); शनकैः (śanakaiḥ) – slowly; त्रुट्यत (truṭyata) – breaking; इव (iva) – as if; चिरं (ciraṃ) – long; ते (te) – your; मध्यस्य (madhyasya) – of the waist; त्रुटिततटिनीतीरतरुणा (truṭitataṭinītīrataruṇā) – like a tree on a cracking river bank (truṭita:cracking | taṭinī:river | tīra:bank | taruṇā:tree); समावस्था (samāvasthā) – state; स्थेम्नः (sthemnaḥ) – firmness; भवतु (bhavatu) – may be; कुशलं (kuśalaṃ) – well-being; शैलतनये (śailatanaye) – O Daughter of the Mountain (śaila:mountain | tanaye:daughter);

contentBlocks.original:
कुचौ सद्यःस्विद्यत्तटघटितकूर्पासभिदुरौ
कषन्तौ दोर्मूले कनककलशाभौ कलयता ।
तव त्रातुं भङ्गादलमिति वलग्नं तनुभुवा
त्रिधा नद्धं देवि त्रिवलि लवलीवल्लिभिरिव ॥ ८०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kucau sadyaḥsvidyattaṭaghaṭitakūrpāsabhidurau
kaṣantau dormūle kanakakalaśābhau kalayatā .
tava trātuṃ bhaṅgādalamiti valagnaṃ tanubhuvā
tridhā naddhaṃ devi trivali lavalīvallibhiriva .. 80..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother Divine! The three folds in Thy middle region look like three strands of Lavali creeper wound by Kama Deva, the god of love, as a support to prevent Thy middle region from breaking under the weight of his creation, Thy quickly perspiring breasts, which (under the excitement of love for Śambhu) have swollen to the size of two golden pots, touching Thy arm pits and bursting the garment covering them. 80.
contentBlocks.meanings: कुचौ (kucau) – breasts; सद्यः (sadyaḥ) – immediately; स्विद्यत् (svidyat) – perspiring; तट (taṭa) – region; घटित (ghaṭita) – formed; कूर्पास (kūrpāsa) – garment; भिदुरौ (bhidurau) – bursting; कषन्तौ (kaṣantau) – touching; दोर्मूले (dormūle) – at the armpits; कनक (kanaka) – golden; कलशाभौ (kalaśābhau) – like pots; कलयता (kalayatā) – considering; तव (tava) – your; त्रातुं (trātuṃ) – to protect; भङ्गात् (bhaṅgāt) – from breaking; अलम् (alam) – enough; इति (iti) – thus; वलग्नं (valagnaṃ) – attached; तनुभुवा (tanubhuvā) – by the body; त्रिधा (tridhā) – threefold; नद्धं (naddhaṃ) – bound; देवि (devi) – O Goddess; त्रिवलि (trivali) – three folds (tri:three | vali:folds); लवलीवल्लिभिः (lavalīvallibhiḥ) – by Lavali creepers (lavalī:Lavali | vallibhiḥ:by creepers); इव (iva) – like;

contentBlocks.original:
गुरुत्वं विस्तारं क्षितिधरपतिः पार्वति निजा-
न्नितम्बादाच्छिद्य त्वयि हरणरूपेण निदधे ।
अतस्ते विस्तीर्णो गुरुरयमशेषां वसुमतीं
नितम्बप्राग्भारः स्थगयति लघुत्वं नयति च ॥ ८१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gurutvaṃ vistāraṃ kṣitidharapatiḥ pārvati nijā-
nnitambādācchidya tvayi haraṇarūpeṇa nidadhe .
ataste vistīrṇo gururayamaśeṣāṃ vasumatīṃ
nitambaprāgbhāraḥ sthagayati laghutvaṃ nayati ca .. 81..
contentBlocks.translation: O Parvati! Thy father, the Mountain-king, gave the weight and extensiveness of his flanks to Thee as dowry at the time of Thy marriage. It is for this reason that Thy hips, covering the earth, have become more extensive than it, and excelling it in weight, rendered it lighter in comparative weight. 81.
contentBlocks.meanings: गुरुत्वं (gurutvaṃ) – weight; विस्तारं (vistāraṃ) – extensiveness; क्षितिधरपतिः (kṣitidharapatiḥ) – Mountain-king (kṣiti:earth | dhara:holder | patiḥ:lord); पार्वति (pārvati) – O Parvati; निजान्नितम्बात् (nijānnitambāt) – from own hips (nija:own | nitambāt:from hips); आच्छिद्य (ācchidya) – cutting off; त्वयि (tvayi) – in you; हरणरूपेण (haraṇarūpeṇa) – in the form of taking (haraṇa:taking | rūpeṇa:in the form); निदधे (nidadhe) – placed; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; ते (te) – your; विस्तीर्णः (vistīrṇaḥ) – extensive; गुरुः (guruḥ) – heavy; अयम् (ayam) – this; अशेषां (aśeṣāṃ) – entire; वसुमतीं (vasumatīṃ) – earth; नितम्बप्राग्भारः (nitambaprāgbhāraḥ) – hip's prior weight (nitamba:hip | prāgbhāraḥ:prior weight); स्थगयति (sthagayati) – covers; लघुत्वं (laghutvaṃ) – lightness; नयति (nayati) – leads; च (ca) – and;

contentBlocks.original:
करीन्द्राणां शुण्डान् कनककदलीकाण्डपटली-
मुभाभ्यामूरुभ्यामुभयमपि निर्जित्य भवती ।
सुवृत्ताभ्यां पत्युः प्रणतिकठिनाभ्यां गिरिसुते
विधिज्ञ्ये जानुभ्यां विबुधकरिकुम्भद्वयमसि ॥ ८२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karīndrāṇāṃ śuṇḍān kanakakadalīkāṇḍapaṭalī-
mubhābhyāmūrubhyāmubhayamapi nirjitya bhavatī .
suvṛttābhyāṃ patyuḥ praṇatikaṭhinābhyāṃ girisute
vidhijñye jānubhyāṃ vibudhakarikumbhadvayamasi .. 82..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Mountain! Thy thighs subdue by their beauty the trunk of lordly elephants as also the stem of golden coloured banana trees, while, O observer of all ordained duties, Thy knees, hardened and perfectly rounded by repeated prostrations to Thy Consort, rival the frontal globes of the heavenly Elephant Airavata. 82.
contentBlocks.meanings: करीन्द्राणां (karīndrāṇām) – of lordly elephants (kari:elephant | indra:lord | āṇām:of); शुण्डान् (śuṇḍān) – trunks; कनककदलीकाण्डपटलीम् (kanakakadalīkāṇḍapaṭalīm) – stem of golden banana trees (kanaka:golden | kadalī:banana | kāṇḍa:stem | paṭalīm:cluster); उभाभ्याम् (ubhābhyām) – by both; ऊरुभ्याम् (ūrubhyām) – by thighs; उभयम् (ubhayam) – both; अपि (api) – also; निर्जित्य (nirjitya) – conquering; भवती (bhavatī) – you; सुवृत्ताभ्यां (suvṛttābhyām) – perfectly rounded (su:well | vṛtta:rounded); पत्युः (patyuḥ) – of the husband; प्रणतिकठिनाभ्यां (praṇatikaṭhinābhyām) – hardened by prostrations (praṇati:prostration | kaṭhina:hardened); गिरिसुते (girisute) – O Daughter of the Mountain (giri:mountain | sute:daughter); विधिज्ञ्ये (vidhijñye) – O observer of duties (vidhi:duty | jñye:knower); जानुभ्यां (jānubhyām) – by knees; विबुधकरिकुम्भद्वयम् (vibudhakarikumbhadvayam) – frontal globes of heavenly elephant (vibudha:heavenly | kari:elephant | kumbha:globe | dvayam:pair); असि (asi) – are;

contentBlocks.original:
पराजेतुं रुद्रं द्विगुणशरगर्भौ गिरिसुते
निषङ्गौ जङ्घे ते विषमविशिखो बाढमकृत ।
यदग्रे दृश्यन्ते दशशरफलाः पादयुगली-
नखाग्रच्छद्मानः सुरमकुटशाणैकनिशिताः ॥ ८३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parājetuṃ rudraṃ dviguṇaśaragarbhau girisute
niṣaṅgau jaṅghe te viṣamaviśikho bāḍhamakṛta .
yadagre dṛśyante daśaśaraphalāḥ pādayugalī-
nakhāgracchadmānaḥ suramakuṭaśāṇaikaniśitāḥ .. 83..
contentBlocks.translation: O Daughter of the Mountain! Surely the five-arrowed Kāmadeva has, in order to conquer Rudra, utilised Thy shanks as a quiver to store therein double the number of arrows he is credited with. For, at the end of Thy feet are seen, under the guise of Thy toe nails, ten crescent shaped arrow heads sharpened on the whetstone of the crowns of prostrating Divinities. 83.
contentBlocks.meanings: पराजेतुं (parājetuṃ) – to conquer; रुद्रं (rudraṃ) – Rudra; द्विगुणशरगर्भौ (dviguṇaśaragarbhau) – containing double arrows (dviguṇa:double | śara:arrows | garbhau:containing); गिरिसुते (girisute) – O Daughter of the Mountain (giri:mountain | sute:daughter); निषङ्गौ (niṣaṅgau) – quivers; जङ्घे (jaṅghe) – shanks; ते (te) – thy; विषमविशिखो (viṣamaviśikho) – Kāmadeva (viṣama:unequal | viśikha:arrow); बाढमकृत (bāḍhamakṛta) – surely made; यदग्रे (yadagre) – at the end; दृश्यन्ते (dṛśyante) – are seen; दशशरफलाः (daśaśaraphalāḥ) – ten arrow heads (daśa:ten | śara:arrow | phalāḥ:heads); पादयुगली (pādayugalī) – pair of feet (pāda:feet | yugalī:pair); नखाग्रच्छद्मानः (nakhāgracchadmānaḥ) – disguised as toe nails (nakha:nail | agra:tip | chadmānaḥ:disguised as); सुरमकुटशाणैकनिशिताः (suramakuṭaśāṇaikaniśitāḥ) – sharpened on divine crowns (sura:divine | makuṭa:crown | śāṇa:whetstone | eka:one | niśitāḥ:sharpened);

contentBlocks.original:
श्रुतीनां मूर्धानो दधति तव यौ शेखरतया
ममाप्येतौ मातः शिरसि दयया धेहि चरणौ ।
ययोः पाद्यं पाथः पशुपतिजटाजूटतटिनी
ययोर्लाक्षालक्ष्मीररुणहरिचूडामणिरुचिः ॥ ८४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrutīnāṃ mūrdhāno dadhati tava yau śekharatayā
mamāpyetau mātaḥ śirasi dayayā dhehi caraṇau .
yayoḥ pādyaṃ pāthaḥ paśupatijaṭājūṭataṭinī
yayorlākṣālakṣmīraruṇaharicūḍāmaṇiruciḥ .. 84..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! The crest of the Veda (i.e. the Vedanta known as the Vedaśiras) bear Thy feet as its head ornament. Mayest Thou condescend to place those feet on my head too—the feet the water-offerings on which form the Gaṅgā in the matted locks of Siva, and the bright red dye of which gives brilliance to the jewels on the diadem ofViṣṇu. 84.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्रुतीनाम् (śrutīnām) – of the Vedas; मूर्धानः (mūrdhānaḥ) – crests; दधति (dadhati) – bear; तव (tava) – your; यौ (yau) – which two; शेखरतया (śekharatayā) – as a crest-jewel; मम (mama) – my; अपि (api) – also; एतौ (etau) – these two; मातः (mātaḥ) – O Mother; शिरसि (śirasi) – on the head; दयया (dayayā) – with compassion; धेहि (dhehi) – place; चरणौ (caraṇau) – feet; ययोः (yayoḥ) – of which; पाद्यम् (pādyam) – water-offering; पाथः (pāthaḥ) – water; पशुपति (paśupati) – of Śiva (paśu:beings | pati:lord); जटा (jaṭā) – matted locks; जूट (jūṭa) – hair; तटिनी (taṭinī) – river; ययोः (yayoḥ) – of which; लाक्षा (lākṣā) – red dye; लक्ष्मीः (lakṣmīḥ) – brilliance; अरुण (aruṇa) – red; हरि (hari) – Viṣṇu; चूडामणि (cūḍāmaṇi) – diadem; रुचिः (ruciḥ) – brilliance;

contentBlocks.original:
नमोवाकं ब्रूमो नयनरमणीयाय पदयो-
स्तवास्मै द्वन्द्वाय स्फुटरुचिरसालक्तकवते ।
असूयत्यत्यन्तं यदभिहननाय स्पृहयते
पशूनामीशानः प्रमदवनकङ्केलितरवे ॥ ८५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namovākaṃ brūmo nayanaramaṇīyāya padayo-
stavāsmai dvandvāya sphuṭarucirasālaktakavate .
asūyatyatyantaṃ yadabhihananāya spṛhayate
paśūnāmīśānaḥ pramadavanakaṅkelitarave .. 85..
contentBlocks.translation: Our praise by way of obeisance to Thy feet, a delight to the eyes because of their brilliance arising from the liquid lac dye applied to them! Thy Consort Paśupati, desiring to be kicked with those feet, is extremely jealous of the Kankeli (Aśoka) tree of Thy pleasure garden, as that tree too is a rival aspirant for such kicks. 85.
contentBlocks.meanings: नमोवाकं (namovākaṃ) – obeisance (namaḥ:obeisance | vākaṃ:word); ब्रूमः (brūmaḥ) – we speak; नयनरमणीयाय (nayanaramaṇīyāya) – delightful to the eyes (nayana:eyes | ramaṇīya:delightful); पदयोः (padayoḥ) – of the feet; तव (tava) – your; अस्मै (asmai) – to this; द्वन्द्वाय (dvandvāya) – pair; स्फुटरुचिरसालक्तकवते (sphuṭarucirasālaktakavate) – brilliant with lac dye (sphuṭa:clear | ruci:brilliance | sālaktaka:lac dye | vate:having); असूयति (asūyati) – is jealous; अत्यन्तं (atyantaṃ) – extremely; यत् (yat) – which; अभिहननाय (abhihananāya) – for being kicked; स्पृहयते (spṛhayate) – desires; पशूनाम् (paśūnām) – of the animals; ईशानः (īśānaḥ) – lord; प्रमदवनकङ्केलितरवे (pramadavanakaṅkelitarave) – to the Aśoka tree (pramada:pleasure | vana:garden | kaṅkeli:Aśoka tree | tarave:to);

contentBlocks.original:
मृषा कृत्वा गोत्रस्खलनमथ वैलक्ष्यनमितं
ललाटे भर्तारं चरणकमले ताडयति ते ।
चिरादन्तःशल्यं दहनकृतमुन्मूलितवता
तुलाकोटिक्वाणैः किलिकिलितमीशानरिपुणा ॥ ८६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛṣā kṛtvā gotraskhalanamatha vailakṣyanamitaṃ
lalāṭe bhartāraṃ caraṇakamale tāḍayati te .
cirādantaḥśalyaṃ dahanakṛtamunmūlitavatā
tulākoṭikvāṇaiḥ kilikilitamīśānaripuṇā .. 86..
contentBlocks.translation: O Devi! When Thou didst deliver a kick (in love quarrel) at the forehead of Thy Consort when he bent his head in shame for calling Thee inadvertently by the name of another woman, his enemy Kama got an opportunity to root out of himself his long-standing rancour towards him, Īśa, for consuming him in fire, and he gave out his joyous acclamations, as it were, in the form of the tinkling of Thy anklets. 86.
contentBlocks.meanings: मृषा (mṛṣā) – falsely; कृत्वा (kṛtvā) – having done; गोत्रस्खलनम् (gotraskhalanam) – slip of the tongue (gotra:family name | skhalanam:slip); अथ (atha) – then; वैलक्ष्यनमितम् (vailakṣyanamitam) – bent in shame (vailakṣya:shame | namitam:bent); ललाटे (lalāṭe) – on the forehead; भर्तारम् (bhartāram) – husband; चरणकमले (caraṇakamale) – lotus-like foot (caraṇa:foot | kamale:lotus-like); ताडयति (tāḍayati) – kicks; ते (te) – your; चिरात् (cirāt) – for a long time; अन्तःशल्यं (antaḥśalyam) – internal thorn (antaḥ:internal | śalyam:thorn); दहनकृतम् (dahanakṛtam) – caused by burning (dahana:burning | kṛtam:caused by); उन्मूलितवता (unmūlitavatā) – uprooted; तुलाकोटिक्वाणैः (tulākoṭikvāṇaiḥ) – with tinkling sounds (tulākoṭi:balance beam | kvāṇaiḥ:with sounds); किलिकिलितम् (kilikilitam) – joyous acclamations; ईशानरिपुणा (īśānaripuṇā) – by the enemy of Īśa (īśāna:Īśa | ripuṇā:by the enemy);

contentBlocks.original:
हिमानीहन्तव्यं हिमगिरिनिवासैकचतुरौ
निशायां निद्राणं निशि चरमभागे च विशदौ ।
वरं लक्ष्मीपात्रं श्रियमतिसृजन्तौ समयिनां
सरोजं त्वत्पादौ जननि जयतश्चित्रमिह किम् ॥ ८७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
himānīhantavyaṃ himagirinivāsaikacaturau
niśāyāṃ nidrāṇaṃ niśi caramabhāge ca viśadau .
varaṃ lakṣmīpātraṃ śriyamatisṛjantau samayināṃ
sarojaṃ tvatpādau janani jayataścitramiha kim .. 87..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother of the Universe! The lotus of Thy feet, which flourishes always on the snow Mountain (Himalayas), blooming night and day and bestowing undecaying wealth (Lakṣmī) on the inner circle of devotees, is undoubtedly far superior to the common lotus flower which perishes in snowļ closes at night and forms the favourite resort of Lakṣmī (the goddess of wealth whose blessings on devotees are very temporary). 87.
contentBlocks.meanings: हिमानीहन्तव्यं (himānīhantavyaṃ) – to be destroyed by snow (himānī:snow | hantavyaṃ:to be destroyed); हिमगिरिनिवासैकचतुरौ (himagirinivāsaikacaturau) – clever in residing on Himalaya (himagiri:Himalaya | nivāsa:residing | eka:one | caturau:clever); निशायां (niśāyāṃ) – at night; निद्राणं (nidrāṇaṃ) – asleep; निशि (niśi) – at night; चरमभागे (caramabhāge) – in the last part (carama:last | bhāge:part); च (ca) – and; विशदौ (viśadau) – clear; वरं (varaṃ) – better; लक्ष्मीपात्रं (lakṣmīpātraṃ) – vessel of Lakṣmī (lakṣmī:Lakṣmī | pātraṃ:vessel); श्रियम् (śriyam) – wealth; अतिसृजन्तौ (atisṛjantau) – bestowing; समयिनां (samayināṃ) – of the devotees; सरोजं (sarojaṃ) – lotus; त्वत्पादौ (tvatpādau) – Thy feet (tvat:Thy | pādau:feet); जननि (janani) – O Mother; जयतश्चित्रम् (jayataścitram) – victorious and wonderful (jayataḥ:victorious | citram:wonderful); इह (iha) – here; किम् (kim) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
पदं ते कीर्तीनां प्रपदमपदं देवि विपदां
कथं नीतं सद्भिः कठिनकमठीकर्परतुलाम् ।
कथं वा बाहुभ्यामुपयमनकाले पुरभिदा
यदादाय न्यस्तं दृषदि दयमानेन मनसा ॥ ८८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padaṃ te kīrtīnāṃ prapadamapadaṃ devi vipadāṃ
kathaṃ nītaṃ sadbhiḥ kaṭhinakamaṭhīkarparatulām .
kathaṃ vā bāhubhyāmupayamanakāle purabhidā
yadādāya nyastaṃ dṛṣadi dayamānena manasā .. 88..
contentBlocks.translation: The upper part of Thy feet are the seat of all excellences worthy of praise and they are the panacea for all dangers. How then do great poets equate them with the hard shell of a tortoise? And how did Thy Consort, the Destroyer of the Cities, in spite of all His tenderness towards Thee, have the heart to place them on a hard granite grinding stone at Thy marriage rite? 88.
contentBlocks.meanings: पदं (padaṃ) – foot; ते (te) – your; कीर्तीनां (kīrtīnām) – of glories; प्रपदम् (prapadam) – supreme position; अपदं (apadam) – without danger; देवि (devi) – O Goddess; विपदां (vipadām) – of dangers; कथं (kathaṃ) – how; नीतं (nītaṃ) – considered; सद्भिः (sadbhiḥ) – by the wise; कठिनकमठीकर्परतुलाम् (kaṭhinakamaṭhīkarparatulām) – hard shell of a tortoise (kaṭhina:hard | kamaṭhī:tortoise | karpara:shell | tulām:comparison); कथं (kathaṃ) – how; वा (vā) – or; बाहुभ्याम् (bāhubhyām) – by the arms; उपयमनकाले (upayamanakāle) – at the time of marriage (upayamana:marriage | kāle:at the time); पुरभिदा (purabhidā) – by the destroyer of cities (pura:cities | bhidā:destroyer); यद् (yad) – which; आदाय (ādāya) – having taken; न्यस्तं (nyastaṃ) – placed; दृषदि (dṛṣadi) – on the stone; दयमानेन (dayamānena) – with compassion; मनसा (manasā) – with the mind;

contentBlocks.original:
नखैर्नाकस्त्रीणां करकमलसंकोचशशिभि-
स्तरूणां दिव्यानां हसत इव ते चण्डि चरणौ ।
फलानि स्वःस्थेभ्यः किसलयकराग्रेण ददतां
दरिद्रेभ्यो भद्रां श्रियमनिशमह्नाय ददतौ ॥ ८९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nakhairnākastrīṇāṃ karakamalasaṃkocaśaśibhi-
starūṇāṃ divyānāṃ hasata iva te caṇḍi caraṇau .
phalāni svaḥsthebhyaḥ kisalayakarāgreṇa dadatāṃ
daridrebhyo bhadrāṃ śriyamaniśamahnāya dadatau .. 89..
contentBlocks.translation: O Candị! Thy feet, of the moon-like nails, which make the lotus-bud-like palms of adoring heavenly damsels close, do shower abundant wealth quickly on humble and poor devotees, and thus mock at the wish-yielding Kalpaka trees of heaven whose arms of tender branches bestow desired gifts only to Devas who live in heavenly affluence. 89.
contentBlocks.meanings: नखैः (nakhaiḥ) – by the nails; नाकस्त्रीणां (nākastrīṇām) – of heavenly women (nāka:heaven | strīṇām:of women); करकमलसंकोचशशिभिः (karakamalasaṃkocaśaśibhiḥ) – moon-like nails causing lotus-bud-like palms to close (kara:hand | kamala:lotus | saṃkoca:closing | śaśibhiḥ:by the moons); तरूणां (tarūṇām) – of trees; दिव्यानां (divyānām) – of divine; हसत (hasata) – mocking; इव (iva) – like; ते (te) – your; चण्डि (caṇḍi) – O Caṇḍi; चरणौ (caraṇau) – feet; फलानि (phalāni) – fruits; स्वःस्थेभ्यः (svaḥsthebhyaḥ) – to those in heaven (svaḥ:heaven | sthebhyaḥ:to those who are); किसलयकराग्रेण (kisalayakarāgreṇa) – by the tender branch tips (kisalaya:tender shoot | kara:hand | agreṇa:by the tip); ददतां (dadatām) – giving; दरिद्रेभ्यः (daridrebhyaḥ) – to the poor; भद्रां (bhadrām) – auspicious; श्रियम् (śriyam) – wealth; अनिशम् (aniśam) – constantly; अह्नाय (ahnāya) – by day; ददतौ (dadatau) – giving;

contentBlocks.original:
ददाने दीनेभ्यः श्रियमनिशमाशानुसदृशी-
ममन्दं सौन्दर्यप्रकरमकरन्दम् विकिरति ।
तवास्मिन् मन्दारस्तबकसुभगे यातु चरणे
निमज्जन्मज्जीवः करणचरणः षट्चरणताम् ॥ ९०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dadāne dīnebhyaḥ śriyamaniśamāśānusadṛśī-
mamandaṃ saundaryaprakaramakarandam vikirati .
tavāsmin mandārastabakasubhage yātu caraṇe
nimajjanmajjīvaḥ karaṇacaraṇaḥ ṣaṭcaraṇatām .. 90..
contentBlocks.translation: Thy feet form a veritable bunch of Mandāra flowers dripping the honey of beauty and are a liberal supplier of wealth to Thy poor devotees. May my spirit with its six organs of knowledge (including the mind) become a six-footed honey-sucking bee at those feet for ever more. 90.
contentBlocks.meanings: ददाने (dadāne) – giving; दीनेभ्यः (dīnebhyaḥ) – to the poor; श्रियम् (śriyam) – wealth; अनिशम् (aniśam) – constantly; आशानुसदृशी (āśānusadṛśī) – like a wish (āśā:wish | anusadṛśī:like); ममन्दं (mamandaṃ) – gently; सौन्दर्यप्रकरमकरन्दम् (saundaryaprakaramakarandam) – honey of beauty (saundarya:beauty | prakara:abundance | makarandam:honey); विकिरति (vikirati) – spreads; तव (tava) – your; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; मन्दारस्तबकसुभगे (mandārastabakasubhage) – beautiful bunch of Mandāra flowers (mandāra:Mandāra | stabaka:bunch | subhage:beautiful); यातु (yātu) – may go; चरणे (caraṇe) – to the feet; निमज्जन् (nimajjan) – immersing; मज्जीवः (majjīvaḥ) – my life; करणचरणः (karaṇacaraṇaḥ) – with six organs (karaṇa:organs | caraṇaḥ:feet); षट्चरणताम् (ṣaṭcaraṇatām) – six-footed (ṣaṭ:six | caraṇatām:footed);

contentBlocks.original:
पदन्यासक्रीडापरिचयमिवारब्धुमनसः
स्खलन्तस्ते खेलं भवनकलहंसा न जहति ।
अतस्तेषां शिक्षां सुभगमणिमञ्जीररणित-
च्छलादाचक्षाणं चरणकमलं चारुचरिते ॥ ९१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
padanyāsakrīḍāparicayamivārabdhumanasaḥ
skhalantaste khelaṃ bhavanakalahaṃsā na jahati .
atasteṣāṃ śikṣāṃ subhagamaṇimañjīraraṇita-
cchalādācakṣāṇaṃ caraṇakamalaṃ cārucarite .. 91..
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess of Holy Fame! The royal Swans inhabiting Thy residence are never abandoning their pursuit of Thee, in their effort to observe Thy gait for correcting their own defective ways. And Thy lotus feet are, it seems, giving instructions to them in the guise of the tinkling sounds made by the anklets on them, studded with precious stones. 91.
contentBlocks.meanings: पदन्यासक्रीडापरिचयमिवारब्धुमनसः (padanyāsakrīḍāparicayamivārabdhumanasaḥ) – intent on learning the gait (padanyāsa:gait | krīḍā:play | paricaya:familiarity | iva:as if | ārabdha:begun | manasaḥ:of the mind); स्खलन्तः (skhalantaḥ) – stumbling; ते (te) – they; खेलं (khelaṃ) – play; भवनकलहंसा (bhavanakalahaṃsā) – royal swans of the residence (bhavana:residence | kalahaṃsā:royal swans); न (na) – not; जहति (jahati) – abandon; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; तेषां (teṣāṃ) – their; शिक्षां (śikṣāṃ) – instruction; सुभगमणिमञ्जीररणितच्छलादाचक्षाणं (subhagamaṇimañjīraraṇitacchalādācakṣāṇaṃ) – giving instruction through anklet sounds (subhaga:beautiful | maṇi:gem | mañjīra:anklet | raṇita:sound | cchalāt:through guise | ācakṣāṇaṃ:giving instruction); चरणकमलं (caraṇakamalaṃ) – lotus feet (caraṇa:feet | kamalaṃ:lotus); चारुचरिते (cārucarite) – O beautiful one (cāru:beautiful | carite:one);

contentBlocks.original:
गतास्ते मञ्चत्वं द्रुहिणहरिरुद्रेश्वरभृतः
शिवः स्वच्छच्छायाघटितकपटप्रच्छदपटः ।
त्वदीयानां भासां प्रतिफलनरागारुणतया
शरीरी श‍ृङ्गारो रस इव दृशां दोग्धि कुतुकम् ॥ ९२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gatāste mañcatvaṃ druhiṇaharirudreśvarabhṛtaḥ
śivah svacchac-chāyā-ghaṭita-kapaṭa-pracchada-paṭaḥ .
tvadīyānāṃ bhāsāṃ pratiphalanarāgāruṇatayā
śarīrī śṛṅgāro rasa iva dṛśāṃ dogdhi kutukam .. 92..
contentBlocks.translation: Thy servitors Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra and Īśvara have taken the shape of Thy cot (i. e. the four legs of the cot) in order to serve Thee very closely, while Sadasiva has formed himself into Thy bedsheet, reflecting Thy crimson glory in his assumed whiteness, thereby causing amazement to Thee by presenting Himself as the very embodiment of erotic sentiment. 92.
contentBlocks.meanings: गताः (gatāḥ) – have gone; ते (te) – they; मञ्चत्वं (mañcatvaṃ) – state of being a cot; द्रुहिणहरिरुद्रेश्वरभृतः (druhiṇaharirudreśvarabhṛtaḥ) – Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra, Īśvara (druhiṇa:Brahmā | hari:Viṣṇu | rudra:Rudra | īśvara:Īśvara | bhṛtaḥ:having assumed); शिवः (śivaḥ) – Śiva; स्वच्छच्छायाघटितकपटप्रच्छदपटः (svacchacchāyāghaṭitakapaṭapracchadapaṭaḥ) – formed into a bedsheet (svaccha:clear | chāyā:reflection | ghaṭita:formed | kapaṭa:deceptive | pracchada:cover | paṭaḥ:cloth); त्वदीयानां (tvadīyānāṃ) – of yours; भासां (bhāsāṃ) – of lights; प्रतिफलनरागारुणतया (pratiphalanarāgāruṇatayā) – by reflection's redness (pratiphalana:reflection | rāga:redness | aruṇatayā:by redness); शरीरी (śarīrī) – embodied; शृङ्गारः (śṛṅgāraḥ) – erotic sentiment; रस (rasa) – essence; इव (iva) – like; दृशां (dṛśāṃ) – of eyes; दोग्धि (dogdhi) – causes; कुतुकम् (kutukam) – amazement;

contentBlocks.original:
अराला केशेषु प्रकृतिसरला मन्दहसिते
शिरीषाभा चित्ते दृषदुपलशोभा कुचतटे ।
भृशं तन्वी मध्ये पृथुरुरसिजारोहविषये
जगत्त्रातुं शम्भोर्जयति करुणा काचिदरुणा ॥ ९३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
arālā keśeṣu prakṛtisaralā mandahasite
śirīṣābhā citte dṛṣadupalaśobhā kucataṭe .
bhṛśaṃ tanvī madhye pṛthururasijārohaviṣaye
jagattrātuṃ śambhorjayati karuṇā kācidaruṇā .. 93..
contentBlocks.translation: Her glory Aruṇā (Kāmeśvarī), the Śakti of Śambhu—the very embodiment of graciousness—with her locks all curly, her smile naturally artless, her heart soft like Śirīṣa flower, her breast as tight and hard like a grinding stone, her waist extremely slender, and her hips and breasts generous in size. 93.
