contentBlocks.meanings: अराला (arālā) – curly; केशेषु (keśeṣu) – in the hair; प्रकृतिसरला (prakṛtisaralā) – naturally artless (prakṛti:nature | saralā:artless); मन्दहसिते (mandahasite) – gentle smile (manda:gentle | hasite:smile); शिरीषाभा (śirīṣābhā) – like Śirīṣa flower (śirīṣa:Śirīṣa flower | ābhā:like); चित्ते (citte) – in the heart; दृषदुपलशोभा (dṛṣadupalaśobhā) – hard like a stone (dṛṣad:stone | upala:stone | śobhā:beauty); कुचतटे (kucataṭe) – on the breast; भृशं (bhṛśam) – extremely; तन्वी (tanvī) – slender; मध्ये (madhye) – in the middle; पृथुरुरसिजारोहविषये (pṛthururasijārohaviṣaye) – generous hips and breasts (pṛthu:generous | uras:breast | ja:born | āroha:hips | viṣaye:in the area); जगत्त्रातुं (jagattrātuṃ) – to protect the world (jagat:world | trātuṃ:to protect); शम्भोः (śambhoḥ) – of Śambhu; जयति (jayati) – victorious; करुणा (karuṇā) – compassion; काचिदरुणा (kācidaruṇā) – some glory Aruṇā (kācit:some | aruṇā:Aruṇā);

contentBlocks.original:
कलङ्कः कस्तूरी रजनिकरबिम्बं जलमयं
कलाभिः कर्पूरैर्मरकतकरण्डं निबिडितम् ।
अतस्त्वद्भोगेन प्रतिदिनमिदं रिक्तकुहरं
विधिर्भूयो भूयो निबिडयति नूनं तव कृते ॥ ९४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalaṅkaḥ kastūrī rajanikarabimbaṃ jalamayaṃ
kalābhiḥ karpūrairmarakatakaraṇḍaṃ nibiḍitam .
atastvadbhogena pratidinamidaṃ riktakuharaṃ
vidhirbhūyo bhūyo nibiḍayati nūnaṃ tava kṛte .. 94..
contentBlocks.translation: The mark on the moon is musk; and her watery disc, a receptacle (canister) of emerald for containing pieces of camphol in the form of the digits of the moon. As and when the contents of that receptacle are exhausted by Thy using them, Brahmā fills it up day by day for Thee with the digits of the waxing moon. 94.
contentBlocks.meanings: कलङ्कः (kalaṅkaḥ) – mark; कस्तूरी (kastūrī) – musk; रजनिकरबिम्बं (rajanikarabimbaṃ) – moon disc (rajanikara:moon | bimbaṃ:disc); जलमयं (jalamayaṃ) – watery; कलाभिः (kalābhiḥ) – with digits; कर्पूरैः (karpūraiḥ) – with camphor; मरकतकरण्डं (marakatakaraṇḍaṃ) – emerald receptacle (marakata:emerald | karaṇḍaṃ:receptacle); निबिडितम् (nibiḍitam) – filled; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; त्वद्भोगेन (tvadbhogena) – by your enjoyment (tvad:your | bhogena:by enjoyment); प्रतिदिनम् (pratidinam) – every day; इदं (idaṃ) – this; रिक्तकुहरं (riktakuharaṃ) – empty cavity (rikta:empty | kuharaṃ:cavity); विधिः (vidhiḥ) – Brahmā; भूयः (bhūyaḥ) – again; भूयः (bhūyaḥ) – again; निबिडयति (nibiḍayati) – fills; नूनं (nūnaṃ) – indeed; तव (tava) – for you; कृते (kṛte) – for the sake;

contentBlocks.original:
पुरारातेरन्तःपुरमसि ततस्त्वच्चरणयोः
सपर्यामर्यादा तरलकरणानामसुलभा ।
तथा ह्येते नीताः शतमखमुखाः सिद्धिमतुलां
तव द्वारोपान्तस्थितिभिरणिमाद्याभिरमराः ॥ ९५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
purārāterantaḥpuramasi tatastvaccaraṇayoḥ
saparyāmaryādā taralakaraṇānāmasulabhā .
tathā hyete nītāḥ śatamakhamukhāḥ siddhimatulāṃ
tava dvār’opānta-sthitibhir aṇim’ ādyābhir amarāḥ .. 95
contentBlocks.translation: Thou art the Consort of the Destroyer of the Cities, residing in His inner apartment, to which votaries with unregenerate mind find no entry and are therefore denied the privilege of the immediate worship of Thy feet. It is therefore that all the Deities headed by Indra have by their unparalleled austerities been able only to attain to Anima and other psychic powers (Siddhis) which are but Thy gate keepers (being only peripheral to Thee and far removed from Thy innermost attributes). 95.
contentBlocks.meanings: पुरारातेः (purārāteḥ) – of the Destroyer of Cities (pura:city | ārāteḥ:of the enemy); अन्तःपुरम् (antaḥpuram) – inner apartment (antaḥ:inner | puram:apartment); असि (asi) – you are; ततः (tataḥ) – therefore; त्वत् (tvat) – your; चरणयोः (caraṇayoḥ) – of the feet; सपर्या (saparyā) – worship; मर्यादा (maryādā) – limit; तरलकरणानाम् (taralakaraṇānām) – of the unsteady-minded (tarala:unsteady | karaṇānām:of the minds); असुलभा (asulabhā) – not easily attainable; तथा (tathā) – thus; हि (hi) – indeed; एते (ete) – these; नीताः (nītāḥ) – led; शतमखमुखाः (śatamakhamukhāḥ) – headed by Indra (śatamakha:Indra | mukhāḥ:headed by); सिद्धिम् (siddhim) – perfection; अतुलाम् (atulām) – unparalleled; तव (tava) – your; द्वार (dvāra) – door; उपान्तस्थितिभिः (upāntasthitibhiḥ) – by those stationed nearby (upānta:nearby | sthitibhiḥ:by those stationed); अणिमाद्याभिः (aṇimādyābhiḥ) – by Anima and others (aṇimā:Anima | ādyābhiḥ:and others); अमराः (amarāḥ) – immortals;

contentBlocks.original:
कलत्रं वैधात्रं कतिकति भजन्ते न कवयः
श्रियो देव्याः को वा न भवति पतिः कैरपि धनैः ।
महादेवं हित्वा तव सति सतीनामचरमे
कुचाभ्यामासङ्गः कुरवकतरोरप्यसुलभः ॥ ९६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalatraṃ vaidhātraṃ katikati bhajante na kavayaḥ
śriyo devyāḥ ko vā na bhavati patiḥ kairapi dhanaiḥ .
mahādevaṃ hitvā tava sati satīnāmacarame
kucābhyāmāsaṅgaḥ kuravakatarorapyasulabhaḥ .. 96..
contentBlocks.translation: O Chastity Emobodied! How numerous are the poets who have courted and attained Sarasvati, the consort of Brahmā and the deity of learning and fine arts! So also who with some wealth fails to become the Lord of Śrī (Śrīpati), the consort of Viṣṇu and the goddess of wealth! But, O the foremost of Chaste Ones! None besides Śiva the Great God,-not even the tree called Kuravaka, has ever had the embrace of Thy breasts. 96.
contentBlocks.meanings: कलत्रं (kalatraṃ) – consort; वैधात्रं (vaidhātraṃ) – of Brahmā; कतिकति (katikati) – how numerous; भजन्ते (bhajante) – attain; न (na) – not; कवयः (kavayaḥ) – poets; श्रियो (śriyo) – of Śrī; देव्याः (devyāḥ) – of the goddess; को (ko) – who; वा (vā) – or; न (na) – not; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; पतिः (patiḥ) – lord; कैरपि (kairapi) – by some; धनैः (dhanaiḥ) – with wealth; महादेवं (mahādevaṃ) – Śiva the Great God; हित्वा (hitvā) – besides; तव (tava) – your; सति (sati) – O Chaste One; सतीनामचरमे (satīnām acarame) – foremost of Chaste Ones (satīnām:of Chaste Ones | acarame:foremost); कुचाभ्याम् (kucābhyām) – with breasts; आसङ्गः (āsaṅgaḥ) – embrace; कुरवकतरोः (kuravakataroḥ) – of the Kuravaka tree; अपि (api) – even; असुलभः (asulabhaḥ) – not easily attained;

contentBlocks.original:
गिरामाहुर्देवीं द्रुहिणगृहिणीमागमविदो
हरेः पत्नीं पद्मां हरसहचरीमद्रितनयाम् ।
तुरीया कापि त्वं दुरधिगमनिःसीममहिमा
महामाया विश्वं भ्रमयसि परब्रह्ममहिषि ॥ ९७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
girāmāhurdevīṃ druhiṇagṛhiṇīmāgamavido
hareḥ patnīṃ padmāṃ harasahacarīmadritanayām .
turīyā kāpi tvaṃ duradhigamaniḥsīmamahimā
mahāmāyā viśvaṃ bhramayasi parabrahmamahiṣi .. 97..
contentBlocks.translation: O Consort of Parabrahman! The scholars who know the real meaning of the Āgamas (scriptures) describe Thee as Sarasvatī, the Goddess of Learning and the Consort of Brahmā. Besides, they speak of Thee as the lotus-born Lakṣmī, the Consort of Viṣṇu, as also as the Daughter of the Mountain (Pārvatī) and the Consort of Hara (Śiva). But Thou art however the Fourth (the unique Power that is the source of the three Deities mentioned) of inconceivable and limitless majesties—the indeterninable Mahāmāyā who revolves the wheel of this world. 97.
contentBlocks.meanings: गिराम् (girām) – of words; आहुः (āhuḥ) – they say; देवीं (devīm) – goddess; द्रुहिणगृहिणीम् (druhiṇagṛhiṇīm) – consort of Brahmā (druhiṇa:Brahmā | gṛhiṇīm:consort); आगमविदः (āgamavidaḥ) – knowers of scriptures (āgama:scriptures | vidaḥ:knowers); हरेः (hareḥ) – of Hari (Viṣṇu); पत्नीम् (patnīm) – consort; पद्माम् (padmām) – lotus-born; हरसहचारीम् (harasahacarīm) – consort of Hara (Śiva) (hara:Hara (Śiva) | sahacarīm:consort); अद्रितनयाम् (adritanayām) – daughter of the mountain (adri:mountain | tanayām:daughter); तुरीया (turīyā) – the fourth; का (kā) – who; अपि (api) – even; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; दुरधिगमनिःसीममहिमा (duradhigamaniḥsīmamahimā) – inconceivable and limitless majesty (duradhigama:inconceivable | niḥsīma:limitless | mahimā:majesty); महामाया (mahāmāyā) – great illusion (mahā:great | māyā:illusion); विश्वम् (viśvam) – world; भ्रमयसि (bhramayasi) – you revolve; परब्रह्ममहिषि (parabrahmamahiṣi) – consort of Parabrahman (parabrahma:Parabrahman | mahiṣi:consort);

contentBlocks.original:
कदा काले मातः कथय कलितालक्तकरसं
पिबेयं विद्यार्थी तव चरणनिर्णेजनजलम् ।
प्रकृत्या मूकानामपि च कविताकारणतया
कदा धत्ते वाणीमुखकमलताम्बूलरसताम् ॥ ९८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kadā kāle mātaḥ kathaya kalitālaktakarasaṃ
pibeyaṃ vidyārthī tava caraṇanirṇejanajalam .
prakṛtyā mūkānāmapi ca kavitākāraṇatayā
kadā dhatte vāṇīmukhakamalatāmbūlarasatām .. 98..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! Tell me when I, a seeker after wisdom, shall have the privilege of imbibing the red-tinged water with which Thy lac-painted feet have been washed,—water that can generate poetic genius even in a naturally dumb person. When shall I expect it (that red-tinged water) to flow out of my mouth as great poetry, which forms the chewed betel leaf juice of Vāṇī, (Sarasvatī), the Goddess of Learning and Poesy? 98.
contentBlocks.meanings: कदा (kadā) – when; काले (kāle) – at the time; मातः (mātaḥ) – O Mother; कथय (kathaya) – tell; कलित (kalita) – imbibed; आलक्तकरसं (ālaktakarasaṃ) – lac-painted water (ālaktaka:lac | rasaṃ:water); पिबेयं (pibeyaṃ) – I shall drink; विद्यार्थी (vidyārthī) – seeker of wisdom; तव (tava) – your; चरणनिर्णेजनजलम् (caraṇanirṇejanajalam) – water washing your feet (caraṇa:feet | nirṇejana:washing | jalam:water); प्रकृत्या (prakṛtyā) – by nature; मूकानाम् (mūkānām) – of the dumb; अपि (api) – even; च (ca) – and; कविता (kavitā) – poetry; कारणतया (kāraṇatayā) – as the cause; कदा (kadā) – when; धत्ते (dhatte) – assumes; वाणीमुखकमलताम्बूलरसताम् (vāṇīmukhakamalatāmbūlarasatām) – betel juice of Vāṇī's mouth (vāṇī:Vāṇī | mukha:mouth | kamala:lotus | tāmbūla:betel | rasa:juice);

contentBlocks.original:
सरस्वत्या लक्ष्म्या विधिहरिसपत्नो विहरते
रतेः पातिव्रत्यं शिथिलयति रम्येण वपुषा ।
चिरं जीवन्नेव क्षपितपशुपाशव्यतिकरः
परानन्दाभिख्यम् रसयति रसं त्वद्भजनवान् ॥ ९९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarasvatyā lakṣmyā vidhiharisapatno viharate
rateḥ pātivratyaṃ śithilayati ramyeṇa vapuṣā .
ciraṃ jīvanneva kṣapitapaśupāśavyatikaraḥ
parānandābhikhyam rasayati rasaṃ tvadbhajanavān .. 99..
contentBlocks.translation: O Mother! A devotee of Thine sports with Sarasvatī (learning) and Śrī (Wealth), and thus courts the jealousy of their consorts, Brahmā and Viṣṇu. By the charm of his body, he attracts the attention of Rati, the wife of Kama Deva (the god of love), and thus violates her chastity. And with the beginningless ignorance-born bondage of Jīvāhood broken, he is immersed in the supreme Bliss of Brahman even in this long-lived embodied state. 99.
contentBlocks.meanings: सरस्वत्या (sarasvatyā) – with Sarasvatī; लक्ष्म्या (lakṣmyā) – with Lakṣmī; विधिहरिसपत्नः (vidhiharisa-patnaḥ) – rival of Brahmā and Viṣṇu (vidhi:Brahmā | hari:Viṣṇu | sapatnaḥ:rival); विहरते (viharate) – sports; रतेः (rateḥ) – of Rati; पातिव्रत्यं (pātivratyaṃ) – chastity; शिथिलयति (śithilayati) – loosens; रम्येण (ramyeṇa) – by charming; वपुषा (vapuṣā) – by body; चिरं (ciraṃ) – long; जीवन्नेव (jīvanneva) – even while living; क्षपितपशुपाशव्यतिकरः (kṣapitapaśupāśavyatikaraḥ) – with bondage destroyed (kṣapita:destroyed | paśu:soul | pāśa:bondage | vyatikaraḥ:state); परानन्दाभिख्यम् (parānandābhikhyam) – called supreme bliss (para:supreme | ānanda:bliss | abhikhyam:called); रसयति (rasayati) – enjoys; रसं (rasaṃ) – essence; त्वद्भजनवान् (tvadbhajanavān) – devotee of Thine (tvad:Thine | bhajana:devotion | vān:having);

contentBlocks.original:
प्रदीपज्वालाभिर्दिवसकरनीराजनविधिः
सुधासूतेश्चन्द्रोपलजललवैरर्घ्यरचना ।
स्वकीयैरम्भोभिः सलिलनिधिसौहित्यकरणं
त्वदीयाभिर्वाग्भिस्तव जननि वाचां स्तुतिरियम् ॥ १००॥

इति श्रीमत्परमहंसपरिव्राजकाचार्यस्य
श्रीगोविन्दभगवत्पूज्यपादशिष्यस्य
श्रीमच्छङ्करभगवतः कृतौ सौन्दर्यलहरी सम्पूर्णा ॥
ॐ तत्सत्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pradīpajvālābhirdivasakaranīrājanavidhiḥ
sudhāsūteścandropalajalalavairarghyaracanā .
svakīyairambhobhiḥ salilanidhisauhityakaraṇaṃ
tvadīyābhirvāgbhistava janani vācāṃ stutiriyam .. 100..

iti śrīmatparamahaṃsaparivrājakācāryasya
śrīgovindabhagavatpūjyapādaśiṣyasya
śrīmacchaṅkarabhagavataḥ kṛtau saundaryalaharī sampūrṇā ..
oṃ tatsat
contentBlocks.translation:
Just as doing Nīrājana (light waving ceremony) to the sun is only the offering of his own light to him; just as making an offering of Arghya to the moon with the water that oozes out of the moon-stone in contact with moon light, is only to give back what belongs to the moon, and just as making water-offering (Tarpaṇa)to the ocean is to return what belongs to it—so is, O Source of all Learning, this hymn addressed to Thee composed of words that are already Thine. 100.

Thus completes Saundaryalahari composed by the revered disciple of the greatly respected, supreme ascetic of the highest order, Sri Govinda Bhagavatpada, who was himself the disciple of Sri Adi Shankaracharya.
Om, That is the Eternal Truth.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रदीपज्वालाभिः (pradīpajvālābhiḥ) – with lamp flames (pradīpa:lamp | jvālābhiḥ:with flames); दिवसकर (divasakara) – sun (divasa:day | kara:maker); नीराजनविधिः (nīrājanavidhiḥ) – light waving ceremony (nīrājana:light waving | vidhiḥ:ceremony); सुधासूतेः (sudhāsūteḥ) – of the moon (sudhā:nectar | sūteḥ:of the progenitor); चन्द्रोपल (candropala) – moon-stone (candra:moon | upala:stone); जललवैः (jalalavaiḥ) – with drops of water (jala:water | lavaiḥ:with drops); अर्घ्यरचना (arghyaracanā) – offering creation (arghya:offering | racanā:creation); स्वकीयैः (svakīyaiḥ) – with one's own; अम्भोभिः (ambhobhiḥ) – with waters; सलिलनिधि (salilanidhi) – ocean (salila:water | nidhi:treasure); सौहित्यकरणं (sauhityakaraṇaṃ) – making satisfaction (sauhitya:satisfaction | karaṇaṃ:making); त्वदीयाभिः (tvadīyābhiḥ) – with your; वाग्भिः (vāgbhiḥ) – words; तव (tava) – your; जननि (janani) – O Mother; वाचां (vācāṃ) – of words; स्तुतिः (stutiḥ) – praise; इयम् (iyam) – this;

contentBlocks.original:
अनुबन्धः
समानीतः पद्भ्यां मणिमुकुरतामम्बरमणि-
र्भयादास्यादन्तःस्तिमितकिरणश्रेणिमसृणः ।
दधाति त्वद्वक्त्रंप्रतिफलनमश्रान्तविकचं
निरातङ्कं चन्द्रान्निजहृदयपङ्केरुहमिव ॥ १०१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anubandhaḥ
samānītaḥ padbhyāṃ maṇimukuratāmambaramaṇi
bhayād āsyād antaḥ -stimita-kiraṇa-śreṇī-masrṇaḥ .
dadhāti tvadvaktraṃpratiphalanamaśrāntavikacaṃ
nirātaṅkaṃ candrānnijahṛdayapaṅkeruhamiva .. 101..
contentBlocks.translation:
Addendum
The sun has assumed the form of a mirror carved out of crystal for Thee to rest Thy feet upon, having withdrawn his rays lest they should burn Thy face. In that mirror constited of the sun is reflected Thy face, which is, as it were, one with the sun’s heart-lotus wherein Thou art present. But being in the Devi’s presence, that heart-lotus never closes before the moon (like ordinary lotus flowers).
contentBlocks.meanings: अनुबन्धः (anubandhaḥ) – addendum; समानीतः (samānītaḥ) – brought together; पद्भ्यां (padbhyāṃ) – by the feet; मणिमुकुरताम् (maṇimukuratām) – form of a jewel mirror (maṇi:jewel | mukuratām:form of a mirror); अम्बरमणिः (ambaramaṇiḥ) – sky jewel (sun) (ambara:sky | maṇiḥ:jewel); भयात् (bhayāt) – out of fear; आस्यात् (āsyāt) – from the face; अन्तः (antaḥ) – within; स्तिमितकिरणश्रेणिमसृणः (stimitakiraṇaśreṇimasṛṇaḥ) – smooth with still rays (stimita:still | kiraṇa:rays | śreṇi:series | masṛṇaḥ:smooth); दधाति (dadhāti) – holds; त्वद्वक्त्रं (tvadvaktraṃ) – your face (tvad:your | vaktraṃ:face); प्रतिफलनम् (pratiphalanam) – reflection; अश्रान्तविकचं (aśrāntavikacaṃ) – unceasingly blooming (aśrānta:unceasing | vikacaṃ:blooming); निरातङ्कं (nirātaṅkaṃ) – without fear (nir:without | ātaṅkaṃ:fear); चन्द्रात् (candrāt) – from the moon; निजहृदयपङ्केरुहमिव (nijahṛdayapaṅkeruhamiva) – like own heart lotus (nija:own | hṛdaya:heart | paṅkeruham:lotus | iva:like);

contentBlocks.original:
समुद्भूतस्थूलस्तनभरमुरश्चारु हसितं
कटाक्षे कन्दर्पः कतिचन कदम्बद्युति वपुः ।
हरस्य त्वद्भ्रान्तिं मनसि जनयन्ति स्म विमलाः
भवत्या ये भक्ताः परिणतिरमीषामियमुमे ॥ १०२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samudbhūtasthūlastanabharamuraścāru hasitaṃ
kaṭākṣe kandarpaḥ katicana kadambadyuti vapuḥ .
harasya tvadbhrāntiṃ manasi janayanti sma vimalāḥ
bhavatyā ye bhaktāḥ pariṇatiramīṣāmiyamume .. 102..
contentBlocks.translation: A chest adorned by well-developed breasts, a sweet smile, side-glances revealing the graces of the god of love, a frame as handsome as the Kadamba tree—these features of Thine, O Uma, the attainment of which is the consummation of all Thy true devotees also, only help to throw Hara, Thy Consort, into confusion (as He cannot distinguish Thee in the assembly of such intimate devotees who have attained oneness of form with Thee).
contentBlocks.meanings: समुद्भूत (samudbhūta) – arisen; स्थूल (sthūla) – large; स्तनभरम् (stanabharam) – breast weight (stana:breast | bharam:weight); उरः (uraḥ) – chest; चारु (cāru) – beautiful; हसितं (hasitaṃ) – smile; कटाक्षे (kaṭākṣe) – in side-glance; कन्दर्पः (kandarpaḥ) – god of love; कतिचन (katicana) – a few; कदम्बद्युति (kadambadyuti) – like Kadamba tree (kadamba:Kadamba tree | dyuti:like); वपुः (vapuḥ) – body; हरस्य (harasya) – of Hara; त्वद्भ्रान्तिं (tvadbhrāntiṃ) – confusion about you (tvad:you | bhrāntiṃ:confusion); मनसि (manasi) – in the mind; जनयन्ति (janayanti) – generate; स्म (sma) – indeed; विमलाः (vimalāḥ) – pure; भवत्या (bhavatyā) – of you; ये (ye) – who; भक्ताः (bhaktāḥ) – devotees; परिणतिः (pariṇatiḥ) – attainment; अमीषाम् (amīṣām) – of these; इयम् (iyam) – this; उमे (ume) – O Uma;

contentBlocks.original:
निधे नित्यस्मेरे निरवधिगुणे नीतिनिपुणे
निराघातज्ञाने नियमपरचित्तैकनिलये ।
नियत्या निर्मुक्ते निखिलनिगमान्तस्तुतिपदे
निरातङ्के नित्ये निगमय ममापि स्तुतिमिमाम् ॥ १०३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nidhe nityasmere niravadhiguṇe nītinipuṇe
nirāghātajñāne niyamaparacittaikanilaye .
niyatyā nirmukte nikhilanigamāntastutipade
nirātaṅke nitye nigamaya mamāpi stutimimām .. 103..
contentBlocks.translation: O My Treasure! O Goddess with an ever-smiling face! O Centre of Infinite Excellences! O Expert in doing what is right and just! O Source of unfailing wisdom! O the Sole Resort of men with well-controlled minds! O Freedom’s haven! O the object adored of all the Vedas! O Faultless one! O Eternal Being! Deign to sanctify this, my hymn of praise.
contentBlocks.meanings: निधे (nidhe) – O treasure; नित्यस्मेरे (nityasmere) – ever-smiling (nitya:eternal | smere:smiling); निरवधिगुणे (niravadhiguṇe) – of infinite excellences (niravadhi:infinite | guṇe:excellences); नीतिनिपुणे (nītinipuṇe) – expert in righteousness (nīti:righteousness | nipuṇe:expert); निराघातज्ञाने (nirāghātajñāne) – source of unfailing wisdom (nirāghāta:unfailing | jñāne:wisdom); नियमपरचित्तैकनिलये (niyamaparacittaikanilaye) – resort of controlled minds (niyama:control | para:supreme | citta:mind | eka:one | nilaye:resort); नियत्या (niyatyā) – by freedom; निर्मुक्ते (nirmukte) – freed; निखिलनिगमान्तस्तुतिपदे (nikhilanigamāntastutipade) – adored by all Vedas (nikhila:all | nigama:Vedas | anta:end | stuti:praise | pade:in the position); निरातङ्के (nirātaṅke) – fearless; नित्ये (nitye) – eternal; निगमय (nigamaya) – sanctify; ममापि (mamāpi) – my also (mama:my | api:also); स्तुतिमिमाम् (stutimimām) – this hymn of praise (stuti:praise | imām:this);
